Having been a victim of bullying, life a.coa:
The victim of bullying starts at a new school.
Life at the new school does not improve for the victim.
To gain power and no longer be a misfit, the victim secretly infects fellow students with a zombie virus.
The infected students exhibit zombie-like behavior.
Having been a victim of bullying, life a.outcomes: Protagonist achieves social status and power as school hero. The conflict is resolved with partial resolution (peers transformed) but underlying issues of bullying and manipulation remain.
Having been a victim of bullying, life a.theme: abuse of power; peer pressure; transformation through education

Olivier, a carpenter by trade who teache.coa: A carpenter teaches at a trades training center and takes on Francis as an apprentice without revealing their connection from five years ago. The carpenter develops a slight obsession with Francis, who is unaware of their connection. The carpenter stalks Francis home, steals his keys, and explores his apartment to learn more about him. This discovery deepens the carpenter's conflicted feelings towards Francis.
Olivier, a carpenter by trade who teache.outcomes: Protagonist maintains a complex emotional state, unable to reconcile his feelings with the moral implications of his actions. The conflict remains unresolved with no clear resolution or transformation.
Olivier, a carpenter by trade who teache.theme: moral ambiguity; trauma and obsession; redemption vs retribution

After one book that ended up in the rema.coa: A novelist struggles financially after one of their books is unsuccessful and ends up in remainder bins, causing tension with their partner. Desperate for income, they meet an Englishman who runs an exclusive escort service and are offered a job, which they keep hidden from their partner. One of the clients is married to a famous author, whom the novelist greatly admires, and they develop a relationship with this author while also working on their latest book together. The novelist becomes torn between their professional obligations to the wife and their growing connection to the famous author.
After one book that ended up in the rema.outcomes: Protagonist is able to maintain a professional relationship with the author while navigating his obligations to Andrea. The conflict is unresolved, as Byron struggles to separate personal and professional responsibilities.
After one book that ended up in the rema.theme: professional integrity; blurred boundaries; creative responsibility

Gary Oldman plays Ben Chase, a brash you.coa:
Ben Chase successfully defends a wealthy client against a murder charge.
The successful defense leads to the client retaining Ben to defend him in another case, before charges are even filed.
Ben knows his new client is guilty of the second murder and struggles with the ethics of defending him.
Ben resolves to secretly oppose his client's interests, hoping he will incriminate himself.
Gary Oldman plays Ben Chase, a brash you.outcomes: Protagonist's career is put at risk due to his secret opposition to a guilty client. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation and professional reevaluation.
Gary Oldman plays Ben Chase, a brash you.theme: ethics vs ambition; loyalty vs duty; moral compromise

The opera singer Maddalena had been cast.coa:
A banker seduces and abandons Maddalena, who becomes pregnant as a result.
Maddalena's family learns about the pregnancy and casts her out due to shame and social standing concerns.
Maddalena returns to her native city, where she is warned by her father to remain away because she poses a threat to their reputation and her sister's upcoming wedding.
The banker threatens Maddalena's family's reputation with an underhanded banking scheme.
The opera singer Maddalena had been cast.outcomes: Protagonist is reconciled with her family. The conflict is resolved with personal reconciliation but broader social implications remain.

In the Soviet Union, American reporter M.coa: Mac secretly writes unflattering stories about the Soviet Union for his newspaper, attributing them to "Comrade X". His identity as Comrade X is discovered by Vanya, who blackmails Mac into getting Theodore out of the country. Mac agrees to help Vanya in exchange for keeping his secret safe. Theodore agrees to a sham marriage with Mac so she can leave the Soviet Union and spread the message of Communism.
In the Soviet Union, American reporter M.outcomes: Protagonist escapes punishment and achieves romantic resolution. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and relational change.
In the Soviet Union, American reporter M.theme: ideological deception; propaganda vs truth; love as redemption

Abandoned at birth in the Greek mountain.coa: A baby is abandoned at birth in the Greek mountains during a storm. The baby is taken in and adopted by someone who has no knowledge of the parents. As a young man, the individual is incarcerated after being involved in a deadly accident. While in prison, he meets Iro, a warden who seems to seek out his presence and takes care of him.
Abandoned at birth in the Greek mountain.outcomes: Protagonist loses his sight but gains a deeper understanding of life and its value. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and acceptance of circumstances.

Luna (Lisa Vicari) is an intelligent and.coa:
Luna's family is killed by foreign agents while they are spending the summer together in the mountains.
The event causes Luna to barely escape with her life.
Luna discovers that her father was a Russian agent living undercover in Germany for 20 years, and that her entire life has been a lie.
This revelation forces Luna to confront the fact that she is now in danger from the Russian secret service.
Luna (Lisa Vicari) is an intelligent and.outcomes: Protagonist survives and achieves revenge for her family's death. The conflict is resolved with conflict_resolved and personal transformation.

After her pimp Carmine has gotten marrie.coa:
A pimp gets married.
The prostitute starts a new life as a marketer in Rome to provide her son with a better life.
She finds her 16-year-old son a job by blackmailing a trattoria owner.
Her son is initially influenced away from his thieving friends and occasional streetwalker.
After her pimp Carmine has gotten marrie.outcomes: Protagonist loses her son and is left a grieving, desperate mother. The conflict is unresolved with personal devastation.
After her pimp Carmine has gotten marrie.theme: motherly love; redemption vs relapse; struggle for a better life

Three brothers, Necati, Hassan and Omer,.coa:
Three brothers travel from Russia to Turkey to transport their father's remains for burial.
They prepare documents in Russia with the help of a Russian woman named Tatiana.
Tatiana accompanies them on the bus with a group of Russian girls traveling to work in Turkey, and also brings her young niece Olga, who is disabled and needs medical treatment in Istanbul.
The bus crosses into Turkish territory.
Three brothers, Necati, Hassan and Omer,.outcomes: Protagonist brothers successfully transport remains to Turkey and ensure safety of Tatiana's niece. The conflict is resolved with personal intervention by Tatiana.
Three brothers, Necati, Hassan and Omer,.theme: human trafficking; family loyalty; protection vs exploitation

An elegant man-about-town leads a secret.coa:
A series of high-end jewelry stores are robbed, resulting in significant insurance payouts.
An investigator is hired by an insurance company to track down the thief responsible for the robberies.
The investigator follows a lead to Paris, where she encounters the jewel thief and becomes aware that he has seen through her scheme.
The thief manages to evade capture in Paris.
An elegant man-about-town leads a secret.outcomes: Protagonist is recaptured but manages to evade long-term consequences. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation, as protagonist adapts to new circumstances.
An elegant man-about-town leads a secret.theme: deception; morality vs law; freedom vs confinement

Street gangsters Armand and Marie are ma.coa: A wealthy scientist's home is robbed by a group of gangsters, led by the woman who is in love with one of them. The police break up the robbery, but the scientist hides one of the gang members from them because he intervened on his behalf when another gang member tried to stab him. The hidden gang member is wounded during the incident and requires time to recover. While recovering, the scientist sets him up with a beautiful woman.
Street gangsters Armand and Marie are ma.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a new relationship with his original love interest (Marie), while the conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social reintegration. The conflict is partially resolved with relational change, as protagonist and Marie are reunited.
Street gangsters Armand and Marie are ma.theme: love vs loyalty; deception and betrayal; redemption through sacrifice

Sezim (Alina Turdumamatova) wants to ful.coa:
A young woman gets kidnapped by a group of men and taken to the hinterland.
The kidnappers force her to marry one of them against her will.
She is threatened with social stigmatization if she refuses the marriage.
She becomes desperate to find a way out.
Sezim (Alina Turdumamatova) wants to ful.outcomes: Protagonist manages to escape her forced marriage and returns to city. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader cultural constraints remain.
Sezim (Alina Turdumamatova) wants to ful.theme: freedom vs tradition; cultural oppression; female agency

At home in his New York City apartment,.coa:
The protagonist is tranquilized at home.
He is kidnapped and taken to an unknown location where he is persuaded by threats of physical force, promises of money, and the lure of a pretty tutor to travel to Africa.
He assumes the identity of an indigenous language-speaking itinerant worker and travels to Africa.
Upon arrival, he passes a test involving survival in a small, overheated room with deep sand on the floor, mimicking African conditions.
He covers himself with sand to avoid heatstroke and wins the contract from his new employer.
The villains are tipped off that he is on his way, but he embarks on a mission to infiltrate and destroy a human trafficking and slavery ring in Africa and France.
At home in his New York City apartment,.outcomes: Protagonist successfully infiltrates and disrupts the human trafficking and slavery ring. The conflict is resolved with partial resolution, as some villains are apprehended but broader threats remain.
At home in his New York City apartment,.theme: exploitation vs empowerment; identity deception; human resilience

In the early 1970s, while the majority o.coa:
The United States Army secretly develops a way to resurrect and control dead bodies in the early 1970s.
The experiments are shut down when the reanimated corpses are unable to control their hunger for human flesh.
Thirty years later, the army decides to reopen the project.
They choose Grover City as the location for the main testing facilities due to its remote location.
In the early 1970s, while the majority o.outcomes: Protagonist group survives and finds temporary solution to zombie outbreak. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing threat of zombie attacks.
In the early 1970s, while the majority o.theme: survival under adversity; control vs nature; human resilience

A young man, Ben Freidman (Scott Wilson).coa:
A young man decides that he is ready to meet his biological parents.
He learns about the death of his biological mother.
This news causes him to feel disappointed and possibly altered his plans for meeting his biological family.
His biological father remains alive but is institutionalized.
A young man, Ben Freidman (Scott Wilson).outcomes: Protagonist experiences emotional closure and acceptance of his past. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation, as he comes to terms with his identity and family history.

Following Marta's wake, her five best fr.coa:
Marta's wake takes place.
Her five best friends decide to spend the night at a rural tourist getaway that Marta never got around to opening.
They arrive at the getaway, which becomes a setting for them to confront their relationships with each other and Marta.
As they talk about life, each friend reveals aspects of herself that were previously unknown or hidden.
Following Marta's wake, her five best fr.outcomes: Protagonist group emerges with a deeper understanding of each other and their bond. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation, but the future remains uncertain.

The film is set in Sagliena, an imaginar.coa:
The war ends. A transfer is made, and a new figure arrives in Sagliena. The new arrival takes over the local Carabinieri station with the help of a maid. The new figure meets a young local girl who is secretly in love with another carabiniere. 

The new figure tries to get engaged to the young local girl, but she is actually in love with someone else. A sacristan is in love with the other carabiniere and wants him to know about it. The sacristan informs a priest, who then informs the new figure. The other carabiniere gets engaged to the young local girl, while the new figure gets engaged to another woman on the evening of a feast.
The film is set in Sagliena, an imaginar.outcomes: Protagonist Marshal Antonio Carotenuto achieves a new sense of purpose through his engagement to Annarella. The conflict is resolved with romantic relationships clarified and stabilized.
The film is set in Sagliena, an imaginar.theme: adapting to change; love vs expectation; finding one's place

Major Bulochkin, senior lieutenant Tucha.coa: Three fighter pilots vow not to fall in love until the war ends. They encounter a female aviation squadron and a pretty journalist, causing them to start giving up on their plans one by one. The female aviation squadron is encountered while the pilots are on a mission, leading to an initial distraction from their vow. As they spend more time with the female pilots and the journalist, two of the three pilots begin to fall in love.
Major Bulochkin, senior lieutenant Tucha.outcomes: Protagonists all eventually succumb to falling in love despite initial vow. The conflict is unresolved as the war continues and personal relationships are complicated.
Major Bulochkin, senior lieutenant Tucha.theme: love vs duty; adaptation under adversity; human vulnerability

Alvaro Cosenza is a young immigrant part.coa:
A young immigrant partisan fights against German-occupied forces and becomes a leader in the process.
The partisan assaults Nina, impregnating her as an insult to Inspector Moretti, who is his main enemy.
Moretti tries to have the partisan killed by his companions after discovering the violence suffered by his daughter.
The partisan kills Moretti in retaliation.
Nina aborts the child she was carrying after learning that the partisan had killed her father.
After liberation, the partisan engages in illicit activities with his gang, earning a reputation for danger.
Left alone, Nina becomes involved with Leandro, who introduces her to prostitution due to her vulnerability after her father's death.
Nina meets the partisan again and discovers she still loves him.
Alvaro Cosenza is a young immigrant part.outcomes: Protagonist survives and achieves some level of resolution through his actions as a partisan leader. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader threats remain.
Alvaro Cosenza is a young immigrant part.theme: redemption; love vs loyalty; human resilience

Marius and Jeannette live in the same wo.coa:
The company that owns the abandoned cement works goes out of business and announces plans to demolish the plant, forcing Marius to squat on the property to save money.
Marius catches Jeannette trying to steal paint from the cement lot and tries to chase her away.
Jeannette is caught by Marius and attempts to flee with the stolen paint.
The next day, Marius visits Jeannette's apartment to apologize for his behavior the previous day and brings her the two cans of paint he had confiscated.
Marius and Jeannette live in the same wo.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a new romantic connection and begins to heal from past emotional trauma. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and relational growth.
Marius and Jeannette live in the same wo.theme: healing through relationships; love as redemption; overcoming past wounds

A gangster, Joe Farrow, kills a man afte.coa: A man is killed after a game of craps. A gangster tries to get another man to take the rap and stand trial for the crime in exchange for $50,000. The second man steals a ledger with information that could incriminate the gangster when he tries to renege on the payment. The ledger is passed to an immigration social worker while the second man is being pursued.
A gangster, Joe Farrow, kills a man afte.outcomes: Protagonist achieves temporary escape from immediate danger but broader threats remain. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but relational dynamics are complicated.
A gangster, Joe Farrow, kills a man afte.theme: justice vs corruption; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Bob McGraw, Max, Gonzer, and Irwin, stud.coa: The four worst students at Lepetomane University are volunteered to compete in a collegiate raft race by Dean Burch, who uses records of one student's checkered past as blackmail. He offers them degrees in their chosen major as an incentive. The team is told they have the distinct honor of being the bottom of the list, and that they're not just at the bottom, but are the bottom. They accept the challenge and begin preparing for the race.
Bob McGraw, Max, Gonzer, and Irwin, stud.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a degree of success in the raft race despite initial reluctance. The conflict is resolved with partial resolution, as some opponents are eliminated and others are thwarted, but broader threats remain.
Bob McGraw, Max, Gonzer, and Irwin, stud.theme: underdog spirit; redemption through achievement; loyalty vs external pressures

Dave Anderson (Bill Cosby) and Manny Dur.coa:
A retired detective has enough evidence on two high-class sneak thieves to put them behind bars.
The detective offers to maintain his silence if the crooks will go straight and do work at a youth center for delinquents.
The crooks are initially reluctant, but eventually gain the trust and admiration of the kids and start to enjoy their new job.
A past heist comes back to haunt the crooks.
Dave Anderson (Bill Cosby) and Manny Dur.outcomes: Protagonists successfully transition from life of crime to rehabilitation through work with delinquents. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social reintegration.

The movie follows a surgeon whose growin.coa:
A woman suffers a car crash outside a surgeon's home.
The surgeon kidnaps the woman and treats her secretly at home after the accident.
He amputates both her legs above the knee to keep her captive.
She tries to choke him, prompting him to amputate her arms above the elbow as well.
This sequence of events is revealed to be a dream when she wakes up in a hospital with all her limbs intact.
The movie follows a surgeon whose growin.outcomes: Protagonist is revealed to be experiencing a delusional episode, and the woman is safe and unharmed. The conflict is resolved as the protagonist's grip on reality is restored.
The movie follows a surgeon whose growin.theme: obsession; captivity; blurred lines between reality and fantasy

The film follows the titular Rabiye Kurn.coa:
The eldest son is detained in Pakistan by the United States government.
The detention leads to a Turkish housewife discovering her son's situation and launching a legal dispute on his behalf.
A human rights lawyer joins forces with the housewife, and together they pursue a campaign for a fair trial.
Their efforts lead them to take their case all the way to the Supreme Court.
The film follows the titular Rabiye Kurn.outcomes: Protagonist Rabiye achieves her son's release from Guantanamo Bay. The conflict is resolved with personal satisfaction and social justice.

The novel depicts the author's teenage f.coa:
The author meets Jeffrey at Eastview Junior High around age 12.
Jeffrey is ridiculed by bullies at school due to his awkward behavior and appearance.
The author and their friends encourage Jeffrey's bizarre behavior, including faking epileptic seizures in school and pretending to have cerebral palsy.
As a result of this attention-seeking behavior, Jeffrey becomes more isolated from the rest of the students.
The novel depicts the author's teenage f.outcomes: Protagonist is left with a complex understanding of the antagonist's troubled past. The conflict is unresolved as the antagonist has not yet committed his first murder.

When orphaned Jimmy Mason is taken in by.coa:
Jimmy Mason is taken in by Aunt Emma and Uncle Henry, where he meets Matt Kelly, who claims to have political connections but is actually a bootlegger.
Kelly hires Jimmy as one of his hands, and during a police raid, Jimmy refuses to identify Kelly, leading to his arrest and sentencing to three years of hard labor in reform school.
In reform school, Jimmy befriends Shorty, who helps him but eventually gets sent to solitary confinement due to his own illness.
Jimmy realizes Shorty needs medical attention and escapes from the school to seek help from Kelly and Peggy Gardner.
When orphaned Jimmy Mason is taken in by.outcomes: Protagonist achieves freedom after exposure of corrupt system. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social reintegration.
When orphaned Jimmy Mason is taken in by.theme: corruption; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

The narrative revolves around Noredin (F.coa:
A pop star is murdered, with her throat brutally slit, in the Nile Hilton hotel.
The investigation into the murder begins, led by a rugged detective known for his chain-smoking habit.
A Sudanese maid emerges as the sole witness to the crime.
The detective discovers that the victim was involved in blackmailing several high-profile individuals, including a politician, using compromising images.
The narrative revolves around Noredin (F.outcomes: Protagonist Noredin successfully solves the murder case, exposing Shafiq's involvement in the crime. The conflict is resolved with justice served and the perpetrator brought to light.
The narrative revolves around Noredin (F.theme: corruption; power dynamics; truth vs deception

The narrative begins in an orphanage whe.coa: An orphanage resident brings a sailor doll to life through an unknown means. The doll becomes captain of a ship, while the resident transforms into a doll herself. The transformed resident is taken aboard the ship by the doll-captain. The ship sets sail in search of lost treasure.
The narrative begins in an orphanage whe.outcomes: Protagonist emerges from transformation with newfound identity and maturity. The conflict is partially resolved with personal growth but broader existential questions remain.
The narrative begins in an orphanage whe.theme: transformation; identity formation; the power of imagination

The novel tells the story of a mysteriou.coa:
A group of settlers is shipwrecked on a nearby island.
Kaw-djer assists them establish their colony.
The colony falls victim to a fight for power.
Kaw-djer temporarily abandons his anarchistic principles and restores order.
The novel tells the story of a mysteriou.outcomes: Protagonist maintains individualism while contributing to the community's stability. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation, as Kaw-djer adapts his principles for the greater good.
The novel tells the story of a mysteriou.theme: individualism vs collectivism; self-sufficiency; moral flexibility

Mallory (Bonamy) is the head of an elite.coa: A team of government agents is dispatched to protect a convent from an undead monster assault. They are ambushed by an unknown assailant, resulting in Durand's death, Vena Cava's injury, and Tina's coma. The pope is kidnapped by masked attackers who resist the bullets of his bodyguards. Mallory is approached to rescue the pope, which she reluctantly accepts after suspecting a connection between her team's assailant and the pope's kidnappers.
Mallory (Bonamy) is the head of an elite.outcomes: Protagonist's team is able to rescue the pope but broader supernatural threats remain. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing danger from vampires and fallen angels.
Mallory (Bonamy) is the head of an elite.theme: good vs evil; power struggles; human resilience

The year is 1492 and Spain is in the gri.coa: The royal edict is issued, expelling the Jewish community from their homes of many generations. Those who have not converted are forced to leave, but 15-year-old Yonah Toledano is left behind. His father and brother have been lost to the troubles, leaving him alone in his family's home. He sets out on a journey across Spain with his donkey, Moise.
The year is 1492 and Spain is in the gri.outcomes: Protagonist survives and achieves escape from persecution. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing systemic injustice.
The year is 1492 and Spain is in the gri.theme: survival under oppression; identity vs conformity; faith vs intolerance

One day Emil Sinclair, an eleven-year-ol.coa: An eleven-year-old boy returns from school to an empty house. He goes upstairs into his father's room and steals sugared and dried figs out of his dad's chest of drawers. The boy feels guilty but doesn't confess the theft, instead telling a lie about buying the figs at the cake shop in Calw. His father takes him to the shop, where he again denies stealing the figs.
One day Emil Sinclair, an eleven-year-ol.outcomes: Protagonist is forgiven by his father and experiences a sense of complete forgiveness. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and reconciliation.

Lambert, a withdrawn middle-aged man, wo.coa:
A middle-aged man works the night shift at a petrol station in Paris.
He has no friends or family and relies on a bottle of rum for companionship.
One night, a young Arab man enters the shop, also alone and struggling financially through drug dealing.
The two men form an unlikely friendship despite their similar circumstances.
Lambert, a withdrawn middle-aged man, wo.outcomes: Protagonist achieves revenge and closure for his friend's death. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as protagonist comes to terms with his own tragic past.
Lambert, a withdrawn middle-aged man, wo.theme: redemption; loss of innocence; human connection

Jennifer (Jessica Cameron), Ray (Shelby.coa: The "Truth or Dare-Devils" create outrageous YouTube videos based on the party game Truth or Dare?, which gains them significant media attention and a fan following. They make a guest appearance at an event, where they have a confrontation with Derik, an obsessed fan who is angry that they haven't responded to his e-mails and are faking elements of their videos for dramatic effect. The group dismisses Derik as just an over-the-top obsessed fan and heads to John's home to rest. They become horrified when they realize that Derik has followed them and is intent on making new Truth or Dare-Devils videos that are all too real.
Jennifer (Jessica Cameron), Ray (Shelby.outcomes: Protagonist group is forced into a life-or-death situation with Derik. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing threat and personal danger.
Jennifer (Jessica Cameron), Ray (Shelby.theme: fan culture; blurred reality; the cost of fame

German spies, using Freya (María Félix).coa:
German spies convince neutral Spaniard to navigate a ship to lay naval mines around British ports in the Mediterranean, promising not to fire on passenger ships.
The neutral Spaniard agrees to help, unaware of the consequences.
One mine destroys the ship carrying his son and many others, killing them.
The loss of his son prompts the neutral Spaniard to change sides and become an ally.
German spies, using Freya (María Félix).outcomes: Protagonist achieves revenge and redemption through personal transformation and sacrifice. The conflict is resolved with a mix of personal and relational change.
German spies, using Freya (María Félix).theme: revenge vs forgiveness; loyalty under duress; human cost of war

In Paris, the relationship between Marti.coa:
Martin's relationship with Claire reaches an impasse in Paris.
Martin receives news of his mother's death and returns to Los Angeles for inheritance formalities.
In Los Angeles, Martin is aided by a family friend, Linda, who takes him to his childhood home and neighborhood.
This return triggers several buried memories that surface and disturb Martin.
In Paris, the relationship between Marti.outcomes: Protagonist achieves resolution and closure on past conflicts. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation.
In Paris, the relationship between Marti.theme: confronting the past; redemption; coming to terms

A group of adventurers journey deep into.coa: A group of adventurers journey deep into the South American jungle in search of ancient Incan treasure. A beautiful woman is brought to their camp by hired bearers, claiming she has come to join her husband, a newer member of the group. The group's dynamics change as tensions rise and jealousies flare among the men over the woman's presence. Fights break out between the men, causing further division within the group.
A group of adventurers journey deep into.outcomes: Protagonist group survives and achieves temporary safety from immediate danger. The conflict is unresolved with potential for further escalation.
A group of adventurers journey deep into.theme: greed vs loyalty; survival under pressure; unity in adversity

(The beginning of the tale is lost.) Dje.coa:
Djehuty invites the prince of Joppa to a party in his camp outside the town.
The prince attends, but is knocked out by Djehuty.
Two hundred of Djehuty's soldiers are loaded into sacks on pack animals.
Djehuty sends a charioteer to announce that the Egyptians have surrendered and are sending tribute.
(The beginning of the tale is lost.) Dje.outcomes: Protagonist Djehuty succeeds in conquering the town. The conflict is resolved with conquest but broader implications remain.

In New York City, David (Donovan), a plu.coa:
A plumber creates a new identity as a film director.
The plumber's unfamiliarity with film is mistaken for inscrutability, making him appear like an indie talent.
He gains recognition and meets women due to his good looks and new persona.
Toni discovers that the script she wrote has been stolen and used by the plumber.
In New York City, David (Donovan), a plu.outcomes: Protagonist maintains his new identity but struggles with guilt and internal conflict. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation and relational damage.
In New York City, David (Donovan), a plu.theme: deception; social status vs authenticity; identity formation

The story takes place against the backdr.coa: A woman with no money but a taste for luxury is dazzled by a poor Italian nobleman trying to recover his debts at the casino. The nobleman enlists the help of people he owes money to in order to attract the woman. The woman agrees to perform at a seedy bistro, singing "Back Home in Indiana" for the enjoyment of a wealthy man who she believes will make her rich.
The story takes place against the backdr.outcomes: Protagonist achieves temporary financial stability through her performance. The conflict is unresolved as broader financial struggles persist.
The story takes place against the backdr.theme: class vs poverty; love as transaction; artistic expression

Set in Zlatibor District, an old man nam.coa: An old man prays for his grandson to go to the city and sell their cow. The grandson goes to the city, but instead of meeting his grandfather's stepbrother, he meets two grandsons who are small-time criminals. He clicks with these men and falls in love with a schoolgirl, deciding to marry her as part of his testament to his grandfather.
Set in Zlatibor District, an old man nam.outcomes: Protagonist successfully finds wife and resolves family conflicts. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation through marriage and community reintegration.
Set in Zlatibor District, an old man nam.theme: love vs adversity; redemption in family; human connection

Vladimir Antonov (Hristo Shopov), a ruth.coa: A group of Marines led by Lieutenant Everett is sent to Russia to capture a ruthless warlord. The U.S. Marines' previous attempt to take down the warlord resulted in 11 deaths and 2 captures, prompting Colonel Anslow's vow to prevent similar situations. Over the next 36 hours, Colonel Anslow will have another chance to show his resolve and capture the warlord. A group of Marines led by Lieutenant Everett is sent to Russia to capture the warlord with the help of an elite team of Russian troops commanded by Major Dmitri Kirilenko.
Vladimir Antonov (Hristo Shopov), a ruth.outcomes: Protagonist Colonel Anslow achieves his goal of capturing warlord Antonov and avenging his men's deaths. The conflict is resolved with the capture of the antagonist, but broader implications for international relations remain.
Vladimir Antonov (Hristo Shopov), a ruth.theme: justice; loyalty vs duty; power dynamics

A widow for fifteen years, Tina Camphaus.coa:
A widow raises three children by herself for fifteen years.
Her first lover returns after a long absence.
She starts developing feelings for him again.
Her son resents the newcomer and feels threatened by his presence in their house.
The son's behavior causes tension between him and the woman, who is now growing closer to her former lover.
A widow for fifteen years, Tina Camphaus.outcomes: Protagonist's relationships are transformed through confrontation of unresolved family issues. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation and relational change.
A widow for fifteen years, Tina Camphaus.theme: family secrets; love vs loyalty; identity formation

Set in Johannesburg in 1963, the film ex.coa: A 13-year-old girl lives a carefree life until her father flees into exile, causing an abrupt change in her circumstances. The family is ostracized by their peers due to the father's involvement with the South African Communist Party. As a result, the girl draws closer to her mother, who is part of the campaign against apartheid. Her mother's activism leads to hardship and political intimidation for the family.
Set in Johannesburg in 1963, the film ex.outcomes: Protagonist comes to terms with her family's involvement in the anti-apartheid movement and finds a sense of purpose. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader systemic injustices remain.
Set in Johannesburg in 1963, the film ex.theme: coming of age; social responsibility; racial injustice

Casey, an American living in Cologne, Ge.coa: Casey works as a money runner with his friend Matthias for a drug dealer. He meets Juliette while she tends bar at one of the dealer's hangouts and asks her out, but she turns him down due to his involvement with the dealer. Inspired by their conversation, Casey quits his job and asks Juliette out again, and they start dating. They move in together after a short time.
Casey, an American living in Cologne, Ge.outcomes: Protagonist succeeds in obtaining funds through the heist, saving his partner's life. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and relational change as protagonist prioritizes love over loyalty.
Casey, an American living in Cologne, Ge.theme: love vs loyalty; redemption through sacrifice; moral compromise

In the resort of Lake Waxapahachie in Ne.coa: Mrs. Prentiss offers to pay Dick to escort her daughter Ann for the summer. Dick accepts the offer despite his fiancée's reservations. Mrs. Prentiss wants Ann to marry T. Mosley Thorpe, but Ann has other plans. Meanwhile, Humbolt is introduced as a character with a weakness for pretty faces.
In the resort of Lake Waxapahachie in Ne.outcomes: Protagonist Dick Curtis achieves a successful resolution of his romantic conflict and secures a stable future through his medical education. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social reintegration for the protagonist and his loved ones.
In the resort of Lake Waxapahachie in Ne.theme: social class; love vs duty; deception

Elvis and Leo run a crime scene cleanup.coa:
A crime scene cleanup business is hired to clean up after a death.
They discover a female humanoid creature with a cow's tail hidden in a basement.
Playing a tape left behind by her captor, they learn of her life in captivity and medical experimentation.
Paramilitary soldiers come to recapture the creature.
The creature's fellow creatures, hulders, arrive and fight off the soldiers, leaving the cleanup business alive.
The creature rejoins the hulders in the wild.
Elvis and Leo run a crime scene cleanup.outcomes: Thale is freed from captivity and reunited with her fellow creatures. The conflict is resolved with a partial resolution of the threat to Thale's life, but broader questions about human treatment of non-human entities remain.
Elvis and Leo run a crime scene cleanup.theme: freedom vs captivity; exploitation of the vulnerable; coexistence between humans and non-humans

Hoke Birdsill rides into Yerkey's Hole d.coa: Hoke Birdsill rides into Yerkey's Hole demanding action against a robber. The mayor sees no law in place and decides to appoint Hoke as sheriff instead of taking action. As sheriff, Hoke becomes embroiled in a cat-and-mouse game with Dingus Magee, who is aided by Anna Hot Water. Dingus tries to steal from the mayor, but Hoke has beaten him to it.
Hoke Birdsill rides into Yerkey's Hole d.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a new status as law enforcer. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social reintegration through collaboration between former adversaries.
Hoke Birdsill rides into Yerkey's Hole d.theme: chaos vs order; loyalty vs duty; redemption

Studying under a disciple of Aleister Cr.coa:
A disciple of Aleister Crowley teaches a leader from an upper class group how to invoke a supernatural force.
The leader uses this knowledge to summon the force, which begins to slowly consume the village of Marienbad and its inhabitants.
As the village is threatened by the spreading force, the mayor of a nearby town commissions the construction of a dam that will flood the valley in 1965.
The dam's completion leads to the flooding of Marienbad, submerging it under water and trapping the supernatural force beneath.
Studying under a disciple of Aleister Cr.outcomes: Protagonist (implied) fails to prevent supernatural threat, but village is sealed off. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing mysterious deaths and disappearances.
Studying under a disciple of Aleister Cr.theme: supernatural power; containment vs release; consequences of meddling

Agent Axon Rey (Filipović), a decorated.coa: Agent Axon Rey is recruited by a secret government organization and trained to become "Sphinx", an assassin. He fails to execute one of his targets, but his life is spared. His supervisor sends him to "Gulag 7" instead of executing him. In Gulag 7, he is forced to fight five other government operatives in a battle for survival.
Agent Axon Rey (Filipović), a decorated.outcomes: Protagonist survives and forms an alliance with his supervisor to take down the Director. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation, but broader threats remain.

Set in Łódź, the film revolves around Kh.coa: The mother dies, leaving behind a family that requires maternal care. Khavtshi takes on this responsibility and is tasked with shopping, cooking, and cleaning for her family. Her siblings get into trouble - the younger brother gets involved with crooks and the sister becomes infatuated with a gangster. The family's unappreciative behavior eventually wears down Khavtshi's morale.
Set in Łódź, the film revolves around Kh.outcomes: Protagonist achieves personal growth through relationships and finds happiness in marriage. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation and social integration.
Set in Łódź, the film revolves around Kh.theme: maternal responsibility; family dynamics; love vs duty

The documentary centers on the opioid ep.coa: The overdose rate in Huntington, West Virginia is 10 times the US average. The city experiences a growing number of heroin and prescription painkiller overdoses, followed by an increase in fentanyl overdoses. Emergency responders treat overdose victims, including those revived with naloxone. First responders witness dozens of overdoses per month, taking a psychological toll on them.
The documentary centers on the opioid ep.outcomes: The opioid epidemic remains a significant issue in Huntington, but efforts to combat it continue. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing struggles against addiction and its effects.

The movie presents life in a prison wher.coa: Men are put on death row in a prison for crimes they claim they didn't commit. Some of them have been wrongfully accused and convicted. One man is condemned to death for a crime he claims he did not commit, sparking efforts from his friends outside the prison to prove his innocence. The drama inside the prison unfolds as these men await their fate.
The movie presents life in a prison wher.outcomes: Protagonist is exonerated and his wrongful conviction is overturned. The conflict is resolved with personal vindication and social reintegration.
The movie presents life in a prison wher.theme: justice vs injustice; redemption; hope in darkness

In 1927, unemployed German-Jewish actor.coa:
In 1927, an unemployed actor creates a German music group inspired by The Revelers.
He holds auditions and recruits four additional singers and a pianist.
The group, named the "Comedian Harmonists", gains international fame and popularity.
They become one of the most successful music groups in Germany.
In 1927, unemployed German-Jewish actor.outcomes: The Comedian Harmonists' career is marked by success but ultimately cut short by the Nazi regime's anti-Semitic policies. The conflict is unresolved with a focus on the personal cost and loss for the protagonist and his group.
In 1927, unemployed German-Jewish actor.theme: artistic expression vs oppression; identity under adversity; prejudice vs talent

Parinamam tackles the age old issue of l.coa: A man retires from his job. He faces callous treatment from his family after retirement. A former judge goes to Kashi in search of peace due to mental disturbance and loneliness. The former judge's story runs parallel to the retired man's story. Five other senior citizens face various problems, including losing everything they hold dear and feeling rejected by their families.
Parinamam tackles the age old issue of l.outcomes: Protagonist Balakrishna Marar finds acceptance and companionship among his peers. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing loneliness and social isolation.

The Girl in the Fog is a psychological t.coa:
A 16-year-old girl goes missing in an isolated region.
The disappearance sparks widespread concern and outrage among the public, with many calling for swift action from the authorities.
The policeman in charge of the investigation begins to scrutinize the males of the small village, suspecting one or more may be involved in the girl's disappearance.
A local high school teacher becomes a prime suspect due to his close proximity to the missing girl and his popularity among students.
The Girl in the Fog is a psychological t.outcomes: Protagonist is found dead, but truth about teacher's innocence remains uncertain. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing speculation and suspicion.
The Girl in the Fog is a psychological t.theme: moral ambiguity; guilt vs innocence; the unreliability of appearances

Events in the film take place during the.coa: The reds come to power in a small provincial town, establishing control over the local government and institutions. The whites then overthrow the reds, taking control of the town themselves. The greens eventually seize power from the whites, leading to a period of instability and flux. The self-taught theater director stages a production of Joan of Arc, driven by his passion for revolutionary art.
Events in the film take place during the.outcomes: Protagonist's artistic vision is partially realized through his production, but broader social and political upheaval continues. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing power struggles between the red, white, and green forces.
Events in the film take place during the.theme: artistic expression vs revolution; chaos and disorder; transformation under duress

An American artist living a bohemian exi.coa:
Tommy's mother mourns the death of his father.
Tommy escapes grief by causing trouble at school and making afternoon deliveries with Pappas.
Tommy becomes close friends with Lady, who is incarcerated in a women's prison for unknown reasons.
An unexpected tragedy occurs, altering Tommy's world.
An American artist living a bohemian exi.outcomes: Protagonist (Tom) achieves closure on his past through confronting unfinished business. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation and emotional healing.

The film follows five simultaneous, loos.coa:
A man kills his wife and buries her in the woods, but she is found alive when he returns home.
The suburban family's life is disrupted when the father is hypnotized into believing he is a bird.
The thuggish hitman and his translator intervene in one of the plot lines, causing chaos and confusion.
The aimless youth are hired by the hitman to burglarize houses.
The film follows five simultaneous, loos.outcomes: Protagonist's marriage is irreparably damaged; father remains hypnotized with no clear resolution; aimless youth are unrepentant; advertising executive's creativity is stifled; hitman's intervention escalates conflict.
The film follows five simultaneous, loos.theme: chaos; identity crisis; the absurdity of modern life

In a small village in Lombardy, the pari.coa:
The parish priest coaches a football team of young boys from the village.
The day of the final match approaches, causing tension between the priest and the communist mayor.
The mayor corrupts the referee to gain an unfair advantage, but the priest discovers this and confronts him.
The confrontation escalates into a physical fight, leading to the priest's exile from the village.
In a small village in Lombardy, the pari.outcomes: Protagonist (Don Camillo) is temporarily removed from the village but ultimately achieves a resolution through the citizens' intervention. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social reintegration for both characters.
In a small village in Lombardy, the pari.theme: ideological differences; loyalty vs duty; community vs corruption

The anarchist group \"Nada\" decides to.coa:
The anarchist group "Nada" decides to kidnap the United States Ambassador to France and demand a ransom for his release.
Some members are reluctant to the plan, but teacher Treuffais refuses to participate in the venture.
During the operation at a brothel where the Ambassador regularly visits, a police officer and an undercover agent are killed.
The Minister of the Interior orders Commissioner Goémond to find the hideout of the group.
The anarchist group \"Nada\" decides to.outcomes: Protagonist Treuffais survives and is freed from involvement with Nada group. The conflict is unresolved, as state interests are still at play.
The anarchist group \"Nada\" decides to.theme: state power vs individual freedom; morality of violence; consequences of extremism

At 2 years, the daughter of Jack the Rip.coa:
A young girl witnesses her mother's brutal murder by her father at 2 years old.
15 years pass, and the girl becomes a troubled young woman who appears to be possessed by her late father's spirit.
She enters a psychotic trance and continues her father's murderous spree without remembering it afterwards.
The psychiatrist takes her in, hoping to cure her condition.
At 2 years, the daughter of Jack the Rip.outcomes: Protagonist is cured and freed from possession, but not from past trauma. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal struggle.
At 2 years, the daughter of Jack the Rip.theme: inherited trauma; possession vs free will; the cyclical nature of violence

While J. D. Cahill (John Wayne), a widow.coa: A U.S. Marshal is away from home, leaving two sons who aid a group of prisoners escape from jail and rob a bank, killing the town's sheriff during the robbery. The younger son hides the stolen money while the rest of the gang returns to their cells as an alibi. The Marshal returns home and begins investigating the crime with the help of a tracker, leading them to arrest four innocent suspects who are later found guilty and scheduled for hanging.
While J. D. Cahill (John Wayne), a widow.outcomes: Protagonist resolves conflict through justice and protects his sons from harm. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as Cahill learns to trust his sons and they learn responsibility.
While J. D. Cahill (John Wayne), a widow.theme: justice vs vigilantism; loyalty and redemption; father-son relationships

Roderick Usher summons his friend to his.coa:
A friend is summoned to a crumbling old mansion in the remote countryside.
The friend arrives at the mansion, where they meet Roderick Usher, who has been painting his wife's portrait.
Roderick's wife passes away, and he has her buried in the family crypt.
Madeline revives from her catalepsy and exits her coffin.
Roderick Usher summons his friend to his.outcomes: Protagonist's friend survives the ordeal with a newfound understanding of Usher's mental state. The conflict is unresolved due to the ongoing psychological trauma of Roderick Usher.
Roderick Usher summons his friend to his.theme: the blurred lines between life and death; the dangers of isolation; the fragility of the human psyche

Conman Raymond Fernandez (Jared Leto) de.coa:
A con artist places personal ads in newspapers to defraud wealthy women.
The ads attract the attention of a woman who responds, leading to a meeting with the con artist.
The woman joins forces with the con artist, posing as his sister, and together they continue the scam.
They travel to various locations, where they meet more women through their ads, and murder them when they become too close or suspicious.
Conman Raymond Fernandez (Jared Leto) de.outcomes: Protagonist Raymond Fernandez is brought to justice for his crimes. The conflict is resolved with conflict_resolved and systemic change through law enforcement intervention.

Marc has inherited the house of his late.coa: The family leaves Paris and moves into a house on the Côte d'Azur that has been inherited by one of them. A lover arrives in the village where the family is staying, sneaking opportunities to be with Béatrix. Martin goes out at night to a local gay cruising area, an old fort on a nearby hillside, and Charly follows him.
Marc has inherited the house of his late.outcomes: Protagonist achieves romantic connection and finds happiness. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and acceptance of relationships.

Based in the Briar Patch (Tikrit Expanse.coa: The story begins on Deep Space 12, a space station in orbit of the planet Ba'ku. The daily life of several officers serving on board the station is introduced. An advanced species, the Grey, are introduced as the primary villain. Their presence sets off a chain of events that spans the entire series.
Based in the Briar Patch (Tikrit Expanse.outcomes: The conflict with the Grey is partially resolved through cooperation with other species, but broader threats remain. The protagonist's understanding of the universe and their place in it undergoes personal transformation.
Based in the Briar Patch (Tikrit Expanse.theme: cooperation vs isolation; ancient knowledge vs modern power; diversity in a complex universe

The plot focuses on Dante Alighieri, a y.coa: A young man inherits 17 million kr with a condition that he must quit smoking in 14 days and remain smoke-free for a year or else his uncle will inherit the money. The young man hires a private detective agency, Little Secret Service, to help him stay smoke-free. His uncle tries to sabotage his efforts by attempting to get him to smoke again. The young man's uncle takes up smoking himself in an attempt to undermine his nephew's resolve.
The plot focuses on Dante Alighieri, a y.outcomes: Protagonist successfully quits smoking within the time limit. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as protagonist overcomes addiction.
The plot focuses on Dante Alighieri, a y.theme: self-control; temptation vs willpower; overcoming addiction

An U.S. Army captain is flying three mis.coa: An airplane carrying three deserters experiences engine trouble. The plane crashes into the ocean near an uncharted island. The survivors find themselves on the island and encounter a primitive society of cave women. The men are mistaken for gods due to their arrival coinciding with a prophecy.
An U.S. Army captain is flying three mis.outcomes: Protagonist and deserters survive and escape from island after destroying The Great One. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as protagonist and men adapt to new circumstances and relationships.
An U.S. Army captain is flying three mis.theme: survival vs sacrifice; cultural clash; redemption through selflessness

Undercover CIA agent Sonni Griffith (Wes.coa:
An undercover CIA agent travels alone to Romania to stop the sale of a nuclear weapon.
The arms dealer is tipped off to the agent's identity.
The agent lands in prison but is quickly released by the CIA.
A new mission is given to the agent: to escort a Russian woman back to the United States.
Undercover CIA agent Sonni Griffith (Wes.outcomes: Protagonist survives and completes his mission, preventing the sale of a nuclear weapon. The conflict is resolved with personal survival and systemic change (prevention of catastrophic event).
Undercover CIA agent Sonni Griffith (Wes.theme: survival under adversity; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

The film takes place in the USSR during.coa:
Pilot fights in the war.
Pilot gets captured during the war.
Pilot manages to escape from captivity.
War ends and pilot is treated with distrust and suspicion due to having been in captivity.
Pilot suffers and struggles to find work or a place in life.
Pilot meets Sasha Lvova, who has carried love for him through the war and postwar period.
Sasha's love helps pilot overcome his difficulties.
Stalin dies.
Pilot is called to the Ministry of Defense after Stalin's death.
Pilot's military award is returned at the Ministry of Defense.
Pilot returns to his squadron.
The film takes place in the USSR during.outcomes: Protagonist achieves redemption and reintegration into society after being cleared of suspicion. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social reintegration.
The film takes place in the USSR during.theme: redemption; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Dr. Julian Blair is engaged in unconvent.coa:
The scientist's wife is killed in an auto accident.
The grief-stricken scientist becomes obsessed with redirecting his research into making contact with the dead.
He receives dire warnings from his daughter and research assistant that he is delving into forbidden areas of knowledge, but he remains undeterred.
He moves his laboratory to an isolated New England mansion.
Dr. Julian Blair is engaged in unconvent.outcomes: Protagonist's obsession with contacting the dead leads to his downfall, but no clear resolution of conflict is achieved. The conflict remains unresolved with a sense of impending doom.
Dr. Julian Blair is engaged in unconvent.theme: grief; obsession; the dangers of delving into forbidden knowledge

Police Sgt. Whitey Brandon works for the.coa:
A police officer works for the Vice Squad and is determined to combat corruption in the city.
He encounters a model who is sent to frame him and succeeds in doing so.
The model's sister visits and is brutally assaulted by a thug who knows the model.
The model, driven by desperation for revenge, enlists the help of the officer she previously framed.
Police Sgt. Whitey Brandon works for the.outcomes: Protagonist uncovers corruption and brings perpetrators to justice. The conflict is resolved with conflict_resolved and personal transformation as protagonist works alongside Carol to achieve their goals.
Police Sgt. Whitey Brandon works for the.theme: corruption; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Steven Schoichet is a recently unemploye.coa:
The protagonist becomes unemployed.
He struggles with self-expression due to this new status.
He develops a skill for ventriloquism, which helps him communicate more effectively.
Fangora "Fanny" Gurkel, his friend, is introduced as an aspiring musician. 
Fanny learns about a klezmer music opportunity and takes interest in the genre.
She convinces her friend to join her in pursuing this new musical direction.
Through ventriloquism, the protagonist overcomes social difficulties with the help of his dummy.
He decides to use his newfound confidence to impress Lorena Fanchetti.
Steven Schoichet is a recently unemploye.outcomes: Protagonist finds a new sense of purpose through ventriloquism and begins to overcome social difficulties. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and potential romantic success.
Steven Schoichet is a recently unemploye.theme: self-expression; overcoming adversity; love as empowerment

The film begins by detailing the trouble.coa:
Uwe argues with his wife in the morning, feeling overburdened by their four young children and their stressful lives together.
As a result of this argument, Uwe leaves for work, while his wife packs up most of their belongings and moves out without him noticing.
When Uwe returns home from work, he finds a note from his wife explaining that she has left him.
Uwe is immediately distressed to tears upon reading the note.
The film begins by detailing the trouble.outcomes: Protagonist Uwe undergoes a transformative experience with his brother. The conflict is unresolved, but both brothers are on a path towards self-discovery.
The film begins by detailing the trouble.theme: self-discovery; inner turmoil; relationships under stress

Wilma Tuttle (Young) is a college profes.coa:
A college professor becomes the target of sexual interest from a student.
The student tries to rape the professor, who is terrified and fights back unintentionally, killing him with an auto part.
The professor covers up her crime by making it seem like the student was killed while diving into the sea from a cliff.
The police investigation into the student's death begins.
Wilma Tuttle (Young) is a college profes.outcomes: Protagonist is trapped by her own guilt and conscience. The conflict is unresolved due to internalized trauma and inability to escape consequences.
Wilma Tuttle (Young) is a college profes.theme: guilt; accountability; the weight of secrets

Baxtiyor (played by Baxtiyor Ixtiyorov).coa:
Baxtiyor falls in love with a girl he sees on TV.
He decides to find her and travels across the Uzbek SSR with his father.
They meet many different people and experience both funny and sad adventures, but do not find Zulfiya.
Upon returning to Tashkent, Baxtiyor's family moves to a new flat from their old house in an old part of the city.
Baxtiyor finds out that he and Zulfiya are neighbors in the new apartment block.
Baxtiyor (played by Baxtiyor Ixtiyorov).outcomes: Protagonist achieves a happy ending with romantic resolution. The conflict is resolved with personal growth through perseverance.
Baxtiyor (played by Baxtiyor Ixtiyorov).theme: love; chance encounters; connection across distance

Ryan is a tough-minded British businessm.coa: A British businessman steals a spaceship with a small group of family and friends, setting out for a planet suitable for colonization. He seals his companions in cabinets designed for suspended animation, intending them to remain that way for the duration of the journey. The businessman makes daily reports on each companion's condition, always noting it as "Condition Steady". As time passes, he begins to experience nightmares and memories of violence from Earth.
Ryan is a tough-minded British businessm.outcomes: Protagonist's mental state is unresolved, with ongoing fear of losing grip on reality. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.
Ryan is a tough-minded British businessm.theme: sanity vs madness; isolation and loneliness; human resilience in the face of uncertainty

The story of a young woman who returns t.coa: A young woman leaves her childhood home for the big city to pursue an elusive dream. She spends years trying to achieve this goal, but ultimately returns home unsuccessful. Upon returning home, she finds that her mother is no longer there. The absence of her mother triggers a series of memories and flashbacks about her mother's life in the village.
The story of a young woman who returns t.outcomes: Protagonist comes to terms with her past decisions and finds a new sense of purpose in reconnecting with her roots. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and a renewed appreciation for the value of home and community.
The story of a young woman who returns t.theme: homecoming; reevaluation of priorities; the power of love and community

Freerunner sees eight freerunners racing.coa: Eight freerunners are selected to participate in a high-stakes racing event after performing well in local non-lethal races. The runners are fitted with collars containing explosives that will detonate under certain conditions, including leaving the designated green zone or being more than three seconds behind the previous racer at each checkpoint. The objective is for the first runner to reach the final checkpoint within 60 minutes and win a million-dollar prize. The race begins, and the runners start racing against time.
Freerunner sees eight freerunners racing.outcomes: Protagonist survives the competition and wins the prize of one million dollars. The conflict is resolved with external success but broader threats remain due to the involvement of international gangsters and businessmen.
Freerunner sees eight freerunners racing.theme: survival under pressure; exploitation for entertainment; human value vs monetary gain

After the destruction of much of coastal.coa: Destruction occurs in coastal areas of Turkey, causing widespread damage. A team of experts from around the world is assembled to pilot a five-person submarine. The crew sets out on their mission, traveling through the world's oceans and planting sensors on the ocean floor to warn scientists of impending earthquakes. The sensors are designed to detect changes in the Earth's crust that could indicate an earthquake is about to occur.
After the destruction of much of coastal.outcomes: Protagonist achieves success in deploying sensors, mitigating future earthquake damage. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as crew learns to work together effectively.

Jean-Pierre Werner, a country doctor who.coa: A country doctor is diagnosed with an inoperable brain tumor. The doctor's medical team advises them to retire and rest due to their health condition. A lady doctor from the city arrives at the rural area to assist with the doctor's practice. The presence of the new doctor causes tension for the ailing doctor, who feels threatened by the arrangement.
Jean-Pierre Werner, a country doctor who.outcomes: Protagonist accepts reality of his condition and begins transition towards retirement. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation but broader existential questions remain.

Ellie Parker is the story of an Australi.coa: The protagonist lives in Los Angeles, struggling to make it as an actress. She attends auditions back and forth across the city, often changing wardrobes and applying makeup en route. Her boyfriend leaves her, contributing to her dissatisfaction with her life. Her best friend, another out-of-work actress, attends acting classes with her to stay sharp.
Ellie Parker is the story of an Australi.outcomes: Protagonist gains new insights into her career and personal life, leading to potential growth and transformation. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader challenges remain.
Ellie Parker is the story of an Australi.theme: self-discovery; navigating uncertainty; finding one's place in the world

Twenty-five-year-old Lucile is the beaut.coa:
Lucile meets a charming young man her own age, Antoine.
She falls in love with him and he finds her a menial job in a publishing firm.
She becomes pregnant with his child despite being unable or unwilling to hold down the job.
Antoine accepts Charles' funding of an abortion against his initial wishes.
Twenty-five-year-old Lucile is the beaut.outcomes: Protagonist Lucile transitions back into relationship with Charles and achieves stability. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation, as she prioritizes material security over emotional connection.
Twenty-five-year-old Lucile is the beaut.theme: materialism vs emotional fulfillment; love vs convenience; self-discovery

Angela de Marco is the wife of Long Isla.coa:
"Cucumber" Frank de Marco is discovered in a compromising situation with Tony "The Tiger" Russo's mistress, leading to his violent dispatch by Tony.
Tony puts the moves on Angela at Frank's funeral, harassing her and causing suspicion among FBI agents Mike Downey and Ed Benitez who are conducting surveillance.
Downey becomes romantically involved with Angela as part of an undercover operation to monitor her movements.
Connie repeatedly confronts Angela with accusations of stealing her husband.
Angela de Marco is the wife of Long Isla.outcomes: Protagonist achieves temporary escape from immediate danger but broader threats remain. The conflict is unresolved with partial personal transformation and relational change.
Angela de Marco is the wife of Long Isla.theme: escape vs entrapment; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

The story revolves around Michelle, an a.coa:
A younger sibling becomes involved in a robbery.
The sibling's involvement leads them into a dark world of underground trades involving DNA Hackers, Loan Sharks, and Gang Fights.
Michelle attempts to keep her sibling away from this world but ultimately fails as the sibling's desire for respect draws them deeper into danger.
The sibling's actions put them at risk of being targeted by DNA Hackers.
The story revolves around Michelle, an a.outcomes: Protagonist Michelle fails to prevent her brother from being drawn into trouble but ultimately holds on to hope that love can conquer all and family bonds are unbreakable. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing threats from DNA Hackers, Loan Sharks, and Gang Fights.
The story revolves around Michelle, an a.theme: redemption; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Three young men (Jacques, Pierre and Mic.coa: A friend of one of them tells Jacques he has a compromising package to deliver and asks him to leave it at their place discreetly. Jacques agrees and leaves for Japan on a one-month trip, telling Pierre and Michel about the package. One of Jacques' former girlfriends drops off a baby before their door, leading Pierre and Michel to believe it is the package they are waiting for. They soon realize that the baby is not the package, but rather a child left at their doorstep.
Three young men (Jacques, Pierre and Mic.outcomes: Protagonists face failure in their carefree lives due to the arrival of the baby and the consequences of Jacques' actions. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing threats from gangsters and the need for personal transformation.
Three young men (Jacques, Pierre and Mic.theme: responsibility; chaos vs order; moral accountability

Jessica has moved from her small Burgund.coa: A young woman moves from her small town to Paris to start a new life. She initially struggles to find work and spends one evening without shelter. She eventually gets a job waitressing at a small café due to an impending influx of customers. The café is located near several artistic venues, including a theatre and concert hall.
Jessica has moved from her small Burgund.outcomes: Protagonist finds stable employment and a sense of belonging in the city. The conflict is partially resolved with personal growth but broader challenges remain.

This third novel of Updike's Rabbit seri.coa:
Harry reaches middle-age and has not relocated from Brewer, Pennsylvania.
He lives comfortably with Janice, who inherited her late father's Toyota dealership, making him rich.
His problems persist, including Janice's drinking, his son's schemes, and his libido.
He becomes smitten with a country-club friend's young wife.
This third novel of Updike's Rabbit seri.outcomes: Protagonist is stuck in a state of dissatisfaction and regret, unable to move forward. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal and relational issues.

The film centres around a Will-they won'.coa: A wealthy man runs off from his fiancée to New York City. He attends an Independence Day block party in Manhattan. A woman falls in love with him at the party. The woman and her flatmate live above a grocer's shop where comic relief is provided by the grocer.
The film centres around a Will-they won'.outcomes: Protagonist finds love with Molly Carr and resolves the will-they-won't-they romance. The conflict is resolved with a romantic relationship.
The film centres around a Will-they won'.theme: love vs societal expectations; chance encounters; freedom of choice

In 1923, the young Kim Shun-Pei moves fr.coa:
The young person moves from Cheju Island to Osaka in 1923.
They become a cruel, greedy, and violent man over the years.
They build a factory of processed seafood products and exploit their employees.
Their behavior causes them to make a fortune, but also destroys the lives of their family members.
In 1923, the young Kim Shun-Pei moves fr.outcomes: Protagonist dies alone in North Korea, having never changed his ways. The conflict is unresolved with no personal transformation or social reintegration.
In 1923, the young Kim Shun-Pei moves fr.theme: exploitation; toxic masculinity; the cyclical nature of abuse

In a fictional, Central European kingdom.coa: The ruler's kingdom faces financial difficulties due to bankruptcy. The ruler is forced to consider marrying a wealthy ruler from another territory to save his country. In an attempt to find a suitable bride, the ruler visits various locations, including a beer garden where he sees the young singer performing. He becomes interested in her and decides to take her in as a favor.
In a fictional, Central European kingdom.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a stable relationship with the ruler, securing her future. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation and financial stability.
In a fictional, Central European kingdom.theme: love vs duty; social class differences; economic survival

Harvard educated Dr. David Lowell's (Col.coa: Halley's Comet passes over the earth. Dr. David Lowell conducts research in the canyon country of southern Utah, using the sound waves generated by the comet to search for a safe and cheap energy source. The Pilgrim Corporation decides that the same canyon would be better used as a remote place to illegally dump nuclear waste. They lease the land from themselves (having previously leased it to Dr. Lowell).
Harvard educated Dr. David Lowell's (Col.outcomes: Protagonist successfully recovers his land and sabotages the nuclear waste operation, but broader environmental threats remain unresolved. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation for protagonist and some social change, but systemic issues persist.
Harvard educated Dr. David Lowell's (Col.theme: environmental degradation; corporate greed vs individual rights; human resilience in the face of adversity

Ben, Sonny, Lloyd, Sam and Ronnie are fr.coa: Five friends make a pact that if one among them died, the other four would bury him next to the grave of their idol. Twelve years pass and one of the five dies after being struck by lightning while playing golf. The remaining four unite and decide to fulfill their promise. They retrieve the deceased's cremated remains and plan a road trip to scatter his ashes over Fremantle Cemetery.
Ben, Sonny, Lloyd, Sam and Ronnie are fr.outcomes: Protagonist group successfully fulfills their promise by scattering Ronnie's ashes over Fremantle Cemetery. The conflict is resolved with a sense of closure and completion, but also acknowledges the passing of time and mortality.

The Elephant Whisperers tells the story.coa: An indigenous couple is entrusted with an orphaned baby Indian elephant. They take great pains to ensure the infant survives and grows into a healthy juvenile. A strong bond develops between the couple and the elephant as it matures. The couple adopts another elephant, allowing the first elephant to have companionship.
The Elephant Whisperers tells the story.outcomes: The protagonist couple successfully raises Raghu into a healthy juvenile, fostering a strong bond with the elephant. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and environmental awareness through coexistence.

The 50-year-old guy Mané who was a resid.coa:
A 50-year-old pubkeeper and youth football trainer is reminded daily of his past success as a goalkeeper.
His old admiration from the community is slowly disappearing due to his current lack of opportunity.
He wants to switch teams, specifically S.L. Benfica, but is unable to do so because he's no longer considered talented enough.
Against his wife's wishes, he travels to Lisbon for a finals match and to help a young player named Kalu gain an opportunity with Benfica.
The 50-year-old guy Mané who was a resid.outcomes: Protagonist Mané returns home sobered and unchanged in his circumstances. The conflict is unresolved with no personal transformation or resolution.
The 50-year-old guy Mané who was a resid.theme: decline of fame; missed opportunities; generational differences

Luicha returns briefly to her home town.coa:
Luicha visits her hometown briefly.
She claims to work as a teacher in Paris.
A young man falls in love with her and asks for her hand in marriage.
He proposes, but Luicha rejects him and quickly leaves the town.

This rejection prompts the young man to follow Luicha to Paris.
Upon arrival, he discovers that Luicha is actually famous there under an alias.
Luicha returns briefly to her home town.outcomes: Protagonist maintains professional life in Paris while keeping personal life separate from hometown relationships. The conflict is unresolved with a sense of tension between Luicha's dual identities.

The film opens with a luff tracing the b.coa: A wealthy expatriate and Egyptian residents live in the building, but they give way to new families after the 1952 coup. The rooftop storage rooms are converted into living space for lower-class families, creating a slum neighborhood on the roof. As the population grows, especially among the poor and working classes, the building's main floors and rooftop become overcrowded and impoverished. This leads to the introduction of the film's principal characters, whose stories intertwine and collide with one another.
The film opens with a luff tracing the b.outcomes: The protagonist (wealthy expatriate) transitions to a new relationship with the disillusioned young girl, offering a glimmer of hope amidst the death and unhappiness. The conflict is unresolved, as the corrupt political system remains in place.

The King Beyond the Gate takes place a c.coa:
A century passes since the events of Legend: Druss The Legend.
The Earl of bronze becomes a legend in his own right.
Ceska, an usurper, seizes power from the rightful ruler of the Drenai.
Over time, Ceska becomes a mad and power-hungry emperor who rules with an iron fist.
The King Beyond the Gate takes place a c.outcomes: Protagonist succeeds in overthrowing the tyrant Ceska. The conflict is resolved with conflict_resolved and personal transformation as Tenaka Khan emerges as a new leader.

An evil Archbishop and his followers sum.coa:
An evil Archbishop and his followers summon a demon.
The demon kills its summoners in a unique manner by turning their bodies into solid shadow that disintegrates at the first touch of light.
The creature gains strength and solidity with each kill, allowing it to resist light more effectively and affect those around it, including a pack of vicious dogs.
The town is orchestrated towards destruction as the demon hunts down a particular child.
An evil Archbishop and his followers sum.outcomes: Protagonist (child) is saved from demon's attack, but world remains threatened by demon's presence. The conflict is unresolved with a powerful evil entity still present.

A young student from Russia (Conrad Veid.coa:
A young student from Russia is studying in Geneva, Switzerland, but wants to go home due to lack of funds.
The student is recruited by a group of Nihilists who need someone to go to Russia for their plot.
The student agrees to be their agent and travels to Russia.
In Russia, the student meets a young Grand Duchess and they fall in love.

A young student from Russia (Conrad Veid.theme: lost love; sacrifice for others; the cost of ambition

Fanfan is a charming, attractive young F.coa: A young Frenchman is approached by the daughter of a recruiting officer who promises him fame, fortune, and marriage to the king's daughter if he joins the army. He signs up for the army based on this promise. The daughter made up the entire story in order to receive a recruiting bonus from her father.

Fanfan is a charming, attractive young F.theme: deception vs sincerity; self-discovery; conflicting desires

The film is set in the Lüneburg Heath re.coa: A poacher begins to evade capture by a game warden. The poacher kills a police officer who tries to stop him. The game warden becomes determined to catch the poacher after the murder. The game warden starts an affair with the poacher's daughter, while she is also involved in a secret relationship with her father.

The film is set in the Lüneburg Heath re.theme: redemption; love vs duty; human relationships

In New York City, a hospital worker is f.coa:
A hospital worker is found to have been devouring bodies in the morgue.
The discovery leads to an investigation by Dr. Peter Chandler, who teams up with a morgue assistant and anthropology expert.
They discover that similar corpse mutilations have occurred in other city hospitals, where immigrants from a specific region are working.
This leads Dr. Peter Chandler to organize an expedition to investigate the source of the problem on the islands where these immigrants come from.
In New York City, a hospital worker is f.outcomes: Protagonist (Peter) survives and resolves the immediate conflict with Doctor Obrero's defeat. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation for Peter, but broader threats remain due to the presence of zombies.
