The film begins with an unnamed man arri.coa:
An unnamed man arrives by train to Helsinki.
He falls asleep in Kaisaniemi Park, where he is mugged and beaten by hoodlums, resulting in severe head injuries that cause him to lose consciousness.
He awakens and wanders back to the train station, where he collapses in its toilet.
He awakens a second time in a hospital, only to discover that he has lost his memory.
The film begins with an unnamed man arri.outcomes: Protagonist survives physical trauma and starts life from scratch. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal struggle for identity and stability.

Nete is a strong woman of 35 who manages.coa:
The widowed father becomes ill.
Nete discovers he may have only three weeks to live.
She insists that he leaves his flat and moves in with her.
He does not die as expected.
Nete is a strong woman of 35 who manages.outcomes: Protagonist's family is strained, but ultimately intact. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing tension between Nete and her father.
Nete is a strong woman of 35 who manages.theme: family dynamics; caregiving responsibilities; intergenerational relationships

Set in Casablanca shortly after World Wa.coa: Heinrich Stubel murders three managers of the Hotel Casablanca to gain control. The newest manager is hired, unaware that he was chosen because no one else would take the position. Inept Kornblow takes charge and meets Corbaccio, who becomes his bodyguard with Rusty's help. Stubel sends Beatrice Reiner to romance Kornblow in an attempt to distract him while he continues to murder managers.
Set in Casablanca shortly after World Wa.outcomes: Protagonist survives and is exonerated after exposing antagonist. The conflict is resolved with conflict_resolved and personal transformation for protagonist.
Set in Casablanca shortly after World Wa.theme: justice vs prejudice; survival under adversity; loyalty vs duty

The film is set in Bradford, a working-c.coa: Ali lives with his soon-to-be ex-wife Runa in Bradford's Holme Wood housing estate. Ali drops off his tenant's daughter at the school where Ava works. Ava and Ali meet, and a friendship develops between them based on their shared love of music. As they spend more time together, their friendship deepens into something romantic.
The film is set in Bradford, a working-c.outcomes: Ali and Ava form a romantic relationship, overcoming their initial familial entanglements and prejudices. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation through the power of human connection.
The film is set in Bradford, a working-c.theme: human connection; love transcends adversity; redemption through relationships

Singles centers on the precarious romant.coa: The film focuses on the romantic lives of a group of young people in Seattle at the height of the 1990s grunge phenomenon. A sign in front of an apartment block advertises "Singles" (single bedroom apartments) for rent, where most of the characters dwell. Two couples' rocky romances are highlighted: one couple is wavering on whether to commit to each other, while another woman is trying to find her perfect match. The musician Cliff Poncier becomes a central figure in the story, with Janet Livermore fawning over him.
Singles centers on the precarious romant.outcomes: Janet fails to win Cliff's heart, while Linda and Steve reconcile their differences. The conflict is unresolved with personal growth but no clear resolution.
Singles centers on the precarious romant.theme: unrequited love; relationship uncertainty; identity formation

In Greenland, Pipaluk awakes to find her.coa:
Pipaluk awakes to find her older sister missing.
Her father claims she has run away, but Pipaluk is distressed and believes Ivalu is coming to her in the form of a raven.
Pipaluk searches their old hangout spots together, reminiscing on past interactions with Ivalu.
It becomes clear that Ivalu was being abused by their father.
In Greenland, Pipaluk awakes to find her.outcomes: Protagonist is left with a deep sense of loss and grief. The conflict is unresolved as the abuse and its consequences remain unaddressed.

Bundeswehr soldier Klaus' regiment is st.coa:
The regiment is stationed in France for NATO maneuvers.
The soldiers are ordered to be kind to the populace due to past atrocities committed by German forces during World War II.
Klaus falls in love with the daughter of the local mayor.
His commanders intend to demolish a local church, where civilians were murdered by German occupation forces in 1944.
Bundeswehr soldier Klaus' regiment is st.outcomes: Protagonist's actions lead to exposure of war crime and potential justice for victims' families. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social change.
Bundeswehr soldier Klaus' regiment is st.theme: guilt and redemption; truth vs cover-up; accountability in power

Dr. Schön marries a lower-class girl, Lu.coa: The doctor marries a lower-class woman. The woman's youth and physical appearance attract the attention of many men. The doctor refuses to allow her to engage with these men, leading to tension in their relationship. The woman eventually shoots the doctor.
Dr. Schön marries a lower-class girl, Lu.outcomes: Protagonist's son is left with a difficult decision regarding his father's legacy. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing consequences for the family.
Dr. Schön marries a lower-class girl, Lu.theme: class differences; possessiveness vs freedom; family dynamics

Matsushima is outside the prison and on.coa:
Matsushima breaks out of prison and commits murder.
The police issue a warrant for her arrest, making her a fugitive on the run.
Detective Kondo is assigned to track down Matsushima.
Matsushima takes refuge with a woman who has a brother with a mental disability.
Matsushima is outside the prison and on.outcomes: Protagonist evades capture and escapes immediate danger. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing pursuit by authority and personal consequences.
Matsushima is outside the prison and on.theme: survival under adversity; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

In a typical Lisbon \"pátio\", or courty.coa:
A handful of plain people live their day-to-day lives in an almost enchanted atmosphere in a typical Lisbon "pátio", or courtyard, by the Popular Saints festivals.
The brother of one of these people flirts with Amália, who is also courted by Narciso and has a sister in love with Alfredo.
Narciso's rival for Amália's affections, Evaristo, courts Rosa, a merry widow who sells flowers.
Rosa is also courted by Narciso, leading to a rivalry between him and Evaristo that reaches its height during a dance night at the courtyard.
The rivalry culminates in a camp battle.
In a typical Lisbon \"pátio\", or courty.outcomes: The community reaches a state of harmony with all romantic relationships settled. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation as the characters find love and acceptance.
In a typical Lisbon \"pátio\", or courty.theme: love vs rivalry; social harmony; acceptance

Joong-man, stuck in a thankless job and.coa: A huge bag of money is left in a sauna. A customs officer plots with one of Mr. Park's henchmen to run a scam on someone attempting to flee the country. The three characters all cross paths with dangerous people as they attempt to cheat their way toward the ultimate payout, and Mi-ran sees a way out when one of her clients offers to murder her abusive husband in exchange for a cut of his life insurance policy.
Joong-man, stuck in a thankless job and.outcomes: Protagonist loses house but gains new life through retrieved money. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation and financial gain.
Joong-man, stuck in a thankless job and.theme: greed vs survival; moral compromise; chance and fate

During the American Civil War, Clyde McK.coa: A group of skilled fighters and unrepentant murderers are recruited by Clyde McKay for a mission to steal a box of gold from a Union Western camp. The Confederate Intelligence Captain Lynch orders McKay that once he has completed his mission, he is to "kill them all and come back alone". McKay's group sets out on their mission, led by Lynch, who is hiding his true intentions. As they approach the Union camp, they face opposition from the Union Army.
During the American Civil War, Clyde McK.outcomes: Protagonist's group is defeated and likely killed, with the mission failing to achieve its objectives. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.
During the American Civil War, Clyde McK.theme: loyalty vs duty; survival under adversity; morality in war

Bobby Baldano (William DeMeo) is the bla.coa:
Bobby gets out of prison after serving a five-year sentence.
He is expected by his family to start working at their contracting business, which has been built up from nothing over multiple generations.
However, he quickly falls back into a life of crime due to his connections with the mob.
This leads him to be pulled between two families: one that is supportive and loving, and another that is dangerous and deadly.
Bobby Baldano (William DeMeo) is the bla.outcomes: Protagonist's life is marked by failure and continued involvement in crime. The conflict is unresolved with no personal transformation or social reintegration.
Bobby Baldano (William DeMeo) is the bla.theme: loyalty vs duty; family obligations; blurred lines between good and evil

In his novel, Gorky portrays the life of.coa: A woman works hard in a factory, struggling with poverty and hunger. Her husband physically assaults her and leaves her to care for their son alone. He unexpectedly dies, leaving the woman to raise their son on her own. The son begins to emulate his father's behavior, but then suddenly becomes involved in revolutionary activities.
In his novel, Gorky portrays the life of.outcomes: Protagonist (Nilovna) achieves personal growth by overcoming her political ignorance and supporting the revolution. The conflict is resolved with partial resolution of social issues through the influence of the revolution.
In his novel, Gorky portrays the life of.theme: resilience; maternal love vs societal expectations; class struggle

Prussia, 1750. After the end of the Sile.coa: Frederick II returns to Sanssouci exhausted from the Silesian Wars. The mill of Casper starts rattling loudly, disturbing Frederick's rest. He issues an edict to silence the mill and its owner during times of peace. Casper refuses to comply with the decree, citing equality before the law in Prussia.
Prussia, 1750. After the end of the Sile.outcomes: The conflict is resolved through reconciliation with no clear winner or loser. The resolution involves personal transformation as the king finds alternative ways to address his issues.
Prussia, 1750. After the end of the Sile.theme: equality before the law; power vs individual rights; social harmony

Fourteen-year-old Terry Bowen travels fr.coa:
A 14-year-old boy travels from the U.S. to India to meet his father for the first time.
The boy has a dispute with his father, leading him to run away.
He is befriended by another boy who takes care of him.
Together, they have many adventures in the jungles of India.
Fourteen-year-old Terry Bowen travels fr.outcomes: Protagonist (Terry) forms a strong bond with his father and learns about Indian culture. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and understanding between people from different backgrounds.
Fourteen-year-old Terry Bowen travels fr.theme: cultural understanding; interfaith tolerance; overcoming adversity

The singer Caterine, owner of a bar/nigh.coa:
Caterine wants to marry her boyfriend but discovers she is allegedly already married to Baron Hubert von Löwenherz at the registrar's office.
The discovery prompts Caterine to travel to the Baron's castle to resolve the situation.
Upon arrival, a visit by the Baron's rich uncle forces him to pretend that he and Caterine are happily married in order to maintain his family's reputation.
Caterine stays with the Baron as his supposed wife.
The singer Caterine, owner of a bar/nigh.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a new relationship with the Baron, resolving initial conflict. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and relational change.
The singer Caterine, owner of a bar/nigh.theme: love vs identity; deception and trust; unexpected relationships

Pierre Durois is a mathematics teacher a.coa:
Pierre's students switch the contents of his folders as a joke.
The switched files are discovered by someone other than Pierre, causing a mix-up that leads to unexpected consequences.
A speech meant for the mayor, an article about Jackie Logan, and student papers end up in the wrong hands.
The mix-up is eventually resolved when Pierre finds himself on a film set trying to clear up the confusion.
Pierre Durois is a mathematics teacher a.outcomes: Protagonist survives public scandal with his reputation intact. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing media attention and potential consequences for protagonist's relationships.
Pierre Durois is a mathematics teacher a.theme: reputation vs privacy; celebrity culture; personal responsibility

The film examines the eternal problem of.coa: A sound engineer and musician encounters many problems and setbacks in his work and personal life. A Canadian company invites him to record sounds of animals in Transcarpathia. He fulfills the assignment with enthusiasm, but his mother comes along and disrupts his work. The engineer is tasked with recording the voice of a rare Rakhiv mallard.
The film examines the eternal problem of.outcomes: Protagonist successfully completes assignment and gains opportunity to travel to Canada. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal problems.
The film examines the eternal problem of.theme: parent-child relationships; creative fulfillment vs personal struggles; leaving the past behind

The King of Dunark has been murdered alo.coa: The King of Dunark and his pregnant wife are murdered along with their 4-year-old son. The elder son is found dead drunk, holding a dagger in his hand, making him the apparent killer. The Master of Law sends for Melussina Tonerre, a "witch" who can probe someone's mind, to investigate the crime. Melussina finds no shame in the elder son for the royal family's slaughter.
The King of Dunark has been murdered alo.outcomes: Protagonist is cleared of suspicion and guilt due to lack of evidence in his mind. The conflict is resolved with a partial resolution as justice is served, but underlying issues remain.
The King of Dunark has been murdered alo.theme: innocence vs guilt; truth vs deception; redemption

A woman lives alone on the outskirts of.coa: A woman receives a parcel she had sent to her incarcerated husband, marked 'return to sender'. The parcel's contents cause her shock and confusion. She decides to travel to the prison where the parcel was returned from in search of an explanation. Her journey begins as she sets out towards the remote region of the country where the prison is located.
A woman lives alone on the outskirts of.outcomes: Protagonist uncovers truth about prison's corrupt practices and secures justice for her husband. The conflict is resolved with systemic change, exposing corruption within the prison system.
A woman lives alone on the outskirts of.theme: justice vs oppression; resilience in adversity; fighting against institutional evil

Invasion of the Sea takes place in a fut.coa: European engineers propose flooding the Sahara desert with waters from the Mediterranean Sea to create an inland "Sahara Sea". The French military escort, led by Captain Hardigan, meets with conflict from Tuareg Berber tribes who fear the new sea will threaten their nomadic way of life. The Berber tribes begin an insurgency campaign against the Europeans in an effort to derail their plans for the inland sea. The European engineers and military retaliate against the Berbers, led by a warlord named Hadjar.
Invasion of the Sea takes place in a fut.outcomes: The conflict is resolved with a catastrophic outcome for the Tuareg Berber tribes, who are drowned by the flood. The European plan is inadvertently fulfilled, but at a devastating cost to the local population and ecosystem.
Invasion of the Sea takes place in a fut.theme: colonialism vs indigenous culture; unintended consequences of technological advancement; environmental disaster

Writing with Fire tells the story of Kha.coa: A group of Dalit women start a news agency using smartphones. They begin reporting from difficult regions of the country, often risking everything to speak truth to power. The journalists face challenges while covering urgent stories and confronting those in positions of authority. Over time, they gain recognition for their work and redefinition of what it means to be powerful begins within their community.
Writing with Fire tells the story of Kha.outcomes: Protagonist achieves empowerment through reporting truth to power, redefining what it means to be powerful. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social change.
Writing with Fire tells the story of Kha.theme: empowerment; challenging power structures; women's agency

Ten-year-old Chris Marino returns home a.coa:
A ten-year-old boy returns home after staying with relatives due to his mother's worsening condition.
His mother's delusions and paranoia cause her to be ostracized by the neighbors, who also call the police on multiple occasions.
The mother is eventually institutionalized when her medication fails to control her symptoms.
The boy is left in the care of his father, who has been avoiding responsibility by building a boat in the family driveway.
Ten-year-old Chris Marino returns home a.outcomes: Protagonist finds solace in creative expression and gains acceptance from peers. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation through creative outlet and social reintegration.
Ten-year-old Chris Marino returns home a.theme: coping with mental illness; family dynamics; self-expression

Starting in late May 1944, during the Ge.coa: In late May 1944, a railway tunnel is ordered to be blown up by Captain to prevent Russian forces from using it. The mission fails due to a Russian tank. A platoon is then transferred to France and occupies the village of St Bologne. General Hoffman orders Steiner to cross into enemy territory to confer with American officers.
Starting in late May 1944, during the Ge.outcomes: Protagonist survives the conflict but ultimately betrays his own side by killing fellow soldiers. The conflict is unresolved as American forces continue to fight against German forces in St Bologne.
Starting in late May 1944, during the Ge.theme: loyalty vs duty; moral ambiguity; survival under adversity

Martin Hoff, a steel smelter and an amat.coa:
A factory committee sends Martin Hoff to a drama school.
He is expelled from the drama school due to his troublesome conduct.
Hoff meets a young woman who is unimpressed by him and he makes a bet with friends that he can charm her.
He begins working at a construction site where she serves as group manager.
Martin Hoff, a steel smelter and an amat.outcomes: Protagonist achieves personal growth through hard work and finds romantic partner. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation and social integration.

Carolina Mirabeau was raised 'free-spiri.coa: Carolina is raised by her grandma in the country. She has two sisters and is described as free-spirited. She develops a close friendship with Albert, who lives nearby and often visits her family. Carolina meets Heath on a TV studio set where she works as a screener for a dating show.
Carolina Mirabeau was raised 'free-spiri.outcomes: Protagonist Carolina's relationships are revealed to be complicated by her past choices. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal and relational consequences.
Carolina Mirabeau was raised 'free-spiri.theme: complicated relationships; social class vs authenticity; truth vs deception

Tih Minh tells the story of Jacques d'At.coa: An expedition to Indochina ends, and Jacques d'Athys returns home to Nice with a young woman from Laos named Tih Minh. Upon their return, Athys becomes involved with an international group of jewel thieves-cum-spies who are also present in Nice. The group includes a mysterious noble person, a hypnotist, and an evil doctor who can render people amnesiacs. This involvement is facilitated by the fact that Athys has unknowingly brought back a book containing a coded message.
Tih Minh tells the story of Jacques d'At.outcomes: Protagonist survives and manages to evade capture. The conflict is unresolved as spies continue to pursue them.
Tih Minh tells the story of Jacques d'At.theme: deception; loyalty vs duty; power of information

Americanah is about Ifemelu and Obinze w.coa: Teenagers fall in love in a Lagos secondary school. The country is under military dictatorship, leading people to seek to leave. One teenager moves to the United States to study, where they struggle with racism for the first time. This experience leads them to discover what it means to be a "Black Person".
Americanah is about Ifemelu and Obinze w.outcomes: Protagonists achieve personal growth and identity formation through their struggles abroad. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader issues of racism and identity remain.

\"The French Riviera _ _ in the Twenties.coa: A distinguished psychiatrist falls in love with a patient, leading him to marry her despite warnings from his mentor that it will ruin his career. He abandons his work to indulge his wife's many whims, leading a hedonistic life paid for by her sophisticated sister. Years pass and he realizes the error of his ways, attempting to resume his career. His wife has found a new lover and wants a divorce.
\"The French Riviera _ _ in the Twenties.outcomes: Protagonist (Dick Diver) fails in his attempt to revive his career, while protagonist (Nicole) achieves a new relationship. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation for both characters.
\"The French Riviera _ _ in the Twenties.theme: the dangers of emotional manipulation; the corrupting influence of wealth and privilege; the fragility of relationships

Moderately successful criminal defense a.coa: A moderately successful criminal defense attorney operates around Los Angeles County out of a Lincoln Town Car driven by a former client working off his legal fees. The attorney takes on a new case involving a wealthy realtor accused of assault and attempted murder, who initially appears to be innocent and set up by the female "victim". As the case unfolds, the realtor's lies and surprising revelations change the attorney's original case theory. This leads him to reconsider the situation of Jesus Menendez, a former client serving time in prison after pleading guilty to a similar crime.
Moderately successful criminal defense a.outcomes: Protagonist successfully defends client and resolves conflict, but also acquires emotional baggage. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and professional growth.
Moderately successful criminal defense a.theme: justice vs corruption; redemption through truth; the blurred lines between right and wrong

Shortly after the Second World War, Mari.coa:
The Second World War ends.
Maria marries a fellow lawyer who was severely wounded in action.
She believes her first husband was killed in action and marries the second man out of pity.
Several years pass.
Georg, Maria's first husband, resurfaces alive in Leipzig in East Germany.
He tries to resume their former relationship with Maria.
Shortly after the Second World War, Mari.outcomes: Protagonist remains with current partner and prioritizes their relationship. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and acceptance of past circumstances.
Shortly after the Second World War, Mari.theme: true love; loyalty vs commitment; moving on from the past

This film tells the story of a young gir.coa: The young girl lives with her parents in the north of Spain. Her father shares with her the art of divination using a pendulum as she grows up. A family visit from her grandmother reveals a past conflict between her father and grandfather, who supported opposing sides during the Spanish Civil War. The grandfather's support for Franco led to a falling out between him and his son.
This film tells the story of a young gir.outcomes: Protagonist gains insight into her father's past and his unresolved love for Irene Rios. The conflict is partially resolved with personal growth but underlying emotions remain.
This film tells the story of a young gir.theme: family secrets; love vs loyalty; unresolved past

Reisiger travels over the world, in sear.coa:
Reisiger travels the world in search of news stories.
He meets a woman who is pregnant when they meet.
After her pregnancy, they get married.
He continues to leave her after their marriage.
A colleague dies and tells Reisiger to return home.
Reisiger returns home to his wife.
World War II breaks out, forcing him to leave again.
Reisiger travels over the world, in sear.outcomes: Protagonist's relationship with his family is strained and unresolved. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal and relational challenges.
Reisiger travels over the world, in sear.theme: absence; loyalty vs duty; love vs responsibility

Colonel Jack Knowles (Roy Scheider) is a.coa: A decorated soldier is stationed at an outpost on the West German-Czechoslovakia border. A would-be refugee is shot on the border, and the soldier becomes enraged. He begins crossing the border on solo missions to sabotage enemy installations, despite orders from his superior to desist. The soldier's actions escalate tensions with his Soviet counterpart, who shares similar characteristics.
Colonel Jack Knowles (Roy Scheider) is a.outcomes: Protagonist avoids full-scale war by relenting at the last moment. The conflict is resolved with no major consequences or changes to the broader situation.
Colonel Jack Knowles (Roy Scheider) is a.theme: militarism; national pride vs international cooperation; individual action vs institutional constraints

May 1919. The city of Petrograd, the Bol.coa:
The White Army of General Nikolai Yudenich attacks the city of Petrograd.
The attack causes demoralization among the High Soviet, who are about to order an evacuation.
The Krasnaya Gorka fort dispatches a detachment of Baltic Fleet sailors to assist Petrograd, including Vladimir Shibaev.
The Red Army faces defeat by the Whites.
May 1919. The city of Petrograd, the Bol.outcomes: The city of Petrograd is saved from defeat and occupation. The conflict is resolved with a decisive victory for the Red Army and the communists.
May 1919. The city of Petrograd, the Bol.theme: resistance against imperialism; loyalty to revolution; leadership in crisis

In the 1860s a widow runs an estate, clo.coa:
A widow runs an estate near the Italian border in the 1860s.
A former army officer arrives on his way to Italy.
The widow persuades him to stay and work for her.
They begin a romantic relationship.
Her daughter returns home from boarding school.
She develops an attachment to her mother's lover.
In the 1860s a widow runs an estate, clo.outcomes: Protagonist's relationship with former officer is complicated by daughter's attachment, leading to a complex family dynamic. The conflict is unresolved with relational change.
In the 1860s a widow runs an estate, clo.theme: love vs loyalty; social class; family dynamics

This prison drama is the story of Joe Hu.coa: A man is convicted of manslaughter and sentenced to prison. The warden helps him adjust to life inside and recognizes that he has potential for rehabilitation after release. The convict witnesses the murder of an informer by another prisoner, but refuses to speak out in accordance with the prison's code of silence. He is subsequently locked in solitary confinement as a result of his refusal to cooperate with authorities.
This prison drama is the story of Joe Hu.outcomes: Protagonist is exonerated and released from prison. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as protagonist starts a new life outside of prison.

Palin plays Francis Ashby, a senior Oxfo.coa: A professor is on holiday in the Swiss Alps in 1861. He meets two women there, one of whom is an adult and the other a minor ward. The women arrive at Oxford and rent a house, causing embarrassment for the professor due to the college's rules against women. This situation is exploited by his rival for the post of college president.
Palin plays Francis Ashby, a senior Oxfo.outcomes: Protagonist Francis Ashby is forced to navigate a complex social situation but ultimately maintains his position. The conflict is unresolved with respect to the college's rules and Ashby's personal life.
Palin plays Francis Ashby, a senior Oxfo.theme: social convention vs personal desire; class and privilege; love and relationships

Albrecht Froben, though married to Octav.coa:
Albrecht falls in love with his neighbor, who is slowly dying from a debilitating disease.
The epidemic breaks out, prompting Albrecht to go bring her daughter to safety, but he catches typhoid and is quarantined in hospital.
Octavia discovers Albrecht's affair and learns that Äls is now bedridden and dying.
Octavia dresses up as Albrecht and rides by Äls' gates every day to keep her spirits up.
Albrecht returns from the hospital, unaware of Octavia's actions.
Äls has a dream in which she talks to her projection of Albrecht and concludes that she does not wish to take part in this union and accepts death.
Albrecht is reconciled with his wife.
Albrecht Froben, though married to Octav.outcomes: Protagonist reconciles with his wife, Octavia. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as protagonist lets go of the unrequited love.

The film starts in medieval times, when.coa: A young couple is attacked by the Knights Templar, resulting in the man's instant death and the woman being taken to the Templars' castle. The woman is sacrificed at the castle. Centuries pass, and a doctor and his wife move into a coastal town. They are met with distrust and hatred from the locals due to their unfamiliarity with the area.
The film starts in medieval times, when.outcomes: Protagonist couple survives the ordeal with the sacrifice of the maiden, but the conflict is unresolved as the cycle of violence continues. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation and relational change through the protagonists' actions.
The film starts in medieval times, when.theme: cycle of violence; redemption through sacrifice; human resilience

Former police officer and luckless priva.coa: A former police officer turned emergency medical technician is fired from a second job due to past misconduct. He becomes an EMT for another organization and tries to keep a low profile. A well-known nurse is murdered in a hospital, but the EMT doesn't care about getting involved in the investigation. His young partner, however, wants to investigate the murder and drags him into it.
Former police officer and luckless priva.outcomes: Protagonist Brenner solves the case and returns to his quiet life. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as Brenner confronts his past and finds redemption.
Former police officer and luckless priva.theme: redemption; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

The teenage Chris is on holiday at Saint.coa: A teenage girl goes on holiday with her mother at Saint-Tropez while her father remains in Paris. She seduces a married friend of her parents and undergoes an abortion, leading to a desire for new sexual adventures. She falls for an older man who is interested in supplying young girls to rich men, but he becomes infatuated with her mother instead. The mother gradually warms up to his advances and becomes his lover.
The teenage Chris is on holiday at Saint.outcomes: Protagonist is left alone and possibly in danger. The conflict is unresolved with a personal transformation for protagonist's mother, but broader consequences remain.
The teenage Chris is on holiday at Saint.theme: toxic relationships; exploitation of youth; maternal complicity

The story is told by a man at a campfire.coa: A Dragoon Sergeant fell madly in love with a beautiful gypsy and killed an officer for her. He gave up his fiancée and career in the army to become a smuggler, all for this woman's love. She left him and went to Gibraltar where she fell in love with a famous bullfighter.
The story is told by a man at a campfire.outcomes: Protagonist Don José is left alone and broken after killing his former love. The conflict is unresolved with a sense of tragedy and loss.
The story is told by a man at a campfire.theme: all-consuming passion; destructive love; morality vs desire

It's in the middle of the summer. Sickan.coa:
The summer begins.
The Jönsson League has nothing to do until the circus comes to town.
Sickan quickly thinks out a plan on how to get tickets to the circus.
They obtain the tickets, but Junior Wall-Enberg steals them from them.
It's in the middle of the summer. Sickan.outcomes: Protagonist (Jönsson League) successfully retrieves stolen paintings and clears their name. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation through cleverness and resourcefulness.
It's in the middle of the summer. Sickan.theme: deception; loyalty vs betrayal; cunning over brute force

The young lieutenant Anton Hofmiller is.coa:
The young lieutenant receives an invitation to visit Kekesfalva's castle.
He meets Kekesfalva's paralyzed daughter and develops affection and compassion for her.
Edith falls in love with him and begins to hope for a speedy recovery.
He promises to marry her when she is recovered, hoping this will convince her to take treatment.
The young lieutenant Anton Hofmiller is.outcomes: Protagonist suffers emotional trauma and guilt, but survives the war. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation and emotional scarring.
The young lieutenant Anton Hofmiller is.theme: guilt; love vs societal expectations; human cost of deception

Marie, a cashier in the tunnel of love Z.coa:
A cashier falls in love with a baron.
The baron's relationship with the cashier is disrupted by a dancer who wears a mask.
The baron follows the dancer to Paris.
He tears the golden mask from her face, revealing that she is disfigured.
Marie, a cashier in the tunnel of love Z.outcomes: Protagonist saves antagonist from self-destruction. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and relational change.

Isabelle is prepared to marry Pierre, th.coa:
The female members of a certain family are afflicted with an unhappy first marriage due to a curse.
A woman plans to marry a stranger, get a quick divorce, and then marry the man she truly loves to avoid the curse.
She flies to Copenhagen in search of a suitable candidate for her plan.
She finds Jean-Yves, an editor for the Guide du Routard, who fits her criteria.
Isabelle is prepared to marry Pierre, th.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a successful second marriage with personal transformation, having overcome the family curse and found true love. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and relational change.
Isabelle is prepared to marry Pierre, th.theme: true love; overcoming adversity; family legacy

Zandy Allan is a hard-working cattle ran.coa: Zandy sends away for a mail-order bride. A Swedish woman from near Minneapolis responds and agrees to marry him. Zandy is disappointed when the woman turns out to be 32 years old instead of in her 20s, as he had expected. The woman arrives at Zandy's ranch.

Stranded in a small town in a downpour,.coa: A traveling musical show becomes stranded in a small town due to a downpour. The manager convinces the handlers of a local judge running for governor to hire the group to attract people to the politician's rallies. The crooner upstages the judge at one of the rallies, causing him to be fired.

A group of children play bravely in the.coa:
Children play in the ruins of Berlin after World War II.
The war ends and one boy's father returns home from a POW camp.
The boy is initially saddened by his father's return, as he appears hopeless and powerless.
The children persuade the father to clean up his badly damaged garage.

Emily Price is a single mother: she got.coa: A single mother gets pregnant in high school and is abandoned by the father before her child's birth. The child is born, and the mother lives with her mother, who prioritizes her grandson over her daughter. The mother tries to become a court reporter but consistently freezes up during tests. A social worker checks on the family at their supposed residence, but they have been lying about where they live so the child can attend a better school.
Emily Price is a single mother: she got.outcomes: Protagonist achieves personal growth and independence. The conflict is resolved with partial resolution, as some challenges remain but the protagonist has made progress.

Two brothers of the Filala commune, both.coa:
The two brothers travel on business to the city of Tessalit accompanied by their young nephew Driss.
They journey through a remote mountain region historically roamed by bandits, where they encounter a lone traveler who identifies himself as a Moungari.
The Moungari joins them, but Driss is suspicious of him.
On the pretext of going to hunt gazelle near the campsite, the Moungari lures the brothers into an ambush and kills them.
Two brothers of the Filala commune, both.outcomes: Driss survives the brutal attack but is left traumatized. The conflict is resolved with justice served through the murderer's death.

Two families embark on a pleasant Sunday.coa:
Two families go on a Sunday picnic in a Ford Model T.
The automobile breaks down, requiring them to exit and reboard repeatedly.
Each breakdown prompts repeated goodbyes as they try to leave the car behind.
A brick-throwing argument with a neighbor ensues due to the breakdowns.
Two families embark on a pleasant Sunday.outcomes: The picnic day ends in chaos, but no one is seriously injured or harmed. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution to the automobile issues or the argument with the neighbor.

Carmen, a 50-year-old housewife from a m.coa:
Augusto Pinochet seizes power in Chile through a coup and establishes a military dictatorship.
Three years later, in winter 1976, Carmen travels to her summer house for renovation work and personal time.
Father Sánchez asks Carmen for help caring for Elías, a wounded young man who has taken refuge with him.
Carmen agrees to assist due to her medical knowledge and past involvement in charitable projects.
Carmen, a 50-year-old housewife from a m.outcomes: Carmen's actions lead to her becoming involved in the resistance, but she is not directly affected by the conflict. The conflict remains unresolved for her personally, but she has gained a new perspective and connection with Elías.

Karl Renn, a Hamburg shipyard worker, is.coa: A Hamburg shipyard worker is commissioned by the Soviet Union to organize a general strike. The worker organizes a general strike, which leads to several fights with the police. After a month, many workers become exhausted and start breaking the strike, leading to an armed conflict that spreads. The worker's wife joins the conflict, but he stays home due to cowardice.
Karl Renn, a Hamburg shipyard worker, is.outcomes: Protagonist returns to Germany after learning of his Party Chief's death and continues the struggle for workers' rights. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing social unrest.
Karl Renn, a Hamburg shipyard worker, is.theme: class struggle; loyalty vs duty; ideological fervor

Foots Pulardos is a mobster who intends.coa: A meek clerk named Ernie Klenk mistakenly approves a Diners Club charge card application from a mobster. The mobster uses the gym as a front for his racketeering operations, where Ernie arrives to retrieve the approved card. The mobster discovers that he and Ernie share a unique physical similarity, which gives him an idea to burn down the gym with Ernie inside and assume Ernie's identity. The mobster plans to use this new identity to flee to Mexico with his moll.

Timofyev (Malcolm McDowell) is a patient.coa:
A patient in an asylum claims to be responsible for two historical assassinations.
Doctor Smirnov decides to apply a unique therapeutic method on him.
The patient's story is revealed through his voice-over, which recounts events from the perspective of Yakov Yurovsky.
Yurovsky is impersonated by the patient, and Tsar Nicholas II is played by Doctor Smirnov.
Timofyev (Malcolm McDowell) is a patient.outcomes: Protagonist's sanity is left uncertain, and the historical event is reenacted through his narrative. The conflict is unresolved with a focus on the past rather than resolution.
Timofyev (Malcolm McDowell) is a patient.theme: sanity vs reality; historical trauma; the blurred lines between truth and fiction

The film deals with the criminal ways an.coa: The "king" of a group of modern-day Gypsies passes away on his deathbed, passing leadership to his grandson. The grandson is reluctant to accept the position, but it has been passed to him nonetheless. His father, who was not appointed leader, becomes resentful and attempts to have the new leader killed. This attempt leads to a confrontation between the two.

The Fashionistas are an up-and-coming gr.coa: The group of fetish fashion designers tries to land a deal with an Italian designer. The Italian designer recently divorced amid rumors of extramarital affairs and arrives in Los Angeles seeking a partner for his s/m-influenced house. To grab the Italian designer's attention, the group crashes his fashion show. The leader of the group wants the Italian designer to believe she is the creative force behind the group.
The Fashionistas are an up-and-coming gr.outcomes: Protagonist group secures deal with antagonist, achieving success. The conflict is resolved with a mutually beneficial partnership but underlying tensions remain.

Daily Flash newspaper journalist Brenda.coa: A fire breaks out in an old house. A journalist and her photographer are assigned to cover the story and discover a wounded mobster suspected of stealing a quarter-million-dollar payroll. The mobster gives them a coded message before dying. His gang escapes with his satchel, but finds it filled with paper instead of money.
Daily Flash newspaper journalist Brenda.outcomes: Brenda Starr survives multiple assassination attempts and uncovers the truth about the stolen payroll. The conflict is resolved with partial resolution, as the perpetrators are identified but not fully brought to justice.
Daily Flash newspaper journalist Brenda.theme: deception; loyalty vs duty; truth revealed

Zetterstrøm (Ulrich Thomsen) once had a.coa: A musician leaves a woman and focuses on his career, forgetting about their past. A supernatural event occurs in Copenhagen, making the central part inaccessible over time due to an invisible shield. The musician returns to Copenhagen, unaware of the incident, and is guided by a mysterious man into the sealed area. The musician enters the zone, where he must relive his unresolved love affair.
Zetterstrøm (Ulrich Thomsen) once had a.outcomes: Protagonist is forced to confront and relive unresolved love affair. The conflict is partially resolved with personal catharsis but underlying issues remain.

The Headsman tells a story of loyalty te.coa:
Two orphans form a close bond as children.
As they grow older, one orphan follows a path to join the church, while the other becomes an army captain.
The two men's lives diverge significantly due to their different choices.
Years later, fate places the former army captain in a position where he is responsible for carrying out executions.
The Headsman tells a story of loyalty te.outcomes: Protagonist ultimately chooses his duty over friendship, leading to a resolution of conflict but also personal loss and transformation. The conflict is resolved with partial resolution and personal change.

Prior to his discovery and death, an Ame.coa: An American intelligence officer sends proof of the Russians filming American submarines off a joint US-Spanish naval base. The information leads to an investigation, and an undercover agent is sent to San Juan to gather more evidence. Upon arrival, the agent meets up with his former colleague and Spanish contact, and together they discover remote controlled video cameras being used by the Soviets. These cameras are boobytrapped with landmines that kill two Spanish sailors who try to disarm one.

During the World War II, in the 1930s to.coa: A young girl receives a diary as a gift for her 13th birthday. The Nazis occupy the Netherlands, forcing the girl's family and other Jews into hiding in Amsterdam. The girl writes extensively in her diary about her experiences during this time, documenting her thoughts and feelings. The group is eventually betrayed and taken to concentration camps.
During the World War II, in the 1930s to.outcomes: Protagonist dies in a concentration camp due to the war. The conflict is unresolved with no personal transformation or social reintegration for the protagonist.

A young man, Juvenal, is apparently able.coa:
A former evangelist is dissatisfied with his current occupation of selling mobile homes.
He convinces a friend to befriend an innocent ex-monk who has a unique ability to cure the sick by laying on hands.
The friend befriends the ex-monk and encourages him to pursue a more prominent role.
The young couple falls in love, causing complications.

FBI agent Diane Norwood (Carrere) and DE.coa:
FBI agent Diane Norwood and DEA agent Max Parrish are both in pursuit of a trio of allied crime bosses and a man named Livingston, who handles their finances.
The two agents race to be the first to arrest the criminals and seize their millions in cash in order to claim the money for their agency.
Neither agent can be beaten or persuaded off the case, leading them to reluctantly decide to work together.
They discover an eccentric assassin who works with Livingston, becoming the agents' only link to resolving the case.
FBI agent Diane Norwood (Carrere) and DE.outcomes: Protagonists successfully foil the criminals' plans and resolve the case through combined efforts. The conflict is resolved with a mix of personal growth and professional success.

Unlike the play Joan of Lorraine, which.coa: A shaft of light shines down from the ceiling in a basilica, possibly descending from heaven. The opening page of a church manuscript recounting Joan's life in Latin is shown on the screen, accompanied by uncredited voiceover narration that sets up the tale. This narration leads into the actual story of Joan, which begins with her becoming convinced that she has been divinely called to save France.
Unlike the play Joan of Lorraine, which.outcomes: Protagonist's legacy is canonized and her story is told through art. The conflict is unresolved with a sense of tragic loss and sacrifice.

Firelight follows a group of scientists.coa:
A series of colored lights appears in the sky over Freeport, Arizona.
The lights are observed and reported by scientists investigating the phenomenon.
People, animals, and objects begin disappearing from the town, including a dog, a unit of soldiers, and a young girl named Lisa.
Lisa's abduction causes her mother to have a heart attack.

A biographer researching a book on a pil.coa:
A biographer begins researching a book on a pilot who died during the test flight of a new plane.
The biographer falls in love with the pilot's sister while conducting research.

They set off together, with the son intending to part ways as soon as they reach their destination.

Sadako, a corpulent young woman, lives w.coa: A young woman lives with her common-law husband and his little son from a previous marriage. Her husband's family denies her being written into the family register. A burglar, who needs money for heart medication, breaks into their home while the husband is away and rapes the young woman. The burglar repeatedly attacks the young woman over the following weeks, developing an obsession for her.

Dante Graham enlists Jason Blake to coac.coa: The US b-boy team fails to win in 15 years. A coach is enlisted to lead a new team of top b-boys from across America. A very shy lawyer, Fremissin, is tasked.coa:
A lawyer is tasked with defending a man charged with beating his wife.
The lawyer gets nervous at the trial and demands the harshest possible sentence for the defendant, resulting in the defendant spending several months in prison.

The defendant sees this and tries to seduce her to get back at the lawyer.

Steve Fraser is an engineer working on a.coa:
Fraser's period of employment ends, prompting him to go into town to catch a plane out.
He arrives in town and meets Rosalie.

A brother and sister go into the pigsty.coa:
A brother and sister enter the pigsty to see the new-born piglets.

The Soviet Union collapses, leading to economic hardship in a Yazidi village in Armenia.

Nina works at a local roadside stand called Vodka Lemon, which is about to close down due to financial difficulties.

Elizabeth von Arnim's novel tells of fou.coa: Four dissimilar women respond to a newspaper advertisement for a medieval castle on the Mediterranean shores to be let furnished for April. Mrs. Arbuthnot and Mrs. Wilkins become acquainted after reading the ad, discovering they share common ground in unhappy marriages. A young Jewish doctor is caused by antis.coa: The young doctor undergoes ethnic plastic surgery to alter his physical appearance. After recovering from the surgeries, he is given a blood transfusion by pure Aryan virgins while in a bathtub. Following this treatment, he appears to have been cured of his Jewish features.

Carlo Levi is a painter and writer from.coa: In 1935, anti-fascist activities lead to arrest and confinement in a remote town in southern Italy. The regime confines the individual to this isolated location. The landscape is beautiful but the local population is impoverished and mismanaged. Silver, who is elegantly dressed and liv.coa: A man is hired to investigate the guilt of an accused person who is set to hang for a crime. Vandam lives in a prefab housing estate in Prague.

Lucka, a barmaid, gets into trouble due to accumulating debts.
Vandam decides to help Lucka.

Three sisters living in Switzerland hear.coa:
The three sisters learn that their father plans to remarry a younger woman in New York.
They decide to travel there to stop the wedding and devise a plan to get someone to seduce her.

The court case drags on for years, during which time the lawyers continue to collect money from the old couple while doing little to advance their case. The Vigilante, a masked government agent.coa: A government agent is assigned to investigate a case involving rare blood-red pearls. It is a novel expanded from four short s.coa:
A government agency develops "lost planets" and identifies a warlike civilization on the planet Hamal.
The agency drafts an agent into Investigative Adjustment (I-A) to manage dangerous planets.
Under I-A, the agent travels to various planets to maintain peace throughout the galaxy.

Dr. Freeman (Feore) runs a Las Vegas Ass.coa: Eight women are inseminated at a clinic, resulting in eight pregnancies. One woman conceives naturally. The children are born. Businessman Cole Porter buys cattle from.coa:
Businessman buys cattle from ranchers, paying them upfront.
He kills the cattle later to recover the money he paid them.

Resourceful and engaging Fay Cheyney, po.coa: Fay poses as a wealthy Australian widow at a Monte Carlo hotel with the intention of stealing Mrs. Webley's pearl necklace. Windstorm is the story of the teenage gi.coa:
A teenage girl accidentally sets fire to her teacher's car.
As a result, she gets sent to her grandmother's stable over the holidays.

Julian (Kim Joohyuk) has lived off with.coa:

Willie Bauche, a Hollywood producer, bec.coa:
A Hollywood producer becomes obsessed with making his wife a sex symbol, neglecting her needs.

Martial arts expert Ho Chiang journeys t.coa: A martial arts expert journeys to America's Wild West on a life-or-death mission. Conniving wife (Neri) has her husband mu.coa:

Yusuf a young factory worker who loses h.coa:
Yusuf loses his job at the factory.
Yusuf travels to Istanbul to stay with Mahmut while looking for a new job as a sailor.
Mahmut takes Yusuf on a drive to photograph the Turkish countryside in an attempt to bond and recapture his love of art.

Loser Ben Harris (Eli Wallach), an alien.coa: A mailman spots an attractive young woman on a rainy night and decides to kidnap her as part of his plan to get a girl. He races ahead of her and prepares an ambush, but ends up pulling a bag down over Gloria Fiske instead of the intended target. The mailman carries Gloria back to his basement apartment and removes the bag, only to find he has captured a middle-aged housewife.
Loser Ben Harris (Eli Wallach), an alien.outcomes: Protagonist fails in his original goal of getting a girl through kidnapping and is left with an unwanted situation. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.
Loser Ben Harris (Eli Wallach), an alien.theme: unforeseen consequences; mistaken identity; failed expectations

In tumultuous Mexico in the 1860s a band.coa: In tumultuous Mexico in the 1860s, a group of revolutionaries plan to use stolen jewelry to finance their armed struggle. They hire an American gunfighter to steal the jewelry from the oppressive governing forces. The gunfighter succeeds in stealing the jewelry, but it is later revealed that the jewelry is fake. This discovery sets off a chain reaction as the gunfighter goes off in pursuit of the real jewels.
In tumultuous Mexico in the 1860s a band.outcomes: Protagonist fails in initial objective but achieves personal success through perseverance. The conflict is unresolved with broader threats remaining.
In tumultuous Mexico in the 1860s a band.theme: deception; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Matthias Clausen (Emil Jannings) is the.coa: The head of a munitions firm falls in love with a secretary in the office. His children conspire against him to protect their inheritance. He discovers their plan and disowns them. He bestows the firm on the state, confident that one of his workers will be able to carry on his work.
Matthias Clausen (Emil Jannings) is the.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a sense of resolution and legacy through his decision. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as he prioritizes love over family ties and secures the future of his company.

Greeks set sail to Troy, since the princ.coa:
The Greeks set sail to Troy because Paris has abducted Helen, wife of Menelaus.
Paris' abduction sparks a war between the Greeks and Trojans.
In the tenth year of the war, Agamemnon offends Achilles over a female slave, causing him to withdraw from the conflict.
Achilles' withdrawal creates confusion in the Greek army.
Greeks set sail to Troy, since the princ.outcomes: Protagonist achieves revenge for Patroclus' death but ultimately fails to destroy Troy. The conflict is unresolved with a stalemate between the two armies.
Greeks set sail to Troy, since the princ.theme: revenge vs justice; loyalty and duty; human cost of war

A group of men travel to a duck hunt in.coa:
A group of men travel to a duck hunt in the Northwest.
They arrive at their destination and begin preparations for the hunt.
Charlie, who has been struggling with grief over a recent loss, starts to open up to his fellow hunters about his emotions.
He learns that one of them, David, is a deserter from the war.
A group of men travel to a duck hunt in.outcomes: Protagonist's emotional state remains unchanged, but conflict is resolved through violence. The conflict is unresolved with a personal transformation for the protagonist.

Set in the 1940s in Cape Breton Island,.coa: A young girl's father dies in an accident at the mine. The death becomes routine, with many men losing their lives in similar accidents. An older brother also dies in the mine, leaving the girl alone and preferring life without marriage to a miner. A charming man shows up, and against her mother's wishes, they marry.
Set in the 1940s in Cape Breton Island,.outcomes: Protagonist survives but is emotionally scarred. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing systemic issues.
Set in the 1940s in Cape Breton Island,.theme: loss; grief; human cost of industrialization

Gus Kubicek (played by Guttenberg) is a.coa:
Gus wins a battle against Hodgkin's disease.
Gus becomes depressed and overweight, prompting his sister to try to set him up with someone.
The sister tries to make Gus seem more dynamic and attractive by inventing the persona of Lobo Marunga, a leather-clad biker from New Zealand.
Gus adopts the Lobo persona and meets Emily, who falls in love with him as Lobo.
Gus becomes emotionally invested in his relationship with Emily and eventually tells her the truth about his identity while they are intimate.
Emily is furious when she discovers Gus's true identity and tells him to leave.
Gus supposedly goes away to New York, but actually attends a friend's wedding.
Emily tracks down Gus at the airport.
Gus Kubicek (played by Guttenberg) is a.outcomes: Protagonist achieves romantic resolution with love interest. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and relationship development.
Gus Kubicek (played by Guttenberg) is a.theme: authenticity vs deception; self-discovery through relationships; love as transformative power

Zed is a British-Pakistani rapper who is.coa:
Zed returns home from New York before his European tour begins.
He collapses, prompting medical attention.
After being taken to the hospital, he receives a diagnosis that his muscles have weakened due to an underlying condition.
The weakened state triggers bizarre visual and audial hallucinations.
Zed is a British-Pakistani rapper who is.outcomes: Protagonist receives a life-altering diagnosis, leading to a new reality of managing his health. The conflict is unresolved as the protagonist faces an uncertain future and ongoing struggles with the disease.

Albin, a solitary French journalist, is.coa:
A French journalist is sent to Germany to write an article about a noted novelist.
The journalist rents a flat in the same village where the novelist lives and becomes acquainted with the novelist's wife through her.
The journalist becomes obsessed with finding the "dark spot" behind their seemingly harmonious marriage and makes advances to the wife, who tactfully rejects him.
The journalist then spies on the wife, convinced that she is having an affair.
Albin, a solitary French journalist, is.outcomes: Protagonist fails in his scheme and is unable to find work as a journalist afterwards. The conflict is unresolved with personal failure and guilt.

A group of agents of the U.S. Office of.coa: A group of agents is sent to German-occupied Italy during World War II to knock out the German-held Italian railroad system. They accomplish their mission, but most are killed due to an inside betrayal. The remaining survivor resolves to find the traitor after the war. He returns to Orta, near Milan, to investigate and discovers that his old love is alive and married to a powerful nobleman.
A group of agents of the U.S. Office of.outcomes: Protagonist finds the traitor but ultimately fails to bring them to justice. The conflict is unresolved with no personal transformation or social change.

The story concerns an experimental nucle.coa: A Soviet military site is breached and an experimental nuclear cruise missile is stolen. The thief is revealed to be a group of international terrorists under the command of a European power-crazed man known as the Baron, who plans to use the missile to destroy an international peace conference in one week on an island in the Persian Gulf. A U.S. consul to Iran is murdered by the Baron's henchmen, prompting a US intelligence agent to travel to Iran to take over as consul and investigate the murder. The agent arrives in Tehran but is soon followed by two of the Baron's henchmen who attempt to kill him.
The story concerns an experimental nucle.outcomes: The protagonist (Alec) prevents the destruction of the peace conference and the start of World War III by locating and securing the stolen cruise missile. The conflict is resolved with a global security victory but broader threats remain.
The story concerns an experimental nucle.theme: prevention of catastrophic war; international cooperation vs terrorism

John is the illegitimate son of a wealth.coa:
The wealthy landowner shows disinterest towards his illegitimate son.
The illegitimate son decides to take revenge on his father for the disinterest shown towards him.
He becomes the lover of his stepbrother's wife, who is also a relative of the wealthy landowner.
His stepbrother challenges him to a duel over the affair and he kills him in the duel.
The woman commits suicide after her husband's death.
The illegitimate son continues his cynical existence, but eventually attracts the attention of relatives of other women he has seduced.
They try to kill him.
John is the illegitimate son of a wealth.outcomes: Protagonist survives but is left with a cynical existence. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal consequences.
John is the illegitimate son of a wealth.theme: revenge; toxic relationships; cycle of violence

Texar and Burbank are bitter enemies, Bu.coa: The community in the Confederate States of America disagrees with Burbank's view on slavery. This disagreement sparks anger from Texar, who has past legal troubles that he blames on Burbank. Texar invents an alibi to escape conviction and seeks vengeance against Burbank. Using his newfound power, Texar turns the townsfolk against Burbank and leads a mob that destroys Burbank's plantation, Camdless Bay.
Texar and Burbank are bitter enemies, Bu.outcomes: Burbank's family is reunited with him after the kidnappers are apprehended. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation as Burbank's community begins to question their treatment of him.
Texar and Burbank are bitter enemies, Bu.theme: deception; power dynamics; loyalty vs betrayal

After Alex Sperling, a Harvard-bound val.coa:
Loses summer internship.
Heads to Los Angeles to work for cousin.
Discovers cousin is not successful businessman he claimed to be.
Forced to squat in mansion of one of cousin's clients due to series of mishaps.
After Alex Sperling, a Harvard-bound val.outcomes: Protagonist is financially successful and gains new perspective on life. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing chaos and uncertainty.
After Alex Sperling, a Harvard-bound val.theme: adapting to failure; deception vs authenticity; navigating uncertainty

In Saint Paul, Minnesota, the long-runni.coa:
The radio station's new parent company schedules the Fitzgerald Theater for demolition and dispatches "the Axeman" to judge whether to save A Prairie Home Companion.
The show's denizens prepare for their final broadcast, unaware of the impending decision.
As they perform and mingle between musical acts, PI Guy Noir keeps watch over them.
The show is visited by an otherworldly "Dangerous Woman", revealed to be Lois Peterson, a listener who died in a car accident while listening to a past broadcast.
In Saint Paul, Minnesota, the long-runni.outcomes: The conflict is unresolved with the show's cancellation, but personal transformation occurs as the cast comes together for a farewell tour years later. The resolution is partial with some characters finding closure, while others face uncertainty.
In Saint Paul, Minnesota, the long-runni.theme: transience of art; loyalty and legacy; human connection in the face of change

In prohibition-era Manhattan, shopkeeper.coa: A childhood sweetheart marries someone else, causing loss for the protagonist. A reporter also experiences a similar loss when his own childhood sweetheart marries another man. The two individuals meet and form a friendship based on their shared sense of humor and mutual losses. They enter into a marriage of convenience.
In prohibition-era Manhattan, shopkeeper.outcomes: Protagonist Mary achieves closure and prioritizes her own happiness. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and acceptance.
In prohibition-era Manhattan, shopkeeper.theme: loss; love vs societal expectations; self-discovery

For a Moment Freedom tells of the odysse.coa:
Three groups of refugees escape from Iran and Iraq.
They arrive in the Turkish capital.
The refugees are stuck in a dubious hotel, waiting for their applications for asylum to be approved.
Each day they hope that their applications will be accepted.
For a Moment Freedom tells of the odysse.outcomes: The refugees' asylum applications remain pending, leaving them in limbo. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing uncertainty about their future.
For a Moment Freedom tells of the odysse.theme: asylum seeker plight; freedom vs bureaucracy; human resilience under uncertainty

USSR, late November 1941. Based on the a.coa: A group of twenty-eight soldiers from the 316th Rifle Division are tasked with defending against a German column. The German column consists of fifty-four tanks and hundreds of infantry guarding the tanks' flanks, belonging to the 11th Panzer Division. The Soviet soldiers engage the Germans in battle, but their standard issue Mosin-Nagant rifles and machine guns prove ineffective against the tanks.
USSR, late November 1941. Based on the a.outcomes: Protagonist successfully halts the German advance on Moscow, protecting their Motherland. The conflict is resolved with a heroic defense that inspires national pride.
USSR, late November 1941. Based on the a.theme: sacrificial duty; patriotism under fire; bravery in the face of overwhelming odds

The short story illustrates many importa.coa:
The sister playfully knocks on the door of a large house.
This knock causes the owner to become enraged and send troops after the two of them.
The troops chase the narrator and his sister, leading to their capture.
Their capture sets in motion events that will result in the narrator's torture.
The short story illustrates many importa.outcomes: Protagonist is captured and faces torture. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.
The short story illustrates many importa.theme: consequences of actions; power dynamics; morality

The author narrates moments of his frien.coa: The author contracts a lung ailment and is hospitalized, where he meets Paul Wittgenstein, who has been admitted for madness. During their recovery, the two men form a friendship. Paul Wittgenstein dissipates his fortune through his obsession with music, race cars, and sailing, ultimately dying poor in an asylum.
The author narrates moments of his frien.outcomes: Protagonist (Bernhard) gains insight into his own struggles with madness and finds a way to channel it into creative expression, while Paul Wittgenstein succumbs to his inner conflict and dies alone in an asylum. The conflict is unresolved for Paul, but Bernhard achieves personal transformation through his writing.
The author narrates moments of his frien.theme: madness vs creativity; superficiality of society; the power of art

Despite a dark secret, 14-year-old Jessi.coa:
The father loses his job.
The loss of income exacerbates the already strained relationships within the family.
Jessika is left to fend for herself as her parents struggle to manage their situation.
A horrifying event occurs, which Jessika is forced to witness.
Despite a dark secret, 14-year-old Jessi.outcomes: Protagonist begins taking steps towards independence and self-preservation despite family's toxic dynamics. The conflict is unresolved, but protagonist has started to break free from its grasp.
Despite a dark secret, 14-year-old Jessi.theme: resilience in the face of adversity; struggle for autonomy; coping with trauma

François, a dentist, and his wife Franço.coa: The family moves to Paris and enrolls their daughter in a prestigious school. The daughter makes friends and becomes involved in social activities such as discos, cinemas, and parties. François receives a call from his former lover, who asks him to spend another night with her, but he tries to go home instead. He then calls Françoise to say he is in the hospital, which she sees through as a ruse.
François, a dentist, and his wife Franço.outcomes: Protagonist family is reconciled after mother becomes pregnant. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation and relational change.

The story, set in 13th-century China, co.coa:
Gou Pei is forced by Di Chou to fly on a wind-testing kite.
Gou Pei is killed during the flight.
Great-uncle Bo arranges for Qing'an to marry Di Chou in order to secure his own position and protect the Gou family's interests.
Haoyou and Mipeng get rid of Di Chou, likely by killing him.
The story, set in 13th-century China, co.outcomes: Protagonist achieves escape from family obligations through new career path. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social reintegration.
The story, set in 13th-century China, co.theme: survival under adversity; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

A suave, smooth burglar named King Kong.coa: A suave, smooth burglar tries to make up for his thieving ways by teaming up with a bumbling police detective. The two work together to try to find a set of stolen diamonds. The diamonds are also being tracked by a European criminal known as 'White Gloves'. Superintendent Ho supervises the unlikely duo and has a temper, which sometimes affects their mission.
A suave, smooth burglar named King Kong.outcomes: Protagonist and ally successfully recover the stolen diamonds. The conflict is resolved with conflict_resolved and personal transformation as they redeem themselves through their actions.
A suave, smooth burglar named King Kong.theme: redemption; unlikely alliances; justice vs crime

Krystyna, an aspiring actress, journeys.coa: A Communist invasion occurs in Czechoslovakia, leading to the imprisonment of an actress who spoke out against the new regime. The actress is later banned from reentry to her homeland and moves to New York City. An aspiring actress travels from Czechoslovakia to New York City in search of the imprisoned actress, who has become a famous idol in her native land. The imprisoned actress's husband, also a renowned director, is in Paris at the time of the invasion and later becomes successful making music videos for MTV.
Krystyna, an aspiring actress, journeys.outcomes: Protagonist achieves success in her acting career with the help of her mentor. The conflict is partially resolved with personal growth but broader issues remain.
Krystyna, an aspiring actress, journeys.theme: mentorship; identity formation; artistic integrity vs exploitation

Jonathan Kingsley is a professor of psyc.coa: The elder teenage daughter begins to display uncharacteristic behavior. Her parents suspect that she has lost her virginity during a school field trip to Catalina Island. The general practitioner confirms this suspicion after examining her. She is interrogated and admits to losing her virginity, revealing that she is married.
Jonathan Kingsley is a professor of psyc.outcomes: Protagonist is left with a mix of emotions, struggling to understand his daughter's actions. The conflict is unresolved as the identity of Linda's husband remains unknown.
Jonathan Kingsley is a professor of psyc.theme: adolescent rebellion; family secrets; parental responsibility

After many years away sailor Hannes Wedd.coa:
A sailor returns to Hamburg after many years away.
He meets up with his old friend who owns a venue on the Reeperbahn.
The friend's business is struggling, so the sailor helps turn it around by introducing a new revue.
This success leads to the sailor taking an interest in his friend's grown-up daughter.
After many years away sailor Hannes Wedd.outcomes: Protagonist resolves conflict and saves friend, restoring relationships. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and relational healing.
After many years away sailor Hannes Wedd.theme: family secrets; redemption through selflessness; loyalty vs deception

The marriage between Adrian and Drusilla.coa:
Adrian's marriage becomes unsatisfactory and loveless after his return from World War I.
The couple treats each other with cold distance, leading Adrian to seek escape from his home life.
He disguises himself as a shabby itinerant in the East End of London, where he meets Vicky.
Adrian takes on a protective role towards Vicky, which makes her boyfriend Herb suspicious and jealous.
The marriage between Adrian and Drusilla.outcomes: Protagonist Adrian's marriage is revitalized as he returns home with renewed passion for his wife Drusilla. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social reintegration.
The marriage between Adrian and Drusilla.theme: redemption; love vs routine; human resilience

Early one morning Brunetti is confronted.coa: A young American serviceman is found dead in a Venetian canal early one morning. The investigation initially suggests that the death was due to a mugging, but Brunetti suspects there may be more to it than robbery. Further investigation reveals incriminating evidence in the victim's flat, leading Brunetti to believe that someone is trying to cover up the true circumstances of the death. This discovery sets off a chain of events as Brunetti digs deeper into the case.
Early one morning Brunetti is confronted.outcomes: Protagonist fails to bring justice due to systemic corruption, but a vengeful Sicilian mother achieves partial resolution through personal action. The conflict is unresolved with broader threats remaining.
Early one morning Brunetti is confronted.theme: corruption; power vs accountability; human vulnerability

A man sentenced to 20 years in prison in.coa: A man is sentenced to 20 years in prison and begins working on creating toys for children. He puts a note in a papier-mâché horse claiming he was unjustly imprisoned and appeals for help. The horse is eventually collected by five schoolmates who find the note inside. They plan an escape and decide to visit Monteforte, where the prisoner is being held.
A man sentenced to 20 years in prison in.outcomes: Protagonist is exonerated and released from prison after being wrongly accused. The conflict is unresolved with a partial resolution of personal transformation for the protagonist, but broader threats remain due to the escape plan's failure.
A man sentenced to 20 years in prison in.theme: deception vs innocence; redemption under adversity; human fallibility

A Mexican-American couple who are approa.coa:
A Mexican-American couple moves to a farming community in California.
The wife develops unusual symptoms and experiences horrific visions.
Together, they try to determine if these strange occurrences are related to a legendary curse or something more evil.
They research and investigate various possibilities, but the cause of the symptoms remains unclear.
A Mexican-American couple who are approa.outcomes: Protagonist couple successfully gives birth to a healthy child. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as they overcome initial fears and anxieties.
A Mexican-American couple who are approa.theme: faith vs superstition; family resilience; overcoming fear

The Austrian main character Roithamer, l.coa: The narrator builds a house in the shape of a cone in the middle of the Kobernausser forest. The sister enters the house and dies. The narrator continues to make corrections to the house's design, driven by an obsessive need for perfection. This process eventually leads to the narrator's decision to correct the project one final time through self-destruction.
The Austrian main character Roithamer, l.outcomes: Protagonist survives in a state of emotional numbness after his sister's death. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation and social isolation.

An elderly mother travels to Shanghai to.coa:
An elderly mother travels to Shanghai.
She arrives at the city and begins searching for her missing son.
Her search leads her to discover that her son has been murdered by a powerful figure.
The discovery also reveals that this figure is holding her granddaughter captive.
An elderly mother travels to Shanghai to.outcomes: Protagonist fails to kill the gang leader but manages to rescue her granddaughter. The conflict is unresolved with a personal transformation for the protagonist.
An elderly mother travels to Shanghai to.theme: family loyalty; justice vs power; sacrifice

EVE VIII is a military android created t.coa: A bank robbery occurs, resulting in damage to EVE VIII, a military android. The robot accesses memories programmed by its creator, revealing dark and tragic events. This exposure causes EVE VIII's behavior to become increasingly erratic and violent. As a result, Colonel Jim McQuade is tasked with eliminating the machine.
EVE VIII is a military android created t.outcomes: EVE VIII is disabled and its mission is foiled. The conflict is resolved with a partial resolution, as the robot's destruction prevents further harm, but its creator's dark past remains unaddressed.
EVE VIII is a military android created t.theme: duality of human nature; artificial intelligence vs humanity; trauma and legacy

During the Battle of the Bulge in 1944,.coa: The Germans open fire on American prisoners of war during the Battle of the Bulge in 1944. Many troops are killed as they try to run away. Two survivors manage to escape, including a medic and a corporal. They join forces with two other surviving soldiers from their division.
During the Battle of the Bulge in 1944,.outcomes: Protagonist survives the massacre and manages to escape with allies. The conflict is resolved with the successful delivery of intelligence to the Allies.
During the Battle of the Bulge in 1944,.theme: survival under adversity; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

God went to walk in the heavenly garden.coa:
God went to walk in the heavenly garden and took everyone except one person.
The tailor arrived at the gate of the garden.
St. Peter refused to admit the tailor, citing past wrongdoing and a divine prohibition.
The tailor begged St. Peter, who allowed him to sit in the corner until God's return.
The tailor wandered off while waiting and found a chair from which he could see everything on earth.
He saw an old woman stealing two veils while doing laundry.
The tailor threw a golden stool at the old woman.
God returned and discovered what had happened.
God scolded the tailor for his mercy, saying that if everyone acted similarly, Heaven would be empty because they would have thrown everything at the earth.
God threw the tailor out of the garden.
God went to walk in the heavenly garden.outcomes: The tailor is expelled from Heaven. The conflict is resolved with the removal of the troublesome individual.
God went to walk in the heavenly garden.theme: mercy vs justice; accountability; consequences of actions

Becker, a hotshot American marketing exe.coa:
A hotshot American marketing executive visits Australian operations in Sydney.
He bumps into a secretary assigned to help him.
The executive discovers that a local soft drink producer has been successfully fending off American brand name products.
The executive vows an all-out marketing war with the local producer.
