Becker, a hotshot American marketing exe.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a shift in perspective and begins to question his role in the corporation. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation, as Becker starts to see the world from a different perspective.
Becker, a hotshot American marketing exe.theme: corporate responsibility; cultural imperialism; identity crisis

Karoline Steinmeier and her husband Jose.coa:
The winemaker sends a letter to the inn requesting to visit and celebrate the grandfather's 100th birthday.
The couple realizes they will be exposed as having collected the pension for years without the grandfather being alive, so they must find a replacement.
They decide to hire an elderly man from a nearby town to pose as the grandfather.
The winemaker arrives at the inn with his family to celebrate the supposed milestone.
Karoline Steinmeier and her husband Jose.outcomes: Protagonist avoids exposure of fraud through temporary solution. The conflict is resolved with a partial resolution and avoidance of immediate consequences.

Arivazhagan has a sister Anbukarasi. Anb.coa:
Anbukarasi falls in love with Vetrivelan, who is commander-in-chief of the royal army.
Vetrivelan tells Anbukarasi and Arivazhagan that he is just an ordinary citizen of the kingdom and marries Anbukarasi.
Arivazhagan leaves on a mission after his sister's marriage.
After some time, Vetrivelan deserts his wife and child and returns to the palace.
Arivazhagan has a sister Anbukarasi. Anb.outcomes: The protagonist's sister is left alone but fights for her husband, while the antagonist is thwarted by the hero. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation and social reintegration.
Arivazhagan has a sister Anbukarasi. Anb.theme: deception; loyalty vs duty; family ties

The play takes place on a single day in.coa:
The four main characters gather at Monte Cristo Cottage on a single day in August 1912.
Tensions arise as they begin to grapple with their individual failings and shortcomings.
Bitterness and jealousy emerge as each member blames and resents the others for various reasons.
Their shared self-analysis and articulate honesty fuel the enduring emotional and psychic stress within the family.
The play takes place on a single day in.outcomes: The family members are unable to resolve their conflicts and achieve reconciliation. The conflict is unresolved with personal and relational transformation but deeper issues remain.
The play takes place on a single day in.theme: family dysfunction; unfulfilled potential; struggle for redemption

Harry Bailey, a former student activist,.coa: A former student activist returns to college as a graduate student. He tries to avoid increasing student unrest on campus and nationwide. Student demonstrations escalate, bringing police to the campus to quell the unrest, leading to clashes between students and police. The heavy police presence forces him to confront his changing values.
Harry Bailey, a former student activist,.outcomes: Protagonist shifts towards aligning with student protests and questioning the educational establishment. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader social tensions remain.
Harry Bailey, a former student activist,.theme: social change; identity crisis; challenging authority

The film follows Quentin Crisp's move in.coa: The film's protagonist moves from London to New York City in the late 1970s. He becomes a local celebrity and begins writing for New York magazines. The AIDS crisis erupts, and he makes flippant comments about it, refusing to recant his statements. His public remarks spark controversy and criticism during this time.
The film follows Quentin Crisp's move in.outcomes: Protagonist Quentin Crisp achieves national celebrity status and finds a sense of belonging in New York City. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and continued artistic expression.

The narrator meets an old Roma traveller.coa:
The narrator meets an old Roma traveller outside his camp and they have a conversation about freedom.
The guest shows interest in the traveller's daughter's singing, prompting him to warn against being charmed by women.
The traveller tells a story about Loiko Zobar and Radda, highlighting her independence and power over men.
Radda orders Loiko to kneel before her in front of other men, crushing his spirits.
The narrator meets an old Roma traveller.outcomes: Protagonist witnesses Loiko's sacrifice and has a vision of bleeding Radda walking through the skies. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.

Timid milkman Burleigh Sullivan works fo.coa: A group of drunk men try to chat up Mae, Burleigh's sister. Burleigh chances by and intervenes in an ensuing brawl. Speed McFarland, the world middleweight champion, gets knocked out during the fight. The incident is witnessed by Gabby Sloan, McFarland's boss.
Timid milkman Burleigh Sullivan works fo.outcomes: Protagonist Burleigh Sullivan achieves success as the new world middleweight champion. The conflict is resolved with a personal triumph but broader corruption remains.
Timid milkman Burleigh Sullivan works fo.theme: underdog victory; redemption through achievement; integrity in adversity

Jane Callahan (Julia Ormond), a beautifu.coa: A beautiful American lady writes to her son about a long kept secret. Twenty years ago, she arrived in Russia to assist an obsessive engineer who needs the patronage of a powerful nobleman to sponsor his invention. On her travels, she meets two men who change her life forever: a handsome young cadet and a powerful General who is entranced by her beauty and wants to marry her. The two men become rivals for her love.
Jane Callahan (Julia Ormond), a beautifu.outcomes: Protagonist's secret is revealed through her son's letter. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation for the protagonist but broader consequences for those involved.
Jane Callahan (Julia Ormond), a beautifu.theme: deception; love vs loyalty; secrets and consequences

A team of foreign agents arrive in Brazi.coa: A team of foreign agents arrives in Brazil. The agents install devices on the country's main radio and TV transmitters to emit subliminal messages. The messages are broadcast, affecting most of the population who listen or watch television, causing them to become a pliable and aggressive mob supporting a revolution. The populace begins to riot and cause chaos throughout the country.
A team of foreign agents arrive in Brazi.outcomes: Protagonists (Carlos and Laura) successfully expose and thwart the international plot, restoring order in Brazil. The conflict is resolved with social reintegration and a return to stability.
A team of foreign agents arrive in Brazi.theme: manipulation of public opinion; control vs freedom; media influence

Leaving the island of San Quinto , marke.coa:
The military government forces the airliner down in an unscheduled stop.
The hijackers attempt to take control of the plane and head towards Caracas.
Captain Roberti is shot during the hijacking attempt, but van Houten disarms the attackers.
The aircraft's landing gear is damaged during the struggle with the hijackers.
Leaving the island of San Quinto , marke.outcomes: Protagonist survives and achieves safe landing despite damage to aircraft. The conflict is resolved with personal survival and heroic action.
Leaving the island of San Quinto , marke.theme: survival under adversity; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

After a brief courtship, a man marries a.coa: A man marries an enchanting woman after a brief courtship. The couple goes on their honeymoon to the foggy Oregon coast. During their stay, he discovers her shocking secrets. Those around them begin to die in horrible and violent deaths, one by one. He learns that she is responsible for the murders.
After a brief courtship, a man marries a.outcomes: Protagonist survives the ordeal but is forever changed by the experience. The conflict is unresolved with a sense of ongoing danger and uncertainty.
After a brief courtship, a man marries a.theme: deception; trust vs knowledge; appearances vs reality

The old grandmother Tina arrives in town.coa: The old grandmother arrives in town for a wedding. She discovers that her medallion has been stolen upon arrival. She goes to the police station to file a complaint about the theft. The investigation is too slow, so she decides to search for the thief herself.
The old grandmother Tina arrives in town.outcomes: Protagonist successfully retrieves stolen item and sees family event proceed as planned. The conflict is resolved with personal satisfaction and community recognition.

Reichau (Bruno Cremer) is a one time Fre.coa: A one-time French Army captain is released from prison after serving three years for belonging to the OAS. He arrives in France, unsure what to do with his life. He decides to take part in a heist organized by Pierre, who sent him to prison. The heist involves stealing a bag containing 500 million francs during a plane flight.
Reichau (Bruno Cremer) is a one time Fre.outcomes: Protagonist participates in the heist but its outcome is not specified. The conflict is unresolved with unknown consequences for the protagonist's life.

Chip Gutchell, an IT specialist and reco.coa: A routine rectal exam is performed, during which small objects inserted into the anus mysteriously disappear. This event sparks a compulsive behavior in the individual, who begins inserting various items into their anus to test the phenomenon. As time passes, the disappearance of these objects escalates to include pets and eventually people. The individual's obsession grows more intense, causing them to become increasingly reckless with their actions.
Chip Gutchell, an IT specialist and reco.outcomes: Protagonist's compulsion is revealed, but no resolution on its consequences. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal struggle.
Chip Gutchell, an IT specialist and reco.theme: obsession; loss of control; the unknown

Beni, age 15, becomes enamoured with the.coa: A 15-year-old boy becomes enamored with the lead singer of a struggling rock band. The lead singer takes him on as the band's 'roadie' and begins a romantic relationship with him, exploiting his youthful innocence. As time passes, the lead singer introduces the boy to substance abuse, which he quickly adopts. The boy's involvement in the band's lifestyle leads to him engaging in prostitution to support the lead singer's addiction.
Beni, age 15, becomes enamoured with the.outcomes: Protagonist is left with emotional trauma and a damaged sense of self-worth. The conflict is unresolved with long-term consequences for protagonist's well-being.

Ken Tani (Shō Kosugi), a martial artist.coa: An F-111 carrying an experimental black ops laser tracking device is shot down over Malta by Russian forces. The incident prompts a summons for a special operative codenamed "Black Eagle" to intervene. The operative and their team travel to Malta, where they discover that the Russians have dispatched a group of elite agents to retrieve the device. A face-off between the two groups becomes inevitable as they converge on the location of the downed plane.
Ken Tani (Shō Kosugi), a martial artist.outcomes: Protagonist successfully retrieves the device and prevents it from falling into enemy hands. The conflict is resolved with conflict_resolved and personal transformation as Black Eagle proves himself effective in his mission.
Ken Tani (Shō Kosugi), a martial artist.theme: loyalty vs duty; survival under adversity; human resilience

Nami, a Bōsōzoku leader, kills a high-ra.coa: A high-ranking member of a yakuza organization is killed by a Bōsōzoku leader due to a turf war. The Bōsōzoku leader is sent to prison as a result. After serving three years in prison, the Bōsōzoku leader finds a home living with her uncle at a pool hall. She meets a pimp named Ryuji and acquires a job as a hostess in Ginza due to his influence.
Nami, a Bōsōzoku leader, kills a high-ra.outcomes: Protagonist is still involved in criminal underworld, struggling to leave her past behind. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing threats from Owada and the yakuza.
Nami, a Bōsōzoku leader, kills a high-ra.theme: redemption; loyalty vs duty; struggle for identity

An impoverished aristocratic family livi.coa: An impoverished aristocratic family decides to draw in unsuspecting travellers as paying guests. They collude with Charlie, the village garage man, who is in love with Amélie, the granddaughter of the châtelaine. A seductive gangster and his two accomplices arrive at the mansion, in possession of 100 million francs. The noble family intend to ensure that they get their hands on this loot.
An impoverished aristocratic family livi.outcomes: The conflict is resolved with personal transformation for the gangster and social reintegration through the hotel's success. The protagonist family achieves a stable path of self-improvement through their new business venture.
An impoverished aristocratic family livi.theme: redemption; hospitality vs greed; human potential

In 2007, during the Iraq War, a Georgian.coa: A Georgian contingent of coalition forces saves the life of an American reporter, but one of his colleagues is killed in the process. One year later, the American returns to Los Angeles, but then travels to Georgia on advice from friends in Tbilisi, who suspect a large conflict is brewing. The American and his cameraman delve deeper into Georgian life as conflict escalates, and they get caught in the crossfire during an air raid that strikes a local wedding.
In 2007, during the Iraq War, a Georgian.outcomes: Protagonist and cameraman manage to escape with their footage, but broader conflict remains unresolved. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation as protagonist bears witness to human suffering.
In 2007, during the Iraq War, a Georgian.theme: humanitarian crisis; media representation; international indifference

Adrien works in an advertising agency in.coa:
Adrien works in an advertising agency during the day and at night becomes the queen of a gay bar run by his best friend Eva.
Eva's bar is a major part of Adrien's life, and he has a close relationship with her.
Alexandre Agut, a major client of Adrien's agency, wants to get to know him better and asks Eva to pose as his wife in order to gain access to Adrien's personal life.
Eva agrees to help Alexandre, but things quickly spiral out of control when Alexandre falls deeply in love with her.
Adrien works in an advertising agency in.outcomes: Protagonist's personal life is complicated due to client's infatuation with bar owner. The conflict is unresolved with relational transformation and blurred boundaries.
Adrien works in an advertising agency in.theme: identity; deception; blurred lines between work and personal life

An Icelandic man in his 40s lives with h.coa:
An Icelandic man in his 40s lives with his mother and works as ground staff at a nearby airport.
He is involved in miniature wargaming in his spare time.
On his 42nd birthday, he starts taking a dance class.
He falls in love with someone from the dance class.
His girlfriend leaves him due to her depression.
He helps his ex-girlfriend find happiness by turning a dump into a flower boutique shop.
Fúsi is at risk of falling back into his old routines.
He decides to make changes for himself and takes a trip to Egypt.
An Icelandic man in his 40s lives with h.outcomes: Protagonist achieves personal growth and transformation through travel and self-improvement. The conflict is resolved with partial resolution of emotional struggles but broader life adjustments remain.

Despite peaceful speeches, the army of t.coa:
The Soviet Russian army attacks Estonia, prompting the country's government to declare a mobilization for all citizens.
The mobilization causes Henn Ahas to hesitate about which military force to join, as he is unrelated to either group and has conflicting allegiances with his brother in one group and schoolmates in the other.
Ahas is captured by Red Guards forces while trying to decide which side to take.
In captivity, Ahas meets Marta, a young bourgeois girl.
Despite peaceful speeches, the army of t.outcomes: Protagonist survives and makes a choice about his allegiance. The conflict is unresolved with partial resolution through personal transformation.
Despite peaceful speeches, the army of t.theme: loyalty vs duty; identity crisis; moral ambiguity

Powerful widow Miss Alice and her lawyer.coa: A wealthy widow offers a large grant to the church on condition that the cardinal's secretary be used as a liaison. The church agrees and sends a lay brother to live with the widow. The lay brother is sent to reside in an altar-like model of the widow's mansion, where he meets her representatives: a lawyer and a butler.
Powerful widow Miss Alice and her lawyer.outcomes: Julian survives his immersion into the corrupt world and gains insight into its dynamics. The conflict is unresolved with a sense of moral ambiguity and critique of institutional corruption.
Powerful widow Miss Alice and her lawyer.theme: corruption; power dynamics; morality vs institution

Ringo, Frank and their sidekick Tom are.coa: Ringo, Frank, and Tom are hired by Walton to rescue his daughter. They successfully shoot their way through a band of Mexicans and complete the rescue. After the rescue, Jane marries Ringo, causing tension in the friendship between Ringo and Frank. The two do not meet again until after the American Civil War ends.
Ringo, Frank and their sidekick Tom are.outcomes: Protagonist achieves personal success and stability as a town sheriff. The conflict is unresolved due to the antagonistic actions of Frank, who obtains wealth but also perpetuates violence and harm.
Ringo, Frank and their sidekick Tom are.theme: friendship vs loyalty; redemption through service; consequences of past actions

Famed gunman Arizona Colt is living in n.coa:
Arizona Colt learns he has a bounty on his head and decides to return to town.
He fakes his own death in order to avoid capture.
The wealthy landlord Moreno asks him to rescue his daughter from Keene's control.
Arizona refuses, wanting to settle down with Sheena instead. 
Double Whiskey is captured by Keene, prompting Arizona to reconsider.
Arizona accepts the mission and sets out to face Keene.
Famed gunman Arizona Colt is living in n.outcomes: Protagonist successfully rescues the landlord's daughter and resolves conflict with antagonist. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as protagonist confronts past and chooses to act.
Famed gunman Arizona Colt is living in n.theme: redemption; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

All That I Love is a film about a young.coa: A young musician's father secures rehearsal space for his punk band in the officer's hall of the police barracks due to his connection as an official of the local military police. The musician rebels against the official repression of lyrical freedom and political activism, despite his family ties. His love interest is initially forbidden from seeing him by her father, who is a member of Solidarity, but they continue to see each other secretly.
All That I Love is a film about a young.outcomes: Protagonist successfully expresses himself through music and inspires others to do the same, contributing to the Solidarity movement. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social change.
All That I Love is a film about a young.theme: artistic freedom; resistance against oppression; solidarity

In the late 1800's, Franz Ferdinand, hei.coa: The heir to the Austro-Hungarian empire falls in love with a Czech countess who is not of royal blood, breaching protocol. The Crown allows their union only after they agree to a morganatic marriage. The emperor demotes the heir to inspector general of the army due to his disregard for protocols and his political ideas. In June 1914, the countess seeks permission to accompany the heir to Sarajevo because she fears for his safety.
In the late 1800's, Franz Ferdinand, hei.outcomes: Protagonist dies in a tragic event that sparks global conflict. The conflict is unresolved with far-reaching consequences.
In the late 1800's, Franz Ferdinand, hei.theme: love vs duty; social class vs personal freedom; tragedy and its consequences

In his remote jungle hideout, the evil D.coa: Dr. Fu Manchu discovers a deadly venom in a lost city in the Amazonian jungle that affects only men, causing blindness and eventual death after six weeks. The venom can be transmitted to women through bites from venomous snakes, making them carriers of the "kiss of death". Using mind control, Dr. Fu Manchu targets six women who are then sent to attack key people with political influence, including Nayland Smith. This prevents those individuals from interfering with Dr. Fu Manchu's plans.
In his remote jungle hideout, the evil D.outcomes: Protagonist thwarts Dr. Fu Manchu's plan for world domination. The conflict is resolved with protagonist's success in stopping the villainous plot.
In his remote jungle hideout, the evil D.theme: power of manipulation; danger of unchecked ambition; protection vs exploitation

In late nineteenth century Alfred Dreyfu.coa:
A French army officer of Jewish heritage is falsely accused of espionage.
The officer is found guilty of treason and drummed out of the army.
The officer is sent to prison on Devil's Island.
The officer's family takes up his case, as does a writer who believes the original investigation was flawed due to anti-Semitism.
The writer publishes an article exposing the flaws in the original investigation and accusing the military of anti-Semitic bias.
Evidence emerges that points to another person as the true culprit.
The true culprit is exposed.
In late nineteenth century Alfred Dreyfu.outcomes: Protagonist is exonerated and reinstated into the army. The conflict is resolved with justice served, but broader societal issues of anti-Semitism remain.
In late nineteenth century Alfred Dreyfu.theme: justice vs prejudice; loyalty and advocacy; redemption

Daniel, 35, is haunted by a stranger who.coa:
A stranger breaks into the protagonist's house and spies on him regularly.
The stranger confronts the protagonist one day, telling him that he is the man of his life.
The protagonist is shocked by this admission and tells the stranger to stay away.
The protagonist has a girlfriend who prioritizes her career over their relationship.
Daniel, 35, is haunted by a stranger who.outcomes: Protagonist manages to evade the antagonist and maintain some stability in his life. The conflict is unresolved, but protagonist's awareness of his situation has increased.

Maya Vargas (Jennifer Lopez) is the assi.coa: The assistant manager awaits a store visit from an executive, Mr. Weiskopf, who has been informed about her exceptional work and dedication to the company. During the meeting, Mr. Weiskopf announces that Maya is being passed up for promotion in favor of Arthur, citing company policy as the reason. The stated reason for this decision is that Maya only holds a GED and lacks a college degree, which is a requirement for advancement. This news is met with surprise and disappointment from Maya and those who had assured her of the promotion.
Maya Vargas (Jennifer Lopez) is the assi.outcomes: Protagonist Maya achieves success and stability through her new role, but also uncovers a long-hidden family secret. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation and professional advancement.
Maya Vargas (Jennifer Lopez) is the assi.theme: social mobility; deception vs authenticity; family secrets

Paul Exben is a remarkable success story.coa: A partner in a prestigious law firm achieves great success and wealth. He discovers his wife's affair with another man, leading him to commit a fatal mistake in a moment of fury. The body of the other man is left behind, and Paul realizes that his perfect life is shattered. Paul assumes the identity of the deceased man and escapes to a remote region along the Adriatic coast.
Paul Exben is a remarkable success story.outcomes: Protagonist survives and achieves a new beginning, transforming his life through self-discovery. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation but broader emotional scars remain.

In 1919, after just a few months of comm.coa: The Hungarian Republic of Councils is dissolved by a nationalist counter-revolution. Admiral Horthy assumes power as the Regent of Hungary. The nationalist forces pursue and execute Hungarian Red Army soldiers, forcing many to flee for their lives. One such soldier takes refuge on a farm run by two women in the Great Hungarian Plain.
In 1919, after just a few months of comm.outcomes: Protagonist survives in hiding but faces moral dilemma. The conflict is unresolved as protagonist must choose between personal safety and reporting crime to authorities.
In 1919, after just a few months of comm.theme: survival under oppression; loyalty vs duty; moral complexity

Law student Martin Bork (Nikolaj Coster.coa: A law student gets a job as night watchman at the Forensic Medicine Institute to have more time for studying. The student is tasked with making rounds to check on deceased people kept in the institute. A series of murders occurs among women in Copenhagen, and strange events start happening in the medical department. The student becomes confused with one of the murder victims, leading to suspicion that they are involved.
Law student Martin Bork (Nikolaj Coster.outcomes: Protagonist is cleared as a suspect and resolves his confusion. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader threats remain.
Law student Martin Bork (Nikolaj Coster.theme: identity crisis; paranoia vs reality; justice vs misidentification

In Houston, Texas, Kris is a 14-year-old.coa: A 14-year-old girl breaks into the home of a man who lives down the street from her in an attempt to steal his alcohol and painkillers. She is caught by the homeowner, but instead of turning her in, he offers her a deal to make amends for her actions. The girl agrees to help the homeowner clean his house and repair his chicken coop as payment for not reporting her crime to the authorities. As she works, she begins to learn about the homeowner's past as a bull rider.
In Houston, Texas, Kris is a 14-year-old.outcomes: Protagonist finds a new passion and direction in life through her friendship with antagonist. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation, but broader challenges remain.

Between 1980 and 1982, Toronto bank empl.coa: Promotion to assistant branch manager gives access to bigger and bigger accounts between 1980 and 1982. This promotion allows for skimming of larger amounts from the bank, which is used for personal gambling activities. The gambler makes weekly trips to Atlantic City, where they are treated well by a casino manager. These trips and lavish treatment go unnoticed by the boss, who trusts the employee.
Between 1980 and 1982, Toronto bank empl.outcomes: Protagonist is caught and brought to justice, facing consequences for his actions. The conflict is resolved with a negative outcome of personal failure and loss of reputation.

The story is set in the Amsterdam of the.coa:
The oaf is neglected by his mother.
He is expelled from school multiple times.
A teacher tries to handle and restrain him.
In a fit of rage and fear, he kills his mother with a knife.
He is sent to a youth detention center.
His father returns from the sea.
He decides to take care of his son together with his wife Aunt Jans.
The story is set in the Amsterdam of the.outcomes: Protagonist survives and receives care from father and aunt after being released from detention. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation through family reintegration.

During his escape, bank robber Joe Maybe.coa: A bank robber escapes and sees US Marshal Jim Noonan stumble and fall off a cliff to his death. The bank robber enters a town on Noonan's horse and is mistaken for him. He decides to hide behind the badge and gains respect in the town as "Marshal Noonan." A past acquaintance, Tessa Milott, arrives in town and nearly reveals the bank robber's true identity by calling him by his surname in front of Judge Kyle.
During his escape, bank robber Joe Maybe.outcomes: Protagonist survives and maintains his cover identity as Marshal Noonan. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing threats from Sam Teeler and the bank robbery.

During the Russian Civil War (1918–1922).coa:
A female commissar finds herself pregnant during the Russian Civil War.
The pregnancy forces her to stay with a poor Jewish blacksmith's family until her child is born.
She lives with the Magazannik family, who initially are not enthusiastic about having her under their roof but eventually share their food and help her prepare for childbirth.
The frontline advances closer to the town, causing the Jews to fear a pogrom by the White Army.
During the Russian Civil War (1918–1922).outcomes: Protagonist returns to her army regiment, leaving her newborn behind. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation but broader threats remain.
During the Russian Civil War (1918–1922).theme: motherhood vs duty; loyalty vs responsibility; human resilience under war

Andrea (Virginia Madsen) is a teenage gi.coa: A teenage girl wins a scholarship to Ettinger, a formerly all-male boarding school. She leaves behind her boyfriend but soon discovers that her friends are changing into personality-less drones. Investigation reveals that the faculty is harvesting life-sustaining chemicals from the student body, causing them to become perfect students focused on academics and obedience. The girl's professor takes pity on her due to her resemblance to a former lover and spares her from this fate.
Andrea (Virginia Madsen) is a teenage gi.outcomes: Protagonist escapes the fate of becoming a drone and is saved by her boyfriend's actions. The conflict is resolved with external intervention and personal survival.
Andrea (Virginia Madsen) is a teenage gi.theme: resistance against conformity; exploitation vs empowerment; individuality in oppressive systems

Jake (Jason Yee) is a hired driver for a.coa: A seedy escort service operates out of "The Naked Eye" Strip Club. A high-class escort is hired for a job, which involves cell phone calls made the night she is murdered. The escort is found dead with no leads except for records of these cell phone calls. Jake, a driver for the escort service, discovers the body and becomes determined to find her killer.
Jake (Jason Yee) is a hired driver for a.outcomes: Protagonist risks everything and walks a bloody path to find justice for Sandy's murder. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader threats of corruption remain.
Jake (Jason Yee) is a hired driver for a.theme: revenge; love vs danger; moral ambiguity

The plot concerns the coming of age and.coa:
Three youths from Holyoke, Iowa are seduced by the gangsta rap lifestyle and yearn to be African American.
They venture to Cabrini–Green housing project in Chicago, Illinois, where they encounter actual criminals.
The youths come into conflict with the police while trying to navigate the unfamiliar environment of the housing project.
A confrontation between the youths and a group of actual criminals leads to one of them being beaten up.
The plot concerns the coming of age and.outcomes: Protagonists are forced back to their original identity and community. The conflict is resolved with a partial transformation as they abandon the gangsta lifestyle.
The plot concerns the coming of age and.theme: identity crisis; cultural appropriation; class struggle

The film revolves around two love storie.coa: Two friends holiday in Kashmir, where they meet two mountain girls and begin affairs. One friend's affair is true and reciprocated, while the other disregards his faithful partner. The couple facing a true love affair face parental opposition, accidents, and an attempted forced marriage. They are finally reunited after overcoming these obstacles.
The film revolves around two love storie.outcomes: Protagonist Pran achieves true love with Reshma. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation for both protagonists, but the antagonist's actions have tragic consequences.
The film revolves around two love storie.theme: true love; redemption; loyalty vs duty

The film follows Michael Sullivan, the s.coa: Michael leaves his job at a large bank and lands a position with the United Nations (UN) as an assistant to Under-Secretary-General Costa "Pasha" Passaris in the fall of 2002. He is assigned to work on the Oil-for-Food Programme, which has been operating since 1995. Local UN chief diplomat Christina Dupre reveals her concerns about corruption within the programme to Pasha, and plans to publish a report exposing the issue. This is the first time Michael hears about the problem.
The film follows Michael Sullivan, the s.outcomes: Protagonist exposes the corruption in the Oil-for-Food Programme, leading to a partial resolution of the conflict with personal transformation but broader systemic issues remaining. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation and social reintegration.
The film follows Michael Sullivan, the s.theme: corruption; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Granger, a former Special Forces soldier.coa:
A former Special Forces soldier is attacked by hooded assassins in their home.
The soldier fights off the assassins but is pulled into a time portal as they try to kill him.
The soldier finds themselves in the past, in the war-torn Kingdom of Ehb, several hundred years earlier than their own time.
They are joined by an unlikely band of allies and a female doctor named Manhattan.
Granger, a former Special Forces soldier.outcomes: Protagonist successfully defeats the Holy Mother and frees the Kingdom from Raven's tyranny. The conflict is resolved with a partial resolution, as the protagonist must still find a way back to his own time.
Granger, a former Special Forces soldier.theme: redemption; survival under adversity; loyalty vs duty

In 1946, bigoted, draft-dodging, gold-di.coa: In 1946, a deal is proposed to sell land owned by two families to owners of a northern canning plant, but it requires purchasing two additional plots, one owned by Henry's cousin and his wife, and the other by a black farmer. The farmer refuses to sell, forming an unlikely partnership with Henry's cousin to strengthen their claim to their land. This infuriates Henry, who tries to persuade his wife to charge the farmer with illegal ownership after Rose, the farmer's mother and Julie's former wet nurse, dies suddenly.
In 1946, bigoted, draft-dodging, gold-di.outcomes: Protagonist Henry Warren is thwarted in his attempts to acquire land and loses his wife Julie Ann. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation for Henry, but broader social injustices remain.
In 1946, bigoted, draft-dodging, gold-di.theme: justice vs prejudice; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

The film takes place in an unidentified.coa: Recent attacks made upon young teenagers in an unidentified city at night occur. Observers identify that these attacks are being made by a group of vampires residing in the city. A vampire leader, Countess Bathory, is correctly identified as the mastermind behind the attacks. She hopes to use humans to feed her growing vampire clan and eventually seize control of the city.
The film takes place in an unidentified.outcomes: Protagonist succeeds in defeating the vampires and preventing their leader's evil plans. The conflict is resolved with conflict_resolved and personal transformation as Van Helsing gains experience and skills to fight against supernatural threats.
The film takes place in an unidentified.theme: good vs evil; power struggle; human resilience

Set in Tokyo in 1940, the peaceful life.coa:
The father is arrested and accused of being a Communist.
His arrest causes his wife to work frantically from morning to night to maintain the household and bring up their two daughters.
World War II breaks out, casting dark shadows on the entire country.
The war exacerbates the family's struggles, but the mother continues to try to keep her cheerful determination.
Set in Tokyo in 1940, the peaceful life.outcomes: Mother's love and determination sustain the family despite father's absence and war-time hardships. The conflict is unresolved due to ongoing war and societal turmoil.
Set in Tokyo in 1940, the peaceful life.theme: motherly love; peace under adversity; resilience

Tom Horn tries to steal back $200 he los.coa: Tom Horn tries to steal back $200 he lost in a poker game. He is thrown into a jail cell with an outlaw whose gang members break them out. The gang robs a Colorado mining company, which leads to Tom being recognized by the company's bookkeeper. The gang rides to Tombstone, Arizona, where they are unhappy about Tom's marriage to the bookkeeper and her decision to join them.
Tom Horn tries to steal back $200 he los.outcomes: Protagonist fails to escape law enforcement and is ultimately captured. The conflict is unresolved with no personal transformation or change.
Tom Horn tries to steal back $200 he los.theme: crime vs consequence; loyalty vs self-interest; morality in gray areas

Tsao, the emperor's first eunuch, has su.coa: Tsao, the emperor's first eunuch, successfully bests General Yu in a confrontation. The general is beheaded and his remaining children are exiled from China. They are being escorted to the western border of the Chinese empire when Tsao plots to have them killed. He orders his secret police to lie in ambush at the desolate Dragon Gate Inn.
Tsao, the emperor's first eunuch, has su.outcomes: Protagonist Tsao's plan is thwarted and General Yu's children are saved. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as Tsao's actions are exposed, but broader threats remain.
Tsao, the emperor's first eunuch, has su.theme: power corruption; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

H6 tells the story of Antonio Frau, a se.coa: A man is released from jail after serving 25 years for a violent murder. He inherits an old motel from a relative he never knew. This inheritance prompts him to start killing again, targeting people who have lost the will to live. He takes his victims to room Number 6 in the motel where he kills them.
H6 tells the story of Antonio Frau, a se.outcomes: Protagonist is arrested and his plan to become rich and famous is foiled. The conflict is unresolved as he is caught but his true intentions remain unclear.
H6 tells the story of Antonio Frau, a se.theme: the blurred lines between good and evil; the destructive nature of grief; the dangers of unchecked ambition

Mason is summoned to the Laxter mansion.coa:
A mysterious fire breaks out at night, resulting in Peter's death.
The will is read, revealing that Wilma is to be disinherited, and Sam and Frank inherit the estate under certain conditions.
Sam threatens Clinker, prompting Perry to intervene.
Perry discovers that Laxter's death was suspicious and the family fortune and diamonds are missing.
Mason is summoned to the Laxter mansion.outcomes: Protagonist uncovers the truth about Peter Laxter's death and the missing fortune. The conflict is resolved with the exposure of the truth, but the full extent of the consequences remains unclear.

Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson come out.coa: A mysterious murder occurs. The Scowrers, an American criminal organisation, asks Professor Moriarty to exact revenge on John Douglas, who had sent them to prison by informing on them. Holmes and Watson come out of retirement to investigate the murder. They discover that Moriarty is involved in the case.
Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson come out.outcomes: Protagonist (Holmes) successfully resolves the case and defeats the antagonist (Moriarty). The conflict is resolved with a sense of justice restored, but broader threats from The Scowrers remain.
Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson come out.theme: justice vs evil; loyalty and partnership; intellectual superiority

A school teacher has the locals learning.coa: The teacher's assistant begins teaching Bomba how to read. The locals are learning to read under the teacher's guidance. Two ivory poachers arrive in the village and try to force Bomba to lead them to a hidden cache of ivory, threatening him if he refuses. Bomba calls on his elephant friends for help.
A school teacher has the locals learning.outcomes: Protagonist successfully educates the community, while Bomba's actions protect his friends and the village from harm. The conflict is resolved with a decisive confrontation.

After missing a train, Marc meets and fa.coa:
A person misses a train.
They meet someone on the platform and fall in love.
The two agree to meet again at a specific location without exchanging contact information.
On the agreed-upon meeting time, one of them is held up and they miss each other.
The person who was supposed to be there moves to a new country with their partner.
Later, the first person meets another woman from the same family as the previous love interest.
After missing a train, Marc meets and fa.outcomes: Protagonist experiences unrequited love but finds new love with ally. The conflict is unresolved as protagonist still pines for original love interest.
After missing a train, Marc meets and fa.theme: unrequited love; chance encounters; family ties

Wilhelm Lehmann is informed that he will.coa: Wilhelm Lehmann receives news that he will receive an award on his sixty-fifth birthday. His children are informed of this, but none of them can attend to honor him and their mother Auguste. A series of comical mistakes ensue, causing chaos and disrupting the planned celebration. The family eventually arrives at the ceremony, where Wilhelm is awarded the Order.
Wilhelm Lehmann is informed that he will.outcomes: Protagonist feels honored and celebrated by family on his special day. The conflict is resolved with a joyful family reunion and traditional celebration.
Wilhelm Lehmann is informed that he will.theme: family bonding; generational values; recognition of hard work

Leon Phelps (also known as the \"Ladies.coa: A young, suave man gives dubious romantic advice and lovemaking tips on a broadcast program, proclaiming he will court any woman who weighs no more than 250 pounds. He goes too far during a broadcast and is fired as a result. After being fired, he receives a note from one of his former flames, expressing her desire for him to return and offering support in high style. The note is unsigned, leaving the suave man uncertain about which woman wants him back.
Leon Phelps (also known as the \"Ladies.outcomes: Protagonist is forced to confront the consequences of his actions and potentially face retribution. The conflict is unresolved with a high risk of further complications.
Leon Phelps (also known as the \"Ladies.theme: consequences of one's actions; deception vs honesty; male entitlement

While living with her grandmother in Pol.coa: A young woman falls in love with her boyfriend, who suggests traveling around Europe and working sporadically to pay for their trip. They cross over into Germany, where the boyfriend's true intentions are revealed and the woman is sold as a prostitute. The captors attempt to break and condition her to a life of servitude, trying to keep her from escaping or seeking help. Time passes, and the woman struggles to stay sane amidst her ordeal.
While living with her grandmother in Pol.outcomes: Protagonist manages to escape her captors and regain freedom. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal struggle for recovery and healing.

Based upon a description in a film magaz.coa: Leila Porter grows tired of her husband due to his prosaic nature and careless personal habits. She obtains a divorce from him. She marries James' friend Schuyler Van Sutphen, but discovers that he is unpleasant. Leila's ex-husband changes after the divorce and expresses his continued love for her.
Based upon a description in a film magaz.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a second chance at love with her first husband. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and reconciliation.
Based upon a description in a film magaz.theme: second chances; love vs compatibility; personal growth

Winky (Ebbie Tam) and her mother (Hanyi.coa:
Winky's family travels from China to the Netherlands.
The move causes Winky to experience culture shock due to differences between the two countries.
She struggles to learn the Dutch language, adding to her difficulties with adjusting to her new environment.
Winky finds comfort and companionship with her neighbors who own an old horse that she instantly adores.
Winky (Ebbie Tam) and her mother (Hanyi.outcomes: Protagonist successfully adapts and forms new relationships with neighbors. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and a new pet.
Winky (Ebbie Tam) and her mother (Hanyi.theme: adapting to change; finding comfort in community; resilience in loss

The story is narrated by Sunny von Bülow.coa:
A Christmas party is held where the main character falls into diabetic shock.
The main character is hospitalized in a coma.
Claus is charged with attempting to murder the main character by giving them an overdose of insulin.
Most people believe Claus is guilty due to his strained relationship with the main character and his cold demeanor.
The story is narrated by Sunny von Bülow.outcomes: Protagonist (Claus) is acquitted due to successful defense by Dershowitz. The conflict is resolved with a verdict of not guilty but broader questions about justice and morality remain.
The story is narrated by Sunny von Bülow.theme: justice vs prejudice; morality in the face of uncertainty; social class and privilege

The ambitious Dr. Knock arrives in the c.coa:
Dr. Knock arrives in Saint-Maurice to succeed Dr. Parpalaid.
The villagers' health is found to be excellent by Dr. Knock, who realizes he was misled about the village's medical needs.
Dr. Knock convinces the villagers that a healthy person is unaware of their own sickness.
The villagers begin to take to bed, and the hotel is converted into a clinic.
The ambitious Dr. Knock arrives in the c.outcomes: Villagers are convinced of their own sickness and become patients. The conflict is resolved with a shift in the villagers' perception of health.

Lilting tells the story of a mother's at.coa:
A mother's life is disrupted by the sudden appearance of her deceased son's lover.
The arrival of the lover sparks a chain of events that forces the mother to confront the reality of her son's personal life.
The mother and the lover struggle to communicate with each other due to their different languages, which exacerbates their emotional pain.
Their attempts at communication are further complicated by cultural differences.
Lilting tells the story of a mother's at.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a deeper understanding of her son through the connection with his lover. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation and emotional healing.

In medieval Persia, Kashma Baba is a mil.coa: A rowdy banquet is held at night, and the morning after, a person takes shelter under Kashma's roof. Word comes that the wicked Caliph is looking for this person. Kashma flees with her to his father's palace. The evil schemers become aware of their escape.
In medieval Persia, Kashma Baba is a mil.outcomes: Protagonist and Kiki escape danger but the conflict is unresolved as evil schemers remain active. The outcome is a temporary reprieve with no lasting resolution.

Dr. John H. Watson (Robert Duvall) becom.coa: Watson becomes convinced that Sherlock is delusional due to cocaine addiction. Moriarty visits Watson to complain about being harassed by Sherlock. Watson enlists the aid of Mycroft to trick Sherlock into traveling to Vienna for treatment. Mycroft convinces Sherlock to go to Vienna, where he will be treated by Freud.
Dr. John H. Watson (Robert Duvall) becom.outcomes: Protagonist successfully treats friend, resolving conflict. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as Holmes confronts his dark secret.
Dr. John H. Watson (Robert Duvall) becom.theme: addiction; mental health; truth vs delusion

Victor (Henshall) is an actor in London.coa:
Victor meets two mysterious dustmen.
The dustmen offer Victor a chance to travel back in time and relive his romance with Sylvia.
Victor accepts the offer and travels back in time.
In this new timeline, Victor's actions lead to Sylvia having an affair with Dave instead of staying with him.
Victor (Henshall) is an actor in London.outcomes: Protagonist's relationship is redefined as he fails to prevent his ex-girlfriend from moving on. The conflict is unresolved with a shift in personal circumstances but no resolution.
Victor (Henshall) is an actor in London.theme: lost love; regret; the consequences of past actions

A young man is commissioned by his jewel.coa:
A young man receives a commission from his father to transport a valuable necklace from Istanbul to Marseille.
He sets out on the sea voyage, carrying the necklace.
During the journey, he is ambushed by a group of thieves who use a beautiful woman as a distraction.
The thieves attempt to steal the necklace from him.
A young man is commissioned by his jewel.outcomes: Protagonist successfully delivers the necklace, achieving his goal. The conflict is resolved with personal success but broader threats remain.
A young man is commissioned by his jewel.theme: trust vs deception; loyalty and protection; danger on unfamiliar terrain

After a fall from a horse, a wealthy Mar.coa:
A wealthy Marquis falls from his horse.
The Marquis is comforted by the singing of a beautiful woman while he lies injured.
He unexpectedly recovers from his injuries.
Believing her to be Empress Eugenie, the wife of Napoleon III, the Marquis tries to seek out this young woman.
After a fall from a horse, a wealthy Mar.outcomes: Protagonist learns the truth about the woman's identity and resolves his misunderstanding. The conflict is resolved with personal growth through correction of mistaken assumptions.
After a fall from a horse, a wealthy Mar.theme: misconceptions; social class vs reality; love vs deception

Wanda Nash (Carole Lombard), an actress.coa: An actress from Brooklyn decides to masquerade as a Swedish princess in order to land a film contract with a big Hollywood studio. She boards the liner Mammoth bound for New York, where she encounters a concertina-playing band leader with a criminal record in his past. The actress and the band leader are both blackmailed by Robert M. Darcy, who is killed shortly after.
Wanda Nash (Carole Lombard), an actress.outcomes: Protagonist Wanda Nash escapes suspicion and clears her name. The conflict is resolved with justice served through their own efforts.
Wanda Nash (Carole Lombard), an actress.theme: identity deception; class vs status; appearance vs reality

Sisters Penny and Jane Lowe are arrested.coa:
The sisters are arrested for hitchhiking and skinny-dipping.
They are sentenced to work on a rural Texas farm as part of their punishment.
The judge who sentenced them is dating the corrupt agricultural magnate, Russ Tropp.
Tropp uses his relationship with the judge to ensure that all delinquents and rule breakers are sent to work at his farm.
Sisters Penny and Jane Lowe are arrested.outcomes: The corrupt system is exposed, but not fully dismantled. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation for some characters, but broader social issues remain.
Sisters Penny and Jane Lowe are arrested.theme: corruption; exploitation; justice vs abuse of power

Young aspiring model Aki Shima is kidnap.coa:
A young aspiring model is kidnapped by a mysterious blind man and taken to his home in a warehouse.
The kidnapper reveals that he is a sculptor who wishes to use the victim as an unwilling muse for his greatest work.
As time passes, the victim becomes entranced by their captor and they begin a romantic relationship.
Their relationship evolves into a series of sadomasochistic games.
Young aspiring model Aki Shima is kidnap.outcomes: Protagonist is transformed into a work of art and ultimately killed by antagonist. The conflict is resolved with protagonist's transformation and antagonist's completion of their masterpiece.
Young aspiring model Aki Shima is kidnap.theme: artistic obsession; blurred lines between love and captivity; the cost of creative vision

During World War II, American deserter L.coa:
American soldier escapes from a Japanese firing squad during World War II.
The soldier hides in the wilds of Borneo, where he is discovered by a head-hunting tribe of Dayaks.
The Dayaks adopt the soldier because they consider his blue eyes divine, and he becomes their leader.
The soldier becomes the reigning king of the Dayaks.
During World War II, American deserter L.outcomes: Protagonist successfully defends his adopted tribe against invading forces. The conflict is resolved with a partial victory and the preservation of tribal autonomy.
During World War II, American deserter L.theme: cultural identity; loyalty vs duty; survival under adversity

After looking for financial backing to s.coa:
College friends look for financial backing to start a business importing Italian scooters.
They meet businessman Gerard, who initially helps them with their plans.
Gerard's behavior turns violent and he begins blackmailing the pair for increasingly large sums of money while psychologically terrorizing them.
The two men are unable to find help from the police.
After looking for financial backing to s.outcomes: Protagonists are able to escape Gerard's control and potentially find justice through the film's exposure. The conflict is resolved with personal survival and partial resolution of the threat.

Benny Martin leaves his small town of Pa.coa:
Benny leaves his small town of Pantera to join the Army after getting into trouble with the police.
While away, Benny's girlfriend is romanced by another man, but refuses his advances and remains loyal to Benny.
Months pass without news from Benny, and eventually the girlfriend agrees to marry the other man.
She later discovers that Benny was killed in action and has been awarded the Medal of Honor posthumously.
The mayor plans a rally for the medal presentation at a beautiful house to avoid embarrassing Benny's family with their modest home.
Benny's father refuses to go along with the plan, insisting on holding the ceremony at his own home instead.
Benny Martin leaves his small town of Pa.outcomes: Protagonist is deceased and honored with Medal of Honor. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as the girlfriend chooses loyalty over convenience.

The eponymous protagonist saves the life.coa: An avalanche approaches a location where someone is in danger. The protagonist directs energy remotely at the avalanche, saving that person's life. This event sparks a series of descriptions about modern technology and its capabilities, often prefaced by the phrase "As you know...". A villain captures the person who was saved earlier.
The eponymous protagonist saves the life.outcomes: Protagonist successfully rescues heroine and saves her life. The conflict is resolved with external intervention, but broader technological themes remain.

Lou Peckinpaugh (Peter Falk), a bumbling.coa:
A private detective tries to prove himself innocent of his partner's murder.
He helps various people recover a lost treasure, but many are murdered in unusual death poses as they search for it.
The detective learns that all these deaths were connected to a large egg-shaped diamond.
Vladimir Korsokovowitz is shot by Marcel during the theft and dies after bleeding for 10 years.
Lou Peckinpaugh (Peter Falk), a bumbling.outcomes: Protagonist clears his name of the murder accusation. The conflict is resolved with personal vindication and social reintegration.
Lou Peckinpaugh (Peter Falk), a bumbling.theme: deception; appearance vs reality; chaos and order

Four unrelated shorts by four different.coa: A teenage girl embarks on a journey home from somewhere. She encounters various people and situations that lead to her involvement in sexual misadventures. The events escalate and become more complicated, ultimately resulting in consequences for the girl. Her experiences have a lasting impact on her life.
Four unrelated shorts by four different.outcomes: Protagonists in each story experience negative outcomes due to their actions. The conflicts are unresolved with no personal transformation or social reintegration.

Johnny Walker (Mickey Rourke) is a down-.coa:
A down-and-out boxer moves into a seaside resort with brain damage.
He falls in love with a carnival owner who shares much in common with him.
The boxer befriends a corrupt promoter who wants to use him as muscle in a robbery.
The promoter asks for the boxer's help, and he is torn between his loyalty to the promoter and his love for the carnival owner.
Johnny Walker (Mickey Rourke) is a down-.outcomes: Protagonist chooses love over friendship, rejecting the corrupt offer. The conflict is unresolved as protagonist's future remains uncertain.
Johnny Walker (Mickey Rourke) is a down-.theme: love vs loyalty; redemption through relationships; moral compromise

After the Metropolitan Police fail to ap.coa: The Metropolitan Police fail to apprehend a serial killer. Sherlock Holmes is approached to investigate recent murders in the Whitechapel district of London. The victims are discovered to be companions of Annie Crook, a woman locked in a mental institution. This revelation leads Holmes to suspect that there may be more to the case than initially meets the eye.
After the Metropolitan Police fail to ap.outcomes: Protagonist succeeds in uncovering the truth behind the murders. The conflict is resolved with justice served, but broader societal issues remain.
After the Metropolitan Police fail to ap.theme: justice vs corruption; power dynamics; truth and deception

Joëlle (Pénélope Lamour) is a beautiful.coa:
A beautiful executive develops a mysterious vaginal infection after being seduced by an attractive blonde girl.
The infection causes her vagina to talk and lead her into indecent sexual acts.
It is revealed that her problems stem from past adolescent sexual hardships and obsessions.
She has sex with her husband, passing the "infection" to his penis.
Joëlle (Pénélope Lamour) is a beautiful.outcomes: Protagonist Joëlle achieves a form of resolution through confronting and resolving her past issues. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation.

As described in a film magazine reviews,.coa:
Prince Danilo meets Sally the dancer.
He proposes marriage, but his uncle and aunt object because she is a commoner.
Sally marries Baron Sadoja, who later dies from a stroke.
The death of Baron Sadoja leads Prince Danilo's parents to encourage him to marry Sally.
As described in a film magazine reviews,.outcomes: Protagonist survives the duel and becomes heir to the throne after Crown Prince Mirko's assassination. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as protagonist gains a new status and perspective.
As described in a film magazine reviews,.theme: social class vs love; loyalty vs duty; redemption through sacrifice

Kevin Matthews (Todd Eric Andrews), beco.coa:
A new pupil joins the school.
The pupil is rejected from the trendy Key Club.
The pupil joins the Science Club instead.
While exploring the lab, the pupil discovers hidden vials made by a former student behind a panel.
The pupil accidentally drinks the contents of the vials and develops psychokinetic powers.
With his newfound abilities, the pupil starts to lift girls' dresses and humiliate the Key Club jocks, becoming popular in the process.
The Key Club plots a cruel revenge against the pupil.
Kevin Matthews (Todd Eric Andrews), beco.outcomes: Protagonist's life is transformed by newfound abilities but also attracts danger. The conflict is unresolved with potential for escalation.
Kevin Matthews (Todd Eric Andrews), beco.theme: power and responsibility; social hierarchy; identity formation

After barely escaping with his life from.coa:
An archaeological dig in Tikal, Guatemala is attacked and a person barely escapes with their life.
They return to New York.
In New York, they learn about the rediscovery of Colonel Percy Fawcett's writings.
The rediscovered writings reveal a story that could change history and challenge scientific beliefs.
After barely escaping with his life from.outcomes: Protagonist survives initial journey to the Amazon. The conflict is unresolved with broader threats remaining, including dangers within the mythical city itself.
After barely escaping with his life from.theme: discovery vs dogma; colonial legacy; cultural identity

Eve Tozer is a society heiress and flapp.coa:
Eve hires World War I ace pilot Patrick O'Malley and his aircraft to find her father before he is declared dead.
O'Malley agrees to take the job because he needs to leave town urgently himself.
Eve insists on accompanying O'Malley in his other aircraft, leading to their first argument.
The two of them set off from Istanbul in two biplanes, "Dorothy" and "Lillian", heading towards China.
Eve Tozer is a society heiress and flapp.outcomes: Protagonist (Eve) finds her father but fails to meet her original deadline due to the damage of their aircraft. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation and a new understanding of priorities.
Eve Tozer is a society heiress and flapp.theme: family loyalty; resourcefulness under pressure; navigating complex relationships

Barbara and Nicolas spin the perfect lov.coa:
The couple falls deeply in love and builds a life together.
They eventually decide they want a child to complete their family.
Barbara becomes pregnant after trying to conceive for some time.
The pregnancy causes tension between Barbara and Nicolas, particularly due to the arrival of a baby girl.
Barbara and Nicolas spin the perfect lov.outcomes: Protagonist couple navigates the challenges of parenthood together. The conflict is unresolved with potential for relational change.
Barbara and Nicolas spin the perfect lov.theme: love vs responsibility; family dynamics; identity through parenthood

Eight spaceships disappear and radio con.coa: Eight spaceships disappear and radio contact with the enormous space station "Margot" is broken off. A flight ban is decreed by Professor Maria Scholl and the high council, but one spaceship manages to leave earth despite this. The cause of the disappearance is discovered to be mysterious signals in Morse code coming from the constellation Cygnus. These signals are deciphered as the word "Eolomea", which appears to refer to a planet.
Eight spaceships disappear and radio con.outcomes: Protagonist uncovers a secretly planned expedition of stolen spaceships heading to Eolomea against government will. The conflict is unresolved with broader threats remaining.
Eight spaceships disappear and radio con.theme: deception; authority vs autonomy; unknown dangers

The film is about the youth perpetrator.coa: Victor hits Scum Baby, causing harm. The police are called as a result of this incident. Victor has the option to go to jail or undergo treatment for his behavior. Victor chooses the treatment instead of going to jail.
The film is about the youth perpetrator.outcomes: Protagonist achieves behavioral change and is cured. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation.
The film is about the youth perpetrator.theme: rehabilitation; violence vs nonviolence; human capacity for change

On New Year's Eve, Harry tells his grand.coa: On New Year's Eve, someone tells a bishop about their intention to marry an actress from a lower social class. The bishop recounts a cautionary tale via flashback about his own youth and a great love affair with a "fallen woman". This story is revealed to be about the bishop when he was 28 years old and met Rita Cavallini at a party given by Cornelius Van Tuyl. At this party, the bishop fell in love with Rita despite rumors that she was Van Tuyl's mistress.
On New Year's Eve, Harry tells his grand.outcomes: Protagonist achieves personal happiness through marrying the woman he loves. The conflict is resolved with a mix of personal transformation and acceptance of societal norms.
On New Year's Eve, Harry tells his grand.theme: love vs social convention; redemption through forgiveness; following one's heart

Peter and Carla have a wasted marriage a.coa: Peter buys expensive camping gear despite Carla's protests. The family travels to Moondah Beach with Cricket, the dog, and meets up with Luke and his girlfriend. Peter stops at a pub, the Eggleston Hotel, and leaves a message for Luke with the owner. Peter takes what he believes is a shortcut to the beach but gets lost instead.
Peter and Carla have a wasted marriage a.outcomes: Protagonist (Peter) fails to improve his marriage and relationship with partner (Carla). The conflict is unresolved with personal failure and relational deterioration.
Peter and Carla have a wasted marriage a.theme: toxic relationships; environmental degradation; consequences of neglect

A photographer and his daughter arrive i.coa:
A photographer and his daughter arrive in Africa to capture local wildlife on film.
They encounter a killer leopard instead of their intended subjects.
The leopard's presence causes them to abandon their photography plans.
The photographer's daughter wears a well-tailored leopard skin, which attracts the attention of Bomba the Jungle Boy.
A photographer and his daughter arrive i.outcomes: The protagonist's daughter is found safe, but the film ends without capturing any wildlife on camera. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation and social reintegration.
A photographer and his daughter arrive i.theme: discovery vs expectation; danger in the unknown; human connection in adversity

Hüseyin (Ata Demirer) is a young man liv.coa: A young man lives in a village with his grandparents. He is forced to leave the village for a city called İstanbul. In the city, he receives support from his clarinet and later from a bar singer. The singer leads a complicated life that becomes even more colorful with the arrival of the young man.
Hüseyin (Ata Demirer) is a young man liv.outcomes: Protagonist survives the transition to city life and maintains relationships with fiancée and new friends. The conflict is unresolved as protagonist navigates complex city life.
Hüseyin (Ata Demirer) is a young man liv.theme: adapting to change; finding support in unexpected places; identity formation

On his return a war veteran finds his fa.coa:
A war veteran returns home to find his farmhouse burned down.
The fire resulted in the deaths of his young Native American wife and little son.
The destruction was caused by a group of men who were enraged about the veteran's stance in the war.
These men are located in a nearby town.
On his return a war veteran finds his fa.outcomes: Protagonist achieves justice by killing the murderers, but at a personal cost. The conflict is resolved with conflict_resolved and personal transformation.

A beautiful young woman named Ivanna tra.coa:
A young woman travels to a remote estate seeking employment as a biochemist.
She finds herself attracted to the estate's owner and immerses herself in her work to suppress her desires.
A series of brutal murders occurs in the area, which she soon discovers is connected to the estate's owner.
The estate's owner transforms into a demon.
A beautiful young woman named Ivanna tra.outcomes: Protagonist is trapped in a state of servitude with no clear escape. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation but broader threats remain.
A beautiful young woman named Ivanna tra.theme: lust vs morality; power dynamics; objectification

Summer of 1956. Curious American girl De.coa:
A curious American girl arrives at St. Clara's Boarding School in Switzerland during the summer of 1956.
The headmistress wants to use this new student as a tool to discipline the other girls, but she is revealed to be more experienced and daring in sexual matters than anticipated.
This revelation prompts the girls to plot with boys from the private school across the lake to lose their virginity.
After having sex with one of these boys in a romantic setting, the girl is expelled from the school.
Summer of 1956. Curious American girl De.outcomes: Protagonist achieves freedom and departure from restrictive environment. The conflict is resolved with personal liberation but broader issues of institutional corruption remain.
Summer of 1956. Curious American girl De.theme: individuality vs conformity; rebellion against authority; coming of age

Vicky (Jonas Hämmerle) is the son of Hal.coa: The village of Flake falls under attack. The attacker kidnaps all the children except Vicky himself. Vicky's father Halvar and the other Vikings decide to rescue the kidnapped children. They set out on a mission to find and rescue the children.
Vicky (Jonas Hämmerle) is the son of Hal.outcomes: Protagonist Vicky successfully leads the rescue mission and proves his ingenuity. The conflict is resolved with the recovery of the kidnapped children and the acquisition of the treasure.
Vicky (Jonas Hämmerle) is the son of Hal.theme: ingenuity vs brute strength; loyalty and teamwork; redemption through self-discovery

Barry (Jack Scalia) works at the shippin.coa: Barry is invited to a barbecue at his boss's house. The guests play a demented version of charades, which leads to a fight breaking out among them. During the commotion, it is revealed that the next door neighbor has been screaming racial slurs over the fence. The widow of the ex-owner of Technoworks arrives and starts asking questions about the party.
Barry (Jack Scalia) works at the shippin.outcomes: Protagonist uncovers truth behind his boss's murder and the kidnapping scheme. The conflict is resolved with justice served and perpetrators brought to account.

The story is set around a Tawaif, Sultan.coa:
A Tawaif accidentally enters the life of a man already in love with another person.
The man is forced to claim that the Tawaif is his wife due to circumstances beyond his control.
The Tawaif wins over everyone's affection with her kindness and intelligence, including the man.
The man develops conflicting feelings for both the woman he initially loved and the Tawaif.
The story is set around a Tawaif, Sultan.outcomes: Dawood achieves a resolution of his conflicting emotions and commits to loving Sultana. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as Dawood acknowledges and accepts his new feelings.
The story is set around a Tawaif, Sultan.theme: love vs societal expectations; identity concealment; unrequited love

Queens car salesman Joey O'Brien must de.coa: A car salesman faces increasing pressures in his life due to an ex-wife demanding alimony, a missing daughter, two mistresses who are desperately in love with him, and a deadline to sell twelve cars or lose his job. He also has an outstanding loan to a Mafia don that must be repaid quickly or else he will lose his life. The car salesman's dealership is taken hostage by a motorcyclist who believes his wife is cheating on him. The motorcyclist is talked out of harming the hostages when the car salesman claims he is sleeping with the motorcyclist's wife, and the police surround the dealership.
Queens car salesman Joey O'Brien must de.outcomes: Protagonist achieves resolution of personal crises and finds a path towards reconciliation with his ex-wife. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social reintegration.
Queens car salesman Joey O'Brien must de.theme: chaos vs order; deception and truth; redemption through crisis

US-American adventurer Stark has been se.coa:
A US-American adventurer is sentenced to death in a Mexican village and is scheduled to be hanged.
A rich Mexican rancher intervenes and saves the adventurer from being executed, but demands that he repay the favour by rescuing his son Fidel.
The rancher tells the adventurer that Fidel has joined a gang, implying that he was coerced or persuaded into doing so, and asks him to bring Fidel back home.
The adventurer sets out to rescue Fidel, believing that the rancher is genuinely concerned about his well-being.
US-American adventurer Stark has been se.outcomes: Protagonist survives and uncovers the truth about the rancher's intentions. The conflict is unresolved with a twist in the narrative.
US-American adventurer Stark has been se.theme: deception; honour vs shame; paternal responsibility

Phoenix Police Detective Charlie Congers.coa: A gangster is forced by his peers to have Jackie Pruitt "whacked" for $1 million. The Italian hitman is hired to carry out the task. Congers assists the FBI in bringing Pruitt back to the USA under their protection. Pruitt is shot as she embraces Congers, just before leaving with the FBI.
Phoenix Police Detective Charlie Congers.outcomes: Pruitt's life is saved by Congers' actions, and Bomposa is killed along with his mafia lieutenants. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation for Congers and a sense of justice achieved.
Phoenix Police Detective Charlie Congers.theme: love vs loyalty; morality in the face of violence; redemption through sacrifice

Happily Ever After dissects the viabilit.coa: The central characters capriciously flirt in a bar. Vincent appears to win the affection of Gabrielle over many other potential suitors. The flirting is revealed to be a ruse; they are actually married with a child. This revelation sets off a chain of events as the film explores their tumultuous relationships with the opposite sex.
Happily Ever After dissects the viabilit.outcomes: The protagonist (Vincent) comes to understand the complexities of fidelity and the unfulfilling nature of casual relationships. The conflict is unresolved with a focus on the ongoing struggle for fulfillment.
Happily Ever After dissects the viabilit.theme: fidelity; love vs expectation; relationship dissatisfaction

A tough undercover cop named John (Cuba.coa: A young defector leaves a five-year-old girl with an undercover cop. The defector is killed, leaving the girl vulnerable to kidnapping. The girl is taken into an underground drug ring where she becomes trapped. The undercover cop learns about the girl's situation and decides to help her.
A tough undercover cop named John (Cuba.outcomes: Protagonist successfully rescues the girl and helps his ally resolve their own situation. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation for both protagonist and ally.
A tough undercover cop named John (Cuba.theme: redemption; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Lymon was 13 years old when the teenage.coa: The teenage group erupts from radios and jukeboxes with their hit song "Why Do Fools Fall in Love?" in 1956, leading to a movie appearance in Rock, Rock, Rock (1956). A solo singing career is started after the success of Mr. Rock and Roll (1957), but it ultimately falls apart. The singer's career ends by the time they are 18 years old. Seven years later, the singer dies from a heroin overdose.
Lymon was 13 years old when the teenage.outcomes: Protagonist's career is over by the time he is 18 years old. The conflict is resolved with a partial resolution of financial issues, but broader personal consequences remain.
Lymon was 13 years old when the teenage.theme: fleeting fame; addiction and downfall; legacy and inheritance

Mystery writer Charles Latimer meets Col.coa: A mystery writer meets a Turkish police officer who believes the writer would be interested in the career of a notorious criminal whose body has been identified in an Istanbul morgue. The officer shares this information with the writer, who is indeed intrigued and begins to investigate the details of the criminal's career independently. As the writer digs deeper, he learns some information that proves more dangerous than anticipated. This discovery puts him in grave danger, prompting a ruthless enemy to target him for elimination.
Mystery writer Charles Latimer meets Col.outcomes: Protagonist survives and manages to evade capture by antagonist. The conflict is unresolved as protagonist remains in danger from unknown forces.
Mystery writer Charles Latimer meets Col.theme: obsession; danger of knowledge; pursuit vs evasion

When Karyn Evans discovers her arrogantl.coa: Karyn discovers her husband is having an affair with a student. She retaliates by starting a relationship with a campus carpenter. Adam learns about Karyn's new relationship and becomes infuriated. The four decide to share a ski house in Vermont, despite their complicated situation.
When Karyn Evans discovers her arrogantl.outcomes: Protagonist achieves temporary satisfaction through revenge but ultimately resolves conflict through family reconciliation. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation as the family comes together to address their issues.

Alison and Jenny McGregor have been kidn.coa: A TV series featuring two women features them as main subjects. The women are kidnapped and a ransom demand is made for a large sum of money. Public donations swell the ransom coffers due to the women's appearance on the TV series, exceeding £6 million. The women's kidnapping sparks an outpouring of grief from the public.
Alison and Jenny McGregor have been kidn.outcomes: Protagonist is rescued and reunited with family. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation for McRae and Samantha, who survives the accident.
Alison and Jenny McGregor have been kidn.theme: resilience; public response to crisis; personal cost of heroism

After drinking a bit too much with two f.coa:
The protagonist drinks too much with two fellow civil servants.
He expounds on his desire to help those in lower social positions.
He attends a wedding celebration of one of his subordinates, Pseldonymov.
He decides to put his philosophy into action and disrupts the party by presenting himself unexpectedly.
After drinking a bit too much with two f.outcomes: Protagonist experiences public humiliation and failure in his attempt to apply kindness-based philosophy. The conflict is unresolved with no personal transformation or social change.
After drinking a bit too much with two f.theme: social awkwardness; class differences; failed idealism

Set in Cherbourg, France and in a social.coa: A 20-year-old woman's father recently dies. She sets out to bury her grief by having multiple lovers. Her boyfriend, a petty criminal, is unable to satisfy her constant desires. He introduces her to his friend, an auto mechanic, whose girlfriend refuses to have sex with him. The woman gives advice to her friends on how to pleasure men and women. Despite various encounters with her boyfriend, the auto mechanic, and even his girlfriend, she remains unsatisfied. She eventually discovers a new path to healing.
Set in Cherbourg, France and in a social.outcomes: Cecile finds a new way to cope with her grief and achieve inner peace. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and emotional growth.
Set in Cherbourg, France and in a social.theme: healing through self-discovery; the search for meaning in a post-crisis world; desire vs emptiness

The film follows the friends of a recent.coa: A minor painter dies and is scheduled to be buried in Limoges. Friends take a train from Paris to attend the funeral.
