The train journey begins with passengers listening to taped conversations about the deceased. Meanwhile, a car carrying the coffin travels recklessly alongside the train.
The film follows the friends of a recent.outcomes: Protagonist group is left grappling with the revelation of Emmerich's secret child and its implications on their relationships. The conflict is unresolved, with underlying tensions and secrets remaining.
The film follows the friends of a recent.theme: identity; family dynamics; acceptance vs rejection

Revak is an Iberian prince from Penda, a.coa: The Carthaginian fleet pillages Penda, enslaving the surviving native men, including one prince. The enslaved prince is transported aboard a galley, where he experiences an eventful passage. He eventually arrives in Carthage and becomes an elephant driver. He catches the attention of local noblewomen and a virtuous Roman captive named Valeria.
Revak is an Iberian prince from Penda, a.outcomes: Protagonist achieves alliance with Rome and secures means for revenge against Carthage. The conflict is unresolved as protagonist's ultimate goal remains unfulfilled.
Revak is an Iberian prince from Penda, a.theme: revenge; loyalty vs duty; survival under oppression

Mohsen Taheri (Navíd Akhavan), son of a.coa:
A father falls ill, forcing his son to travel to East Germany to buy sheep.
The son arrives in East Germany and meets a woman who is a former shot-putter and a vegetarian.
He tells the villagers that he comes from a Persian textile dynasty, which sparks their interest in reviving their own textile industry.
The villagers' hopes for Mohsen's supposed expertise make him increasingly popular.
Mohsen Taheri (Navíd Akhavan), son of a.outcomes: Protagonist is forced to confront his deception and reconcile with his family. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and a re-evaluation of priorities.

During World War I, a seemingly respecta.coa:
A middle-aged man devises an ingenious means to obtain money to supplement his dwindling income.
He adopts various assumed names and lures middle-class women to his villa just outside Paris.
He kills the women and burns their bodies, then helps himself to their bank accounts.
The murders continue until he has killed ten women and one boy. 
He is finally captured by authorities.
He is placed before a court of law.
During World War I, a seemingly respecta.outcomes: Protagonist is captured and brought to justice, losing freedom and reputation. The conflict is resolved with the protagonist's punishment but broader societal issues remain.

Bernard Barthélémy, owner of a BMW car d.coa:
A man owns a BMW car dealership and is married to an exceptionally beautiful woman.
He falls in love with a plain-looking but warm-hearted temporary secretary who works at his dealership.
The temporary secretary starts working at the dealership.
The man becomes infatuated with the temporary secretary.
Bernard Barthélémy, owner of a BMW car d.outcomes: Protagonist's life is transformed by his affair and he must confront the consequences of his actions. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal turmoil.

The story itself takes place in 1992. Th.coa: In 1992, incumbent president's power is threatened by African majority. A female Methodist real-estate agent goes missing outside Ystad. Detective Chief Inspector investigates her disappearance and finds her body. The crime scene also yields a severed black finger.
The story itself takes place in 1992. Th.outcomes: Protagonist (Detective Chief Inspector Wallander) successfully solves the case, preventing the assassination of President de Klerk. The conflict is resolved with averted catastrophe and maintained social order.
The story itself takes place in 1992. Th.theme: justice vs extremism; power struggles; racial tensions

Caine, a gunrunner, becomes stranded in.coa: A gunrunner becomes stranded in a small port on the Red Sea. He meets an attractive woman who propositions him to dive into shark-infested waters off the coast, claiming it's for scientific research. The woman and her partner are actually treasure hunters seeking to raid a wreck, which they believe is located near where the diving will take place. The gunrunner eventually discovers their true intentions.
Caine, a gunrunner, becomes stranded in.outcomes: Protagonist successfully retrieves treasure, compensating for his losses. The conflict is resolved with material gain but broader questions about morality remain.
Caine, a gunrunner, becomes stranded in.theme: opportunism; moral compromise; survival vs principle

Starting with the birth of the Jesus chi.coa:
The Virgin Mary gives birth to twins.
Only one of the twins is portrayed in the story.
The dark agents (the Four Evangelists) are introduced and begin to shadow the life of the One twin.
The One twin develops a fondness for drinking blood at the Last Supper.
Starting with the birth of the Jesus chi.outcomes: Jesus survives and becomes immortal as a vampire. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation but broader implications of his new nature remain.
Starting with the birth of the Jesus chi.theme: divine nature; free will vs destiny; the struggle for power

The story is about a businessman who is.coa:
The businessman becomes bored in his day-to-day affairs at the office.
He decides to contact some of his customers personally.
He meets one of them, an old man, at the old man's house.
He notices how frail the old man has become.
The story is about a businessman who is.outcomes: Protagonist fails to achieve business success with N but gains insight into his own weaknesses. The conflict is unresolved, with the protagonist's personal issues remaining unaddressed.
The story is about a businessman who is.theme: aging and mortality; loneliness vs connection; self-discovery

Rose-Marie Lemaitre is a French-Canadian.coa:
A father is killed.
Rose-Marie is raised by a mountie uncle.
She falls in love with a handsome criminal hiding in the mountains.
Her uncle discovers she's involved with the criminal, leading to conflict.
Rose-Marie Lemaitre is a French-Canadian.outcomes: Protagonist is forced to confront the consequences of her choices, leading to a resolution that prioritizes her duty over personal relationships. The conflict is unresolved with partial resolution and ongoing tension.
Rose-Marie Lemaitre is a French-Canadian.theme: loyalty vs duty; love vs loyalty; moral ambiguity

Carroll Baker plays a dual role in this.coa:
Julie receives threats from mysterious men while in Holland.
One of the men attempts to kidnap Julie while wearing a gorilla mask.
The men are confusing Julie with her twin sister Mary, who has stolen a massive diamond and betrayed her husband Craig.
Mary's actions cause the men to target Julie instead.
Carroll Baker plays a dual role in this.outcomes: Protagonist (Julie) escapes immediate danger but the mystery remains unsolved. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing threats to Julie's safety.

Marguerite Muir is a dentist, single and.coa:
A wallet is discarded on the street and picked up by Georges, who turns it in to the police station.
The police return the wallet to Marguerite, whose handbag was stolen earlier.
Georges allows himself to imagine a romantic encounter with Marguerite after returning her wallet.
Marguerite initially rejects Georges's advances but later becomes drawn to him.
Marguerite Muir is a dentist, single and.outcomes: Protagonist achieves romantic connection and shared experience with Georges. The conflict is unresolved with an enigmatic question left unanswered.
Marguerite Muir is a dentist, single and.theme: chance encounters; love vs expectation; uncertainty in relationships

Howard Nightingale (Douglas), a U.S. mar.coa: A U.S. marshal leads an elite uniformed posse to track down and capture Jack Strawhorn, the infamous train robber. The marshal captures Strawhorn, which appears to secure his election as a U.S. Senator. However, Strawhorn escapes from custody and kidnaps the marshal in the process. He demands a ransom of $40,000 for the marshal's safe return.
Howard Nightingale (Douglas), a U.S. mar.outcomes: Protagonist is kidnapped but ultimately rescued. The conflict is unresolved with a shift in power dynamics between protagonist and his posse.
Howard Nightingale (Douglas), a U.S. mar.theme: loyalty vs duty; corruption of power; personal sacrifice

The mighty Ursus is given a potion to dr.coa: A potion is given to Ursus, causing him to transform into a murderous werewolf on certain nights. He kills several innocent people in the forest while in this state. The villagers become aware of the killings and begin searching for the creature responsible. They eventually target Ursus as the one they seek to destroy.
The mighty Ursus is given a potion to dr.outcomes: Protagonist (Ursus) survives and achieves self-awareness of his condition. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader threats remain.

A young adult collie is taken in by Kath.coa:
A young adult collie is separated from his mother as a pup and grows up in a forest.
The collie is taken in by Kathie Merrick and she names him Bill.
Bill begins working as a sheepdog under the supervision of Mr. MacBain, but is later hit by a truck.
Bill is sent to an animal hospital where he is conscripted as a war dog for the Aleutian Islands Campaign.
A young adult collie is taken in by Kath.outcomes: Protagonist's aggressive behavior is resolved through recognition of war hero status, allowing protagonist to return to ally's care. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader issues remain.

The Valley is about four prospectors who.coa: Four prospectors enter a valley, unaware that it's a rift in the time/space continuum. One prospector gets taken away by a harpy, causing chaos among the group. Another prospector falls off a cliff, leaving two remaining members to fend for themselves. The two survivors must fight and destroy a cyclops that appears.
The Valley is about four prospectors who.outcomes: Protagonists survive but are stranded in post-apocalyptic world. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing threat from mythical beasts.
The Valley is about four prospectors who.theme: disorientation; loss of control; survival in the unknown

A group of friends are marooned on an un.coa:
A sailboat crashes on an uncharted island, leaving a group of friends stranded. The group is killed off by ancient humanoid creatures resembling humans and baboons. One person escapes capture and flees through the jungle, covering themselves in multiple plants' sap. They are cornered by the Alpha of the pack but manage to elude it by standing still while the plant sap obscures their scent.

The creatures raided the group's camp and stole their only life raft. The sole survivor covers their entire body in the sap and sneaks into the lair, where they set off all their remaining flares when unable to remain hidden. They make an escape but are caught by the Alpha in a clearing.
A group of friends are marooned on an un.outcomes: Protagonist survives and assumes leadership of the humanoid creatures. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social reintegration.
A group of friends are marooned on an un.theme: survival under adversity; leadership and power dynamics; human resilience

Upon returning home from work on his bir.coa:
Steve returns home from work on his birthday to find the house dark and empty.
He notices a chair, TV, and video tape set out for him to watch.
He turns on the TV and VCR, and begins watching the tape made by Alexandra.
The tape starts with a clip of Alexandra and their children wishing Steve a happy birthday.
Upon returning home from work on his bir.outcomes: Protagonist is emotionally devastated by his wife's betrayal. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation and relational damage.
Upon returning home from work on his bir.theme: marital deception; emotional manipulation; trust vs control

François Perrin is a belligerent factory.coa: Perrin pushes Berthier during a training session, leading to demands for Perrin's expulsion from the team. Shortly after, Perrin is fired from his job at the factory owned by the same businessman who owns the football club. The town turns against Perrin, and he is prohibited from entering the local bar. A drunk Berthier tries to rape a woman one night, but Perrin is immediately framed for the crime and brutalized by the police before being jailed.
François Perrin is a belligerent factory.outcomes: Protagonist achieves redemption and social reintegration through sports success. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and justice served.
François Perrin is a belligerent factory.theme: redemption; social stigma; justice vs prejudice

Rasputin's success as mystical healer in.coa:
A mystical healer gains popularity in a small village through their supposed healing abilities.
Their success attracts the attention of authorities who bring them to St. Petersburg.
In St. Petersburg, they use their charm and mysticism to gain influence over the ruling family's gravely ill son.
The ruler's gratitude for the healer's success with the son leads to increased trust and reliance on the healer.

A group of aristocrats, resentful of the healer's growing power and influence, begin to plot against them.
Their womanizing behavior is exposed, further fueling resentment among the aristocracy.
The ruling family continues to rely on the healer for guidance, despite their questionable character.
The aristocratic plotters become more determined to eliminate the healer due to their perceived threat to the ruler's power.
Rasputin's success as mystical healer in.outcomes: Protagonist is ultimately murdered by a group of conspirators. The conflict is unresolved with lasting impact on the royal family and Russian politics.
Rasputin's success as mystical healer in.theme: corruption; abuse of power; influence vs loyalty

Struggling composer Franz von Suppé admi.coa: A struggling composer admires the works of a famous composer and wants to emulate him. The owner of the theatre where he works is uninterested in his talents and only wants to stage the admired composer's work. With encouragement from a singer, he manages to turn out several hit operettas. His success leads to marriage with the singer who had encouraged him.
Struggling composer Franz von Suppé admi.outcomes: Protagonist achieves artistic success through perseverance and support. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation, but external threats remain.
Struggling composer Franz von Suppé admi.theme: artistic ambition; loyalty vs distraction; love vs temptation

A king has three sons: the eldest and th.coa: A king casts three feathers into the air, and when they land, each of his children should follow to see where it leads. The youngest child follows one feather that has landed in a lake or pond where a toad lives that speaks in rhyme. The king asks for marvellous gifts - impossibly difficult to find, or make - for his sons to show him: a carpet and a ring of extraordinary splendour as a second gift. Simpleton manages to produce the items in front of his father.
A king has three sons: the eldest and th.outcomes: Protagonist (Simpleton) succeeds in completing final task and wins the hand of a princess, securing his inheritance of the kingdom. The conflict is resolved with personal success and social elevation.
A king has three sons: the eldest and th.theme: cleverness over brawn; humility rewarded; love conquers adversity

Twin sisters, Jenny and Sally travel the.coa: The twin sisters travel with their grandfather's medicine show, performing horse riding and sharp-shooting skills. Their grandfather has a gambling problem that eventually leads to his demise during a poker game. He is accidentally shot and killed while holding the winning hand, causing him to die in a state of triumph. The twins inherit the ranch won by their grandfather as a result of his win.
Twin sisters, Jenny and Sally travel the.outcomes: Twin sisters successfully defend their inherited ranch against external threats. The conflict is resolved with the help of allies, but underlying issues remain.

Ralph Milan is a contract killer who is.coa:
A contract killer is paid to kill a witness whose testimony could harm an organisation.
The contract killer waits in a hotel room for the witness, but is interrupted by a suicidal neighbour who tries to hang himself on the waterpipes.
The neighbour's attempt causes a flood and attracts unwanted attention from the police if he tries again.
The contract killer offers to talk the neighbour out of killing himself until after his own task is complete.
Ralph Milan is a contract killer who is.outcomes: Protagonist fails to complete contract killing due to neighbour's interference. The conflict is unresolved with no resolution or change.
Ralph Milan is a contract killer who is.theme: chaos vs order; human connection; moral ambiguity

A frustrated Dagwood resigns his office.coa:
Dagwood resigns his office job.
Blondie takes over Dagwood's position at work.
Dagwood leaves their toddler unattended at home to go on a fishing trip with a friend and two attractive young women.
The group returns from the fishing trip, causing Dagwood to be uneasy due to the presence of the two women.
Blondie's sister and her boyfriend arrive at their home to stay for a jitterbug championship at Mr. Dithers' nightclub.
Dagwood substitutes in the dance contest after his sister-in-law's boyfriend backs out.
Dagwood performs amazing acrobatic dance steps, impressing everyone.
Blondie sees pictures of the fishing trip and packs her bags to leave Dagwood due to jealousy.
Blondie changes her mind about leaving Dagwood.
The couple neglects to buy Mr. Dithers' nightclub as part of a building project.
A frustrated Dagwood resigns his office.outcomes: Protagonist (Dagwood) survives chaotic situation without major consequences. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation, as Dagwood adapts to new circumstances and finds success in dance contest.

Grethe Koblanck and her husband own a su.coa:
The owner of a successful fur fashion house suspects her husband of flirting with younger women, including one from South America.
She employs a hidden camera to keep an eye on him.
He discovers the camera and gets a doppelganger to take his place.
The double also becomes interested in attractive women.
Grethe Koblanck and her husband own a su.outcomes: Protagonist's marriage is exposed as troubled but ultimately intact. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.
Grethe Koblanck and her husband own a su.theme: deception; identity crisis; love vs loyalty

Theo and Gustav Benthin are two brothers.coa:
Theo illegally transfers goods to Gustav across the border.
Gustav sells the goods in West Germany.
The People's Police catch Theo, disrupting the smuggling operation.
Gustav's business collapses due to lack of cheap merchandise from the East.
Theo and Gustav Benthin are two brothers.outcomes: Protagonist's smuggling operation is dismantled, but protagonist survives with a new job as a steel worker. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation for some characters, but broader economic and social issues persist.
Theo and Gustav Benthin are two brothers.theme: capitalism vs socialism; loyalty and family ties; adaptation to changing circumstances

After graduating from the École national.coa: Graduates from the École nationale d'administration. Lands a job as speechwriter in the Foreign Ministry. Existing senior advisers do not welcome the newcomer due to potential competition. The Minister recognizes the newcomer's abilities, as does Maupas, the career official heading the department.
After graduating from the École national.outcomes: Protagonist successfully delivers speech on behalf of Minister, contradicting US claims and arguing for disarmament of Iraq. The conflict is resolved with a clear expression of France's position in the international community.
After graduating from the École national.theme: talent vs bureaucracy; loyalty vs ambition; effective communication under pressure

The film begins with Confucius as an old.coa: The film begins with an old man thinking back. The old man is shown in his early 50s, being promoted from Mayor to Minister for Law in a state called Lu. This promotion leads to him confronting ethical issues after saving a slave-boy who was due to be buried alive with his former master. The confrontation sparks complex politics and war.
The film begins with Confucius as an old.outcomes: Protagonist (Confucius) achieves lasting impact through his written work, securing his legacy despite personal setbacks. The conflict is resolved with a mix of personal transformation and systemic change.

Vitus, played by Teo Gheorghiu, is a hig.coa: At 12 years old, a highly gifted pianist rebels against over-protective parents by seeking refuge with his grandfather. The grandfather loves flying and has a relationship with the protagonist that is different from their parents'. The protagonist fakes a head injury to gain freedom and secretly amasses a fortune on the stock market. The accumulated wealth allows the grandfather to purchase an aircraft, and the father returns triumphantly to his former company after being fired.
Vitus, played by Teo Gheorghiu, is a hig.outcomes: Protagonist achieves success as a pianist and resolves family conflicts through financial stability. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and professional fulfillment.
Vitus, played by Teo Gheorghiu, is a hig.theme: rebellion vs responsibility; pursuing one's passion; coming of age

When he was a newborn baby, Edith Guetz.coa: A newborn baby is told by their mother that they can stay at home forever if they want to. 28 years pass and the son grows into a successful university teacher of Asian languages who lives in his parents' home despite being an adult. The father longs for his son to leave the nest, a desire shared by his wife. The parents decide to make their son's life at home miserable in order to encourage him to move out.
When he was a newborn baby, Edith Guetz.outcomes: Protagonist continues to live in his parents' home despite their efforts to push him out, indicating a failure of parental influence. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.
When he was a newborn baby, Edith Guetz.theme: dependence on family; adult children living at home; generational conflict

A woman's mistakes come back to haunt he.coa:
A woman is attacked by one of her patients. She has an inoperable brain tumor that's growing at an alarming rate. Her marriage is in bad shape. She needs a weekend away from the city, so her husband sets up a short holiday at a remote cabin for her and their sister to join him while he edits his book.

The woman watches sparks begin to fly between her sister and her husband. Harlan Pyne and Adrian break into the cabin and shoot the woman's pet dog.
A woman's mistakes come back to haunt he.outcomes: Protagonist survives the ordeal but is left traumatized and questioning her own sanity. The conflict is unresolved as broader threats remain from escaped convict Harlan Pyne.
A woman's mistakes come back to haunt he.theme: trauma; revenge; blurred lines between reality and madness

Andrei Dubrovsky is an old poor nobleman.coa: A landowner's property is confiscated by a wealthy aristocrat. The son of the landowner seeks revenge and justice by gathering a group of serfs to steal from the rich and give to the poor. This banditry attracts attention and causes concern among the local population, particularly those who are targeted by the thefts. As a result, the son becomes notorious in the area.
Andrei Dubrovsky is an old poor nobleman.outcomes: Protagonist fails to achieve revenge or justice for his father. The conflict is unresolved with tragic consequences.
Andrei Dubrovsky is an old poor nobleman.theme: revenge vs morality; class struggle; love as weakness

In San Juan, Puerto Rico, Madison Taylor.coa:
Madison is caught in the crossfire between two corrupt cops and gets injured.
The corrupt cops try to finish the job, but Madison wakes up in a hospital where she's placed under protection as a witness.
Pierce and Tull realize Madison is key to tracing them back to their crime and show up at the hospital to kill her.
Madison calls for help from Wakes when Pierce and Tull arrive.
In San Juan, Puerto Rico, Madison Taylor.outcomes: Protagonist survives the night and escapes immediate danger. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation as protagonist uses resourcefulness to stay alive.
In San Juan, Puerto Rico, Madison Taylor.theme: survival under adversity; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Karen McCoy is released from prison with.coa:
Karen is released from prison with nothing but the clothes on her back.
She wishes for a quiet life after being a notorious bank robber before her incarceration.
A dirty parole officer tries to extort money from her, making it difficult for her to start anew.
The parole officer's actions are complicated by old business partners who also want something from Karen.
Karen McCoy is released from prison with.outcomes: Protagonist successfully completes the heist but faces uncertain consequences for herself and others. The conflict is unresolved with potential long-term repercussions.
Karen McCoy is released from prison with.theme: reform vs relapse; loyalty under pressure; survival through crime

Two brothers Michael (Brandon Douglas) a.coa:
Two brothers and a teenaged Native American girl and her brother go on vacation from Chicago to Spirit Island.
They plan to save Spirit Island to preserve their heritage.
A conflict arises regarding the development of tribal burial grounds.
The land developers, who seek to transform Spirit Island into a vacation resort, are opposed by the group.
Two brothers Michael (Brandon Douglas) a.outcomes: Protagonist achieves preservation of ancestral burial ground through community-led resistance. The conflict is resolved with social change and protection of cultural heritage.
Two brothers Michael (Brandon Douglas) a.theme: cultural preservation; indigenous rights; community vs development

In 1941, the Polish town Kielce is occup.coa:
The Nazis occupy the Polish town in 1941.
A Polish family's child dies, and they take in another child from a prisoner before being sent to a concentration camp.
The war ends, and the former prisoner returns to his hometown seeking his daughter.
He finds that she has grown up with the Polish family, unaware of her true parents.
In 1941, the Polish town Kielce is occup.outcomes: Protagonist is reunited with his daughter but faces a difficult choice between claiming her or respecting her new life. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation and relational change.

Paul Manning discovers one day that his.coa:
A neighbor is caught cheating on their spouse by a friend who asks about it.
The friend shares stories of men who have successfully committed adultery, including tactics they use.
While listening to these stories, the friend becomes aware of an attractive woman living nearby.
The friend starts to get close to cheating on their own spouse, but doesn't follow through.
Paul Manning discovers one day that his.outcomes: Protagonist maintains marriage and avoids infidelity. The conflict is resolved with personal restraint and avoidance of temptation.
Paul Manning discovers one day that his.theme: temptation vs morality; fidelity in relationships; the dangers of peer influence

The grandma's boy is a timid coward who.coa:
The timid boy is afraid of wooing his girl due to fear of his rival.
His loving grandma gives him an object that she claims has magical powers from the Civil War era.
He uses this object, which he believes gives him courage, and captures a town criminal.
With newfound confidence, he wins the girl's heart.
The grandma's boy is a timid coward who.outcomes: Protagonist successfully woos his love and overcomes his fears. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social reintegration.

The story revolves around seven-year-old.coa:
Barbro discovers she has a secret twin sister named Ylva-li, who calls her Most Beloved Sister.
Ylva-li tells Barbro that she will die when the roses on Salikon wither.
Barbro refuses to believe Ylva-li and returns to her parents.
The next day, the roses on Salikon are dead.
The story revolves around seven-year-old.outcomes: Protagonist (Barbro) experiences loss and separation from twin sister Ylva-li. The conflict is unresolved as the magical world is lost, but Barbro's relationship with her parents is superficially restored.
The story revolves around seven-year-old.theme: loss of innocence; sibling bond; impermanence

Bruno, 20, and Sonia, 18, are surviving.coa:
Sonia becomes pregnant.
Bruno sells their baby to a black market adoption ring while Sonia is absent.
He tells Sonia about the sale, saying they can "make" another baby, but she faints from shock and disgust.
Sonia presses charges against Bruno for selling their child.
Bruno, 20, and Sonia, 18, are surviving.outcomes: Protagonist is incarcerated, facing the consequences of his actions. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation but broader threats remain.

The son of the Countess Mensdorf runs aw.coa:
The son runs away from home due to his mother's relationship with the Baron Von Mallock.
He becomes a trapeze artist and adopts the stage name Frattani after many years abroad.
Frattani returns home and meets Madeleine, a young dancer.
They fall in love and he wants to leave the circus for a normal life.
The son of the Countess Mensdorf runs aw.outcomes: Protagonist achieves escape from abusive relationship and finds new love. The conflict is resolved with the removal of the antagonist (Baron) through his own actions.
The son of the Countess Mensdorf runs aw.theme: escape from abuse; identity transformation; love vs adversity

The film is told in a flashback format w.coa: A child lives in a remote Greek village called Lia in the 1940s. The village is affected by the Greek Civil War between the government forces and the communist guerrillas of the Democratic Army of Greece. Communist guerrillas arrive at the village, leading to violence and chaos. A mother is killed by the communist guerrillas.
The film is told in a flashback format w.outcomes: Protagonist uncovers truth and finds closure about his mother's death. The conflict is resolved with personal catharsis but broader historical context remains unchanged.
The film is told in a flashback format w.theme: trauma; loss of innocence; impact of war

France in 1885: Eugénie worked as a chef.coa:
Eugénie worked as a chef for the famous restaurant owner for 20 years.
Over the years, feelings arose between Eugénie and the restaurant owner because they spent a lot of time in the kitchen together.
Their shared love of food led to the creation of unique, delicious, and exquisite dishes that attracted many diners from all over the world.
The restaurant owner decided to cook for Eugénie for the first time.
France in 1885: Eugénie worked as a chef.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a balance between love and career, maintaining her independence. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation through mutual understanding and compromise.
France in 1885: Eugénie worked as a chef.theme: love vs duty; personal growth; professional passion

An erotic film from photographer David H.coa:
A group of three young girls are shipwrecked on a remote Mediterranean island.
The girls survive and begin to explore their new surroundings.
They meet and interact with local boys, leading to romantic relationships for two of the girls.
One girl becomes involved with the husband of a beautiful pianist who lives on the island.
An erotic film from photographer David H.outcomes: The protagonists navigate their newfound freedom and relationships, leading to personal growth and self-discovery. The conflict is resolved with partial resolution and transformation.
An erotic film from photographer David H.theme: self-discovery; female empowerment; coming of age

During Bastille Day when most people are.coa: A group of thieves prepare to commit a warehouse robbery at an industrial park on Bastille Day. Meanwhile, a special agent leads an international team escorting the captured leader of the Albanian mafia, who is due in court. The mafia's henchmen set up an ambush and the special agent escapes with the captive. They take refuge in the warehouse being robbed by the group of thieves.
During Bastille Day when most people are.outcomes: Protagonist (Laborie) survives and manages to contain the situation. The conflict is unresolved with multiple parties still engaged in combat.
During Bastille Day when most people are.theme: chaos; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

The film depicts a bourgeois Italian fam.coa: A bourgeois Italian family lives in an apartment that was bought by the grandfather. Two world wars occur, causing significant changes to the family's life. The economic boom follows one of the wars, bringing about new opportunities for the family. The patriarch of the family is a retired professor who has lived through these events and now narrates them.
The film depicts a bourgeois Italian fam.outcomes: The protagonist comes to terms with the passing of time and the decline of his family's influence. The conflict is resolved with a sense of acceptance and appreciation for the human experience.
The film depicts a bourgeois Italian fam.theme: family legacy; nostalgia; the passage of time

Marcos, a rich engineer, discovers on hi.coa: A rich engineer murders his bride on their wedding night. The murder case becomes a sensation in the media, leading to Marcos' acquittal. Marcos moves to Guaraíba to start anew and finds employment managing the construction of a factory. He meets Décio, who lives with his paralyzed mother and adoptive sister Sônia.
Marcos, a rich engineer, discovers on hi.outcomes: Protagonist escapes punishment and achieves a new relationship. The conflict is unresolved with the protagonist's actions causing further harm.
Marcos, a rich engineer, discovers on hi.theme: morality; love vs guilt; consequences of violence

The patriarch (François Marthouret) of a.coa:
The patriarch returns home with a small white rat.
The animal's presence has an adverse effect on the family members, causing them to act out their darkest desires.
The son loudly announces his homosexuality and begins throwing wild orgies, while the daughter deliberately flirts with death and practices sadomasochism on her boyfriend.
The mother seduces her son in an attempt to "cure" him of his orientation.
The father eventually kills and devours the offending rat.
After consuming the rat's remains, he transforms into a white rat himself.
The family discovers their father's transformation and band together to brutally slay him.
The patriarch (François Marthouret) of a.outcomes: The patriarch is killed by his own family. The conflict is resolved with a violent confrontation.
The patriarch (François Marthouret) of a.theme: corruption; influence of evil; family dynamics

The plot is loosely based on the origina.coa: A bank robbery occurs by two masked gunmen. The Tojo Clan loses ten billion yen as a result of the robbery. Meanwhile, a search for Mizuki begins in Kamurocho. A convenience store meeting between Kiryu, Haruka, Majima's men, and Poppo employee Satoru takes place.
The plot is loosely based on the origina.outcomes: Protagonist (Kiryu) survives and resolves the immediate conflict with his childhood friend turned rival. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader threats remain.
The plot is loosely based on the origina.theme: loyalty vs duty; redemption under adversity; human resilience

1816. After the Battle of Waterloo, Loui.coa: The Battle of Waterloo ends, restoring Louis XVIII to power. De Rochefort sets sail for Senegal on the frigate Méduse with the future governor and his wife on board. The voyage becomes tense due to hatred and mistrust among passengers. The tension escalates between Captain Chaumareys and Lieutenant Coudein.
1816. After the Battle of Waterloo, Loui.outcomes: The protagonist survives the shipwreck but the conflict is unresolved with broader consequences for those on board. The conflict is unresolved with systemic implications for governance and leadership.

As described in a review in a film magaz.coa:
Castro arranges the marriage between his son and Julietta, and she travels from her family's home for the wedding.
The wedding occurs, but on the same night, El Tigre loots the estate with Carlotta's help.
Carlotta wears Julietta's mantilla while embracing El Tigre, leading the Don to believe Julietta is unfaithful.
Disgusted by this perceived infidelity, the Don seeks revenge against El Tigre.
As described in a review in a film magaz.outcomes: Protagonist is disillusioned with women but finds redemption through love. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation, but broader issues of jealousy and deception remain.
As described in a review in a film magaz.theme: redemption; jealousy vs love; human fallibility

A baby is abandoned at the United Nation.coa:
A baby is abandoned at the United Nations headquarters by a mother who heard a radio show about children's rights.
The baby is found by a UN guide, who brings it to the security office because its nationality is unknown due to being in international territory.
Because of this situation, a proud bachelor is forced to take care of the baby until a proper home can be found.
He smuggles the child into his apartment after his landlord forbids kids.
A baby is abandoned at the United Nation.outcomes: Protagonist successfully adopts child and forms loving relationship with authority. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and relational change.
A baby is abandoned at the United Nation.theme: love conquers adversity; children's rights; redemption through service

Vollmöller's play wordlessly tells the s.coa: A wayward nun deserts her convent with a knight. The music of an evil minstrel influences their actions. A statue of the Virgin Mary comes to life and takes the physical place of the nun. The nun, now in disguise as the statue, navigates the world and faces various challenges. She is eventually accused of witchcraft but manages to escape. The nun returns to her convent with a dying infant.
Vollmöller's play wordlessly tells the s.outcomes: Protagonist is forgiven by authority and reconciled with her community. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and redemption.
Vollmöller's play wordlessly tells the s.theme: redemption; identity crisis; faith vs temptation

Master thief Thomas Taylor (Slater) is r.coa:
A master thief is released on parole.
The thief starts working as a Paramedic.
An emergency call is made to the betting office where two armed robbers are making their stand.
One of the robbers gets shot during the confrontation.
The female thief is injured and needs help escaping with the money.
The thief helps the female thief escape from the scene, hiding the stolen cash in a stretcher.
An FBI agent follows them, tracking the marked money.
Master thief Thomas Taylor (Slater) is r.outcomes: Protagonist successfully escapes with the stolen money. The conflict is unresolved as the protagonist's past continues to haunt them.
Master thief Thomas Taylor (Slater) is r.theme: crime and redemption; loyalty vs duty; moral ambiguity

Yvonne Valbret is a Parisian kept woman.coa: A young student of foreign diplomacy falls in love with an artist's model who poses as an artist. The student learns about the model's past and multiple lovers, leading him to leave her. Years later, they cross paths again, and the student pays for the model to live in his country cottage outside Paris, where they engage in a platonic relationship.
Yvonne Valbret is a Parisian kept woman.outcomes: Protagonist Yvonne is left heartbroken and alone after choosing her old lover. The conflict is unresolved with a focus on personal transformation as Yvonne prioritizes love over societal expectations.
Yvonne Valbret is a Parisian kept woman.theme: love vs societal expectations; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Katya, a poor and desperate widow, and h.coa: A poor widow and her young son try to survive in a post-World War II Soviet Union setting during the early 1950s. They meet a handsome officer on a train, who seduces the mother. The officer pretends to be the mother's husband and acts as a stepfather to the child, who is initially distrustful of him.
Katya, a poor and desperate widow, and h.outcomes: Protagonist is trapped in an abusive relationship with no clear escape. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal and relational harm.
Katya, a poor and desperate widow, and h.theme: deception; exploitation of vulnerability; toxic relationships

The film is set in the 1920s. Peter Brak.coa:
A sculptor creates a neoclassical-style statue of Venus in the 1920s.
The sculptor buries the statue in a Bavarian field to protect the identity of the woman who modelled for it.
The buried statue is later dug up by unknown individuals.
Experts believe that the statue, now known as 'Venus vom Acker', was created by Praxiteles.
The film is set in the 1920s. Peter Brak.outcomes: Protagonist fails to prove authorship but ultimately achieves recognition for his work. The conflict is resolved with partial resolution, as the protagonist's artistic integrity is acknowledged, but the model's reputation remains intact.

New York City janitor Daryll Deever is a.coa:
A wealthy Vietnamese man is murdered in an office building where a janitor works.
The reporter suspects the janitor knows something about the murder.
She keeps after him for information, which he allows because of his romantic interest in her.
This pursuit convinces the real killers that the janitor has vital information.
New York City janitor Daryll Deever is a.outcomes: Protagonist survives the ordeal with his life intact but shaken. The conflict is unresolved with a lingering threat to protagonist's safety.
New York City janitor Daryll Deever is a.theme: false accusations; danger of assumption; media scrutiny

In this comedic film, renowned journalis.coa: The renowned journalist travels extensively for work due to his job as a correspondent. His wife secures a divorce in Reno after becoming dissatisfied with his constant travel. Upon learning about the divorce while on assignment abroad, he becomes resolute in mending their relationship. He returns to the United States and discovers that she is now engaged to another man.
In this comedic film, renowned journalis.outcomes: Protagonist successfully rekindles romance with former wife. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation.
In this comedic film, renowned journalis.theme: reconciliation; second chances; love vs career

Diane is a lawyer who went through a div.coa:
A lawyer loses their phone.
The phone is found by a man who intends to return it.
He calls the owner and they have a conversation that goes well, leading them to arrange a date.
The two agree to meet in person for the first time.
Diane is a lawyer who went through a div.outcomes: Protagonist learns to let go of superficial expectations and develops an open-minded attitude towards relationships. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and a new perspective on love.
Diane is a lawyer who went through a div.theme: overcoming prejudice; letting go of expectations; inner transformation

The Art of Love is composed of several c.coa: Isabelle has not had sex in a year. She declines an offer from her friend Zoé to "borrow" her husband, instead deciding to impersonate Amélie. This decision is made after Isabelle realizes she cannot bring herself to sleep with Boris, who was offered to her by Zoé. Meanwhile, Achille thinks his prayers have been answered when a new neighbour knocks on his door wearing a negligee and suggests they have an affair.
The Art of Love is composed of several c.outcomes: Protagonist Isabelle resolves her sex life through impersonation and connection with Amélie. The conflict is unresolved as the couples face various relationship challenges.
The Art of Love is composed of several c.theme: infidelity; desire vs responsibility; love in relationships

Pilot John Dooley and the crew of a Worl.coa: A World War II-era Douglas C-47 Skytrain experiences icy conditions and is forced to execute an emergency landing on a frozen lake near the Quebec–Labrador border. The plane crash results in survivors who can only provide an approximate position to rescuers. Temperatures plummet to −70 °F (−57 °C), making survival difficult for the downed crew. The crew waits for rescue while struggling to stay alive in the extreme cold.
Pilot John Dooley and the crew of a Worl.outcomes: Protagonist and crew survive the extreme winter conditions but remain stranded until rescue. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing uncertainty about their fate.
Pilot John Dooley and the crew of a Worl.theme: survival under adversity; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Return Engagement tells the story of Lun.coa:
A Triad boss is sent to prison in Canada.
He is imprisoned while his daughter is taken to Hong Kong for safety reasons.
Upon his release from prison, the Triad boss travels to Hong Kong.
In Hong Kong, he meets a young Triad who knows of his reputation and respects him.
Return Engagement tells the story of Lun.outcomes: Protagonist reunites with his daughter, resolving the immediate conflict of separation. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation as protagonist reconnects with family.

During the night of 9 April 1940, the Da.coa: The Danish army is alerted that the German army has crossed the border on the night of 9 April 1940, putting Denmark at war with Europe's strongest army. The alert triggers an order for a Danish bicycle infantry company and motorcycle platoon to hold off the German advance in South Jutland until reinforcements can arrive. However, this unit is quickly overwhelmed by superior German forces. They are forced to retreat from their initial position.
During the night of 9 April 1940, the Da.outcomes: Protagonist's unit suffers defeat, but Second Lieutenant Sand survives and leads his men to safety. The conflict is unresolved with the Danish army struggling to hold back the German advance.

A magician named Montag the Magnificent.coa: A magician puts on elaborate magic shows in Los Angeles, featuring tricks where beautiful young women appear to die in brutal torturous fashions. The women appear alive and unharmed at the end of each trick. Later, it is discovered that some of these women are found dead with the same wounds inflicted during the tricks. Ed Bigelow, a journalist, becomes aware of the deaths and starts investigating.
A magician named Montag the Magnificent.outcomes: Protagonist is exposed and brought to justice, but the full extent of his actions remains unclear. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing investigation and potential personal consequences for protagonist.

Two swindlers get to prison for alcohol.coa:
Two swindlers get to prison for alcohol theft.
After serving their sentence, the two swindlers are released from prison.
They find employment in a lab where they work on inventing a device for regeneration of old cows.
The device is completed and tested successfully, with rejuvenated results on an old cow.

The owner of a salon becomes interested in using the device for human rejuvenation, not just animal.
The swindlers are approached by the salon owner to adapt the device for human use.
They agree to make the necessary modifications to the device.
However, something goes wrong during the modification process.
Two swindlers get to prison for alcohol.outcomes: Protagonists undergo physical transformation from adults back to children. The conflict is unresolved with unintended consequences and new challenges.
Two swindlers get to prison for alcohol.theme: unintended consequences; playing god; identity crisis

Autumn 1944 – The Third Reich is near to.coa: The Third Reich is near collapse in Autumn 1944. Allied soldiers arrive on German soil. The Nazis plan a propaganda film called Das Leben geht weiter to show how Germans cope with destruction and horror. Cameras start rolling for the film just outside Berlin.
Autumn 1944 – The Third Reich is near to.outcomes: The film is completed but its premiere is delayed due to the war's end. The conflict is unresolved with the Nazi regime collapsing.
Autumn 1944 – The Third Reich is near to.theme: propaganda; resilience under oppression; futility of attempts to control narrative

Pietro Paladini and his brother Carlo, a.coa:
Pietro Paladini rescues two women from drowning.
At the same time, Pietro's wife dies unexpectedly at home.
The funeral takes place after her death.
Pietro falls into a state of Quiet Chaos and spends more time with his daughter Claudia.
Pietro Paladini and his brother Carlo, a.outcomes: Protagonist emerges reborn, having transitioned through personal loss to a new sense of self. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation.
Pietro Paladini and his brother Carlo, a.theme: grief and renewal; resilience in adversity; transformation from tough manager to compassionate father

In Paris Madame Vernier discovers that h.coa:
Madame Vernier discovers that her husband is having an affair with Lilette.
She seeks advice from Eva and decides to have a flirtation of her own.
She meets Marcel Douzet, a bookbinder, and uses a false name to conceal her identity, pretending to be a maid.
This leads to a situation where she has to pretend to be her own servant.
In Paris Madame Vernier discovers that h.outcomes: Protagonist resolves the situation by choosing to maintain her marriage. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation, but underlying issues remain.

The film is set at a United States Army.coa: The United States Army base in Kentucky is preparing for the end of 1944 during World War II. The protagonists are assigned to dull jobs, with one wanting to be transferred to active duty overseas but being refused. A talent show is announced, requiring the protagonists to rehearse a song-and-dance act. They also need to obtain passes to attend personal events, including seeing a wife and new baby.
The film is set at a United States Army.outcomes: Protagonists successfully participate in talent show and resolve personal issues. The conflict is resolved with a sense of accomplishment and camaraderie.
The film is set at a United States Army.theme: resilience under bureaucracy; creative expression as coping mechanism; loyalty vs duty

Former mercenary Yeo-hoon has reformed a.coa:
Yeo-hoon is framed for the death of a prominent CEO.
The framing leads to Yeo-hoon being pursued by unknown assailants.
Yeo-hoon escapes from his pursuers but takes a bullet and ends up in a hospital bed.
A medical resident, Tae-joon, helps Yeo-hoon escape from the hospital.
Former mercenary Yeo-hoon has reformed a.outcomes: Protagonist survives and achieves temporary safety with allies. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing danger and threats.
Former mercenary Yeo-hoon has reformed a.theme: survival under adversity; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

The film takes place in an unspecified W.coa: A fascist government attempts to suppress a socialist uprising. A revolutionary leader is imprisoned but escapes to the Soviet Union. In the Soviet Union, an engineer invents a "death ray" that can explode gunpowder and fuel mixtures at a distance. The device falls into the hands of a fascist intelligence agent who steals it and returns it to his country.
The film takes place in an unspecified W.outcomes: Protagonist (workers) successfully resist government oppression through self-defense. The conflict is resolved with a shift in power from oppressors to oppressed.
The film takes place in an unspecified W.theme: resistance vs oppression; technology as double-edged sword; class struggle

Victoria is a beautiful young woman who.coa:
Victoria begins to gain success as a jewelry designer in Los Angeles.
She meets Jack, a wealthy entrepreneur who is handsome and charming.
Jack seduces Victoria with his charm and sensuality, making her experience intense emotions and sensations.
Victoria becomes deeply involved with Jack, wanting to make him happy.
Victoria is a beautiful young woman who.outcomes: Protagonist is left emotionally drained but wiser about her own desires. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.
Victoria is a beautiful young woman who.theme: the dangers of excessive desire; the blurring of boundaries in relationships

The sons (and a daughter) of the origina.coa:
The original Four Musketeers are alerted to the Queen's situation.
They inform their sons and daughter about the Queen's predicament.
The sons and daughter rush to Court to help the Queen, who is being pressured by the Duc de Lavalle into a marriage with Princess Henriette.
The Duc de Lavalle reveals that he has manipulated the Queen into agreeing to the marriage.
The sons (and a daughter) of the origina.outcomes: The protagonists successfully rescue the Queen and prevent her forced marriage. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and romantic fulfillment for D'Artagnan Jr. and Claire.

The plot concerns the visit of a lieuten.coa:
A lieutenant visits the house of a Jewish owner of a vodka distillery to collect a debt owed to his cousin.
The lieutenant is enticed by the woman to supper and stays overnight, but she relieves him of the IOUs without paying.
Furious, Kryukov resolves to visit her and recover the debt himself.
Kryukov also spends the night with her and returns unpaid.
The plot concerns the visit of a lieuten.outcomes: Protagonist (Kryukov) is left with a sense of disillusionment and moral ambiguity. The conflict is unresolved as both Sokolsky and Kryukov are revealed to be equally flawed characters.
The plot concerns the visit of a lieuten.theme: moral ambiguity; deception; the corrupting influence of desire

In the late 1960s, a white preacher in A.coa:
A white preacher announces that the world is due to end soon because he has captured an emissary of the devil.
The "emissary" escapes from the preacher.
He contacts a local liberation leader, revealing his true identity as a Latin American revolutionary who supports the local liberation movement.
The revolutionary offers assistance to the liberation leader in their fight against Imperialism.
In the late 1960s, a white preacher in A.outcomes: Protagonist succeeds in infiltrating and assisting the local liberation movement. The conflict is resolved with partial resolution through liberation, but broader Imperialist threats remain.
In the late 1960s, a white preacher in A.theme: deception; imperialism vs liberation; faith vs reality

A lazy woman did not like to spin and wh.coa: The woman did not like to spin and would often leave the thread on the bobbin. Her husband complained about this, but she claimed that a reel was necessary for spinning. He decided to cut a reel, but she warned him that whoever cut it would die, causing him to hesitate. Despite her warning, he still expressed his dissatisfaction with her spinning methods.
A lazy woman did not like to spin and wh.outcomes: Protagonist's wife achieves her goal and husband stops complaining. The conflict is resolved with a shift in power dynamics.

Students Dieter and Sonja, who are a cou.coa:
Dieter dances with Siegi at a masquerade, mistaking her for his girlfriend.
He realizes later that she is not his girlfriend, but he cannot forget her.
Sonja becomes bitter and jealous upon learning of Dieter's feelings for Siegi.
She starts an affair with Edy, Siegi's boyfriend.
Students Dieter and Sonja, who are a cou.outcomes: Protagonist finds love with Siegi, while his original partner Sonja ends up with him instead of Edy. The conflict is resolved with a change in relationships but no deeper resolution.
Students Dieter and Sonja, who are a cou.theme: love vs deception; jealousy and anger; superficial relationships

Tai, a young man arrested on a crime cha.coa: A young man is arrested on a crime charge but is later discharged due to help from his twin brother. He finds his brother in a coma with severe injuries after being set free. His brother's girlfriend explains that he got involved in some risky business to raise money for the young man's case, causing him to feel guilty. The young man then starts tracing what happened to his brother, leading him into illegal basketball gambling.
Tai, a young man arrested on a crime cha.outcomes: Protagonist survives and uncovers the truth about his brother's injuries. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing danger and uncertainty.

Elliott is a twenty-year-old Danish fish.coa:
A twenty-year-old fisherman performs at a club one night as a favor to his friend.
He is noticed by a successful music manager.
The manager pairs him with her estranged daughter and music producer.
Elliott accepts their help, trying to write and record a song.
Elliott is a twenty-year-old Danish fish.outcomes: Protagonist becomes a star, achieving fame and success. The conflict is partially resolved with personal growth but unresolved issues from the past remain.
Elliott is a twenty-year-old Danish fish.theme: overcoming adversity; finding one's voice; love vs ambition

Roland, the narrator, begins the story a.coa: The narrator becomes an English professor years after the central action takes place. The narrator explains that he was a poor student and would often end up intoxicated on the streets of Berlin. His father sends him off to university in the country as a result of his struggles. This leads to the narrator becoming enamored with the intellectual, bewildering, and isolated world of his college professor.
Roland, the narrator, begins the story a.outcomes: Protagonist achieves academic success as an English professor, reflecting personal transformation through engagement with intellectual pursuits. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing struggle between desire for connection and fear of vulnerability.
Roland, the narrator, begins the story a.theme: love vs isolation; intellectualism vs emotional connection; identity formation

When Jane, a beautiful but troubled Amer.coa: A group of thugs attempts to rob a backpacker. The backpacker successfully fends off the thugs. This incident draws the attention of Shu, a female fighting champion. Shu recruits and trains the backpacker to fight in an underground martial arts tournament known as The Kumite.
When Jane, a beautiful but troubled Amer.outcomes: Protagonist (Jane) emerges as a top contender in The Kumite, but broader threats and forces remain. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation through martial arts training.

The film takes place decades after the F.coa: A magician and stand-up comedian from Buenos Aires saves money from various events to fund a trip to the Falkland Islands, which he documents with a hidden camera. He plans to impregnate an islander as part of a scheme to "infiltrate" the islands. He targets Camilla, whom he saw in church, and has initial success, having sex with her twice. He manages to impregnate her before leaving for Buenos Aires.
The film takes place decades after the F.outcomes: Protagonist is exposed as shallow and condescending but manages to escape consequences. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.

In the mysterious metropolis of Rain Cit.coa:
A former policeman is released from prison after serving eight years on a murder rap.
He returns to his former hangout, Wanda's Cafe, which is still run by someone he knows.
New arrivals in town are a down-on-his-luck man and his family, who move into the area.
The down-on-his-luck man gets a job with a gangster, but doesn't do well at it.
In the mysterious metropolis of Rain Cit.outcomes: Protagonist (Hawk) finds redemption and possibly love in the end. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader threats remain.
In the mysterious metropolis of Rain Cit.theme: redemption; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

At the center of the action is the unjus.coa: The twin brother of the French King is unjustly imprisoned and exiled, wearing an iron mask that prevents him from removing it himself. The aging D'Artagnan and his musketeer friends become aware of the situation and decide to help the prisoner. They attempt to free the prisoner, but their efforts are complicated by the fact that only he has a legitimate claim to the throne of France. This leads to numerous confusions and intrigues at court as various factions try to take advantage of the situation.
At the center of the action is the unjus.outcomes: Protagonist is freed from imprisonment and exile. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as protagonist regains his rightful place in society.
At the center of the action is the unjus.theme: justice vs oppression; identity concealment; loyalty and redemption

A Roma family lives in the Bosnia-Herzeg.coa: A Roma family lives in the Bosnia-Herzegovina countryside. Senada feels an acute stomach pain. She goes to the hospital, where doctors tell her that her unborn child has died and she needs an urgent operation to prevent septicaemia. Without funds or insurance, they cannot afford treatment.
A Roma family lives in the Bosnia-Herzeg.outcomes: Protagonist's partner undergoes emergency surgery, but the outcome is uncertain due to lack of resources. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal and relational consequences.
A Roma family lives in the Bosnia-Herzeg.theme: access to healthcare; economic inequality; resilience in crisis

The story features three human character.coa: The Galeriebesucher witnesses the Kunstreiterin and her Pferd suffering during a performance. The Direktor forces them to continue performing despite their distress. The Galeriebesucher rushes into the arena to intervene, believing the situation is dire. This action causes the Direktor to become visibly upset.
The story features three human character.outcomes: Protagonist's perception changes from subjective to objective understanding. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.
The story features three human character.theme: perception vs reality; empathy and understanding

When soldier Jack Spade learns that his.coa:
A soldier returns home after learning that their brother overdosed on gold chains and died.
The soldier surveys the old neighborhood and notices the negative impact of gold chains on the community.
This observation sparks a desire for justice, leading the soldier to vow to destroy the source of the problem, Mr. Big.
The soldier seeks help from a local hero in planning the demise of Mr. Big's empire.
When soldier Jack Spade learns that his.outcomes: Protagonist succeeds in destroying chain lord's empire and reducing gold trade in the streets. The conflict is resolved with social change and community transformation.

Edison (Carrot Top) is a poor, failed in.coa: A poor inventor spends his rent money on another unsuccessful invention, leading to financial struggles and joblessness. A wealthy business magnate's car breaks down, and the inventor uses his inventions to assist him. The magnate is impressed with the inventor's ingenuity and they become friends after going surfing together.
Edison (Carrot Top) is a poor, failed in.outcomes: Protagonist Edison achieves financial stability and success through his inventions and business acumen. The conflict is resolved with the exposure of Bradford's deceit and the protection of the company.
Edison (Carrot Top) is a poor, failed in.theme: redemption; social mobility; innovation vs sabotage

While visiting England, 18 year old Meli.coa:
A Regimental Ball is held in England.
The protagonist meets a handsome Major at the ball and they develop an attraction to each other.
A whirlwind courtship ensues, leading to marriage.
After the honeymoon, the young bride discovers her husband's true identity as a Russian spy.
While visiting England, 18 year old Meli.outcomes: Protagonist survives and escapes danger with her life. The conflict is unresolved with a threat remaining.
While visiting England, 18 year old Meli.theme: deception; loyalty vs duty; love vs betrayal

To gain entry to Heaven, ghost Marion Ke.coa:
A ghost is trying to gain entry to Heaven by doing good deeds on earth.
The ghost's task involves reuniting a divorcing couple.
The couple's troubles began when one partner mistakenly believed the ghost was the other's mistress.
This mistake was caused by the ghost's presence in their lives.
To gain entry to Heaven, ghost Marion Ke.outcomes: Protagonist successfully facilitates reconciliation between Cosmo and Clara Topper. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation as the couple reunites.

In the mid-1930s mobster Roberto La Rocc.coa:
Mobster arrives in Marseille from elsewhere, intent on helping a friend who was framed by local crime bosses.
Friend's frame-up leads to clashes between different gangs, drawing mobster into conflict.
Clashes escalate, resulting in mobster being accused of murdering several enemy gang members and subsequently arrested.
Mobster is sentenced to long prison term for murder.
In the mid-1930s mobster Roberto La Rocc.outcomes: Protagonist escapes from prison and achieves freedom. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader threats remain.
In the mid-1930s mobster Roberto La Rocc.theme: survival under adversity; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

The bandit Sancho kills the wife of John.coa: A bandit kills the wife of a man, abducting his young son instead of killing him. The boy grows up and kills his fiancée when she discovers his plan for a robbery. The father continues searching for his son and eventually encounters him while saving the other man's life. They cross paths again after the father confronts and kills the bandit who originally abducted his son, learning about the son's true identity from the bandit's wife.
The bandit Sancho kills the wife of John.outcomes: Protagonist achieves revenge and justice for his family. The conflict is resolved with the death of the antagonist and the prevention of further harm from the evil offspring.

Synopsis from Doomed Marathon: \"A group.coa:
A group of tourists travel to an island to see its exotic botanicals.
They meet a reclusive scientist who is studying esoteric horticulture and experimenting with crossbreeding plants.
The tourists learn that the scientist has created a plant that can drain human blood through a small hole in their cheek.
One tourist dies after being bitten by the plant, causing panic among the remaining group.
Synopsis from Doomed Marathon: \"A group.outcomes: Protagonist group is decimated, and only a few may survive. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.
Synopsis from Doomed Marathon: \"A group.theme: beauty vs danger; scientific hubris; human vulnerability

In pre-revolutionary Russia, young Chine.coa:
A young Chinese variety singer falls in love with a dashing officer.
The officer's commander, a grand duke, becomes infatuated with the singer and tries to win her affection.
The singer's brother attempts to protect her from the Grand Duke's advances and shoots him as a result.
The Grand Duke is injured and the singer's brother is sentenced to death.
In pre-revolutionary Russia, young Chine.outcomes: Protagonist is left heartbroken and separated from her lover, while her brother escapes to freedom. The conflict is resolved with a personal sacrifice for the protagonist and a partial resolution of the external threat.
In pre-revolutionary Russia, young Chine.theme: sacrificial love; loyalty vs duty; social hierarchy

The film, with a similar plot to High No.coa: The Fourth of July wedding between Dan Ballard and Rose Evans is planned. Marshal Fred McCarty and his deputies ride into town, looking for Ballard. McCarty accuses Ballard of murdering his brother, prompting the townspeople to turn against him initially. The townspeople's opinion shifts further when they believe Ballard has killed the town sheriff.
The film, with a similar plot to High No.outcomes: Protagonist Dan Ballard is ultimately cleared of the accusation, but not before the town's reputation is tarnished. The conflict is resolved with personal vindication but broader social implications remain.
The film, with a similar plot to High No.theme: justice vs false accusations; loyalty under pressure; public perception vs truth

The First Emperor searches for the elixi.coa:
The First Emperor sends 500 teenage boys and girls on a mission to find the elixir of immortality.
One of the despatched maidens is discovered to be in love with General Meng Tianfang, one of the emperor's soldiers.
Their forbidden love is exposed, and the girl reveals she has found the elusive elixir.
The emperor orders their execution, and General Meng Tianfang is sentenced to death by being encased alive in clay as a terracotta warrior.
The First Emperor searches for the elixi.outcomes: Protagonist (soldier) survives and adapts to new life, but faces ongoing threats from looters and thugs. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation and social disorientation.
The First Emperor searches for the elixi.theme: reincarnation; love vs duty; cultural preservation

Southold, New York, 1843. Young Mary (St.coa: The matriarch dies under mysterious circumstances. The family's patriarch interrogates Mary about her involvement in the death, but she is blindfolded and unable to see or defend herself. As the interrogation continues, flashbacks reveal that Mary had been secretly meeting with a maid, who was also being mistreated by the family for their relationship. The arrival of an unknown intruder coincides with the discovery of dark secrets within the household.

The couple's lives are marked by extreme events as they navigate their illicit affair.

Linda Randolph looks for her husband on.coa:
A white woman travels to an island east of Borneo despite warnings about its dangers.

New York theatrical producer Larry O'Bri.coa:
A theatrical producer plans to found a motion picture company in Hollywood.

As the team begins to ride together, organizers realize that their athletes' needs extend beyond athletic training. The team's riders struggle with the psychological effects of the 1994 genocide.
Professional cyclist Jacques “Jock” Boye.outcomes: Protagonist helps the team achieve success and stability, with many athletes overcoming their past traumas. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social reintegration, as the team becomes a positive force in Rwanda's recovery.

Marvin Blake is a sharecropper's son who.coa:

As described in a film magazine, Count F.coa: The Emperor mandates that the count be affianced to a woman he does not love. A 20-year-old poet travels from his provincial home to Paris after a contentious affair with a local society lady.

He becomes beholden to the world of low-brow journalism and succumbs to bribery and cronyism at the behest of his boss, achieving wealth and standing but losing artistic integrity and former friendships.

A young boxer named Buzz Kinney, fresh o.coa:

Gwyn Marcus (Sarah Jessica Parker) is in.coa: The protagonist accepts a proposal from her boyfriend. She learns of various affairs within her family. Forensics expert David Hunter is recover.coa: A forensics expert is working as a doctor in an isolated Norfolk village after recovering from a personal tragedy three years earlier. The discovery of the corpse sparks suspicion among the villagers, including suspicion that the expert may be involved.
