Judith Moore had what she thought was a.coa:
Judith puts her studies on hold to find a job and support herself and her husband.
Many years pass until the husband leaves Judith for another doctor.
Depressed, Judith holes up in her apartment.
The middle-aged building superintendent Pat Francato makes contact with Judith.
Judith Moore had what she thought was a.outcomes: Protagonist emerges from depression and begins to reconnect with others. The conflict is unresolved as protagonist still struggles with feelings of abandonment and rejection.

21-year-old Oliver Mansfeld is the son o.coa: Oliver tries to complete his abitur in a boarding school, belatedly. He falls head over heels in love with Verena, who is ten years older than him. Oliver begins an affair with Verena, despite her being married to an older banker who does business with Oliver's father. The affair becomes violent and short-lived.
21-year-old Oliver Mansfeld is the son o.outcomes: Protagonist dies by suicide, failing to escape the entanglements that led to his downfall. The conflict is unresolved with no personal transformation or social reintegration.

The film is set on 27 Down, the Bombay-V.coa: The Bombay-Varanasi Express is traveling from Bombay to Varanasi. The protagonist remembers his life in flashbacks as the train travels. He recalls giving up his dreams of becoming an artist and instead taking a job as a railways ticket checker to support his family. This decision was made because he needed to provide for them, having taken over his father's profession.
The film is set on 27 Down, the Bombay-V.outcomes: Protagonist's relationship with Shalini ends due to family opposition, and he remains single. The conflict is unresolved with personal sacrifice and unfulfilled dreams.

After witnessing his mother being shot b.coa: The Imperial German Army invades and shoots Artūrs' mother. Artūrs decides to enlist in the national Latvian Riflemen battalions of the Imperial Russian Army with his father, seeking revenge and glory. They are deployed on the Eastern Front during World War I. Artūrs loses his father and brother while fighting for the Imperial Russian Army.
After witnessing his mother being shot b.outcomes: Protagonist survives the wars and starts anew in his homeland. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation, but broader systemic issues remain.

The documentary concerns the captivity o.coa: In 1983, an orca was captured off the coast of Iceland. The orca was taken to Sealand of the Pacific, where it was allegedly harassed by fellow captive orcas. This harassment is said to have contributed to the orca's aggression. The orca went on to be involved in the deaths of three people.
The documentary concerns the captivity o.outcomes: Protagonist's aggression leads to his own survival but also to the death of three people. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing threats from Tilikum and other captive orcas.

A reporter is killed in his car on his w.coa:
A reporter is killed in his car on his way to work.
Inspector Kras gets a call from Peter Cornelius, who had a vision of the crime but not the perpetrator.
Peter Cornelius's vision prompts Inspector Kras to investigate further.
The investigation leads Inspector Kras to suspect that the murder may be connected to an old case involving Dr. Mabuse.
A reporter is killed in his car on his w.outcomes: The reporter's killer is revealed to be Hieronymus B. Mistelzweig, who was using the Luxor Hotel as a front for his nefarious activities. The conflict is resolved with the exposure and defeat of the villainous Dr. Mabuse's re-emergence.

In Sicily, truck driver Salvatore Colasb.coa: A truck driver is murdered while delivering cement to a highway construction project. The murder occurs within sight and earshot of the house of a local resident and their husband. Police captain Bellodi learns that there may be irregularities in the highway's construction, possibly involving corruption. He also hears rumors about the local resident's morals, which she denies.
In Sicily, truck driver Salvatore Colasb.outcomes: Protagonist fails to determine the true cause of the murder but uncovers corruption in the construction project. The conflict is unresolved with broader threats remaining due to entrenched corruption and honour system.

Jay Moore, the slovenly manager of a san.coa:
The manager's wife discovers his involvement in shady activities and threatens to go to the police.
In response, he hires a hit man to scare her.
However, the hit man decides to kill her instead.
The police chief suspects the manager of his wife's murder.
Jay Moore, the slovenly manager of a san.outcomes: Protagonist is eventually caught and implicated in his wife's murder. The conflict is unresolved with a complex web of consequences.
Jay Moore, the slovenly manager of a san.theme: deception; moral accountability; chaos vs order

Anne-Marie (Renée Faure), a well–off you.coa: A well-off young woman decides to become a nun by joining a convent that rehabilitates female prisoners. She meets a woman who refuses any help because she claims innocence of the crime for which she was convicted. The woman is released from prison and murders the man she believes is responsible for her imprisonment, then seeks sanctuary in the convent. Her presence causes tension among the nuns, particularly with one young nun who clashes with them over her zeal to reform the newcomer.
Anne-Marie (Renée Faure), a well–off you.outcomes: Protagonist fails to reform antagonist, but instead is manipulated by her. The conflict is unresolved with no personal transformation or social reintegration.

With the death of Chief Issetibbeha, cus.coa:
Chief Issetibbeha dies.
Custom demands that all his prized possessions be buried alive with him, including his black servant.
The slave makes a desperate bid for freedom by escaping into the swamps.
The dead chief's son is forced to marshal his forces and begin a manhunt for the fugitive slave.
With the death of Chief Issetibbeha, cus.outcomes: Protagonist (unnamed slave) achieves temporary freedom but broader systemic issues remain unresolved. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.
With the death of Chief Issetibbeha, cus.theme: slavery; cultural decline; loss of identity

As a result of rape, Amzine, a young Mus.coa:
A young Muslim woman gives birth to a child as a result of rape.
The child grows up and becomes 12 years old, serving as a daily reminder of the suffering.
A Christian girl suffers from a gunshot wound to her knee that refuses to heal.
She undergoes successful surgery in Berlin, giving her hope for a pain-free existence.

At Nell's joyous 21st birthday party her.coa:
A woman turns 21 years old and celebrates with a party.

The knowledge shatters her self-image and changes the course of her life.
Years later, after her father's death, he leaves behind clues about her past.

She receives the clues in 1975 and they trigger memories that lead her to travel to England.
While there, she discovers a rare fairytale book connected to her past.
The book helps her uncover her true identity despite being thought dead for over 60 years.
She travels to Cornwall, where she finds Blackhurst Manor on the coast.

Her plans are interrupted when her granddaughter comes to stay temporarily, but the stay becomes permanent.
The granddaughter is haunted by her own griefs and struggles with her mother's death.
In 2005, the granddaughter follows in her grandmother's footsteps to continue the journey of discovery.

The time for the divorce arrives, prompting the husband to return home to get his clothes.
He is persuaded to stay by his wife, who has been disgusted by him due to suspicions of infidelity.
As described in a film magazine review,.outcomes: Protagonist's marriage is reconciled but underlying issues remain unresolved. The conflict is partially resolved with relational transformation but deeper problems persist.
As described in a film magazine review,.theme: unrequited love; marital infidelity; deception vs honesty

Two aging men, Goodwin and Johnson (Mitc.coa: Two aging men are fishermen in their spare time and try to buy a new boat, but their pier is controlled by a gangster who extorts "protection" money from them. The gangster demands $190 from them after learning that one of them had promised this amount to his daughter for a holiday. The two men plan to kill the gangster, but neither can go through with it. The gangster attempts to strike one of them and falls into the sea, drowning.
Two aging men, Goodwin and Johnson (Mitc.outcomes: Protagonist Goodwin achieves escape from immediate danger and recovers extorted money. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as Goff's true nature is revealed.

Mujhe Jeene Do, is a classic dacoit dram.coa: A dacoit is involved in a wedding where he meets a courtesan. They elope together and fall in love, resulting in the birth of their son. The dacoit is about to be captured by the police, so he sends his lover away with their son to a neighbouring village.
Mujhe Jeene Do, is a classic dacoit dram.outcomes: Protagonist is captured by the police, but his loved ones are saved. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation through love and redemption.
Mujhe Jeene Do, is a classic dacoit dram.theme: redemption; love conquers adversity; human connection

Brendan Byers III is a rich playboy who.coa: A wealthy playboy enlists to fight in a war but is classified as unfit for duty. He forms his own army by recruiting other unfit individuals and loyal volunteers, financing their training and equipment. The group travels to the front in Italy, with one member impersonating a high-ranking Nazi officer. They plan to pull back German lines to allow the Allies to advance again.
Brendan Byers III is a rich playboy who.outcomes: Protagonist successfully completes his initial mission and achieves a tactical victory. The conflict is partially resolved with personal growth but broader strategic challenges remain.

Susannah Kelton, a newly married woman w.coa:
Susannah learns that her real parents have died and left their property to her.
She and her husband travel to inspect the property on an island off the coast of Massachusetts.
They find a local culture that is clannish, backward, and ignorant, and make a few friends among the locals who warn them about the family mill being cursed.
The couple refuses to leave and considers rebuilding the abandoned mill.
Susannah Kelton, a newly married woman w.outcomes: Protagonist survives and achieves resolution of conflict with the help of an unknown entity. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as protagonist overcomes past fears.
Susannah Kelton, a newly married woman w.theme: overcoming superstition; facing inner demons; redemption through confrontation

On the outskirts of N'Djamena in Chad, A.coa:
A 15-year-old girl becomes pregnant.
The girl does not want the pregnancy.
Her mother discovers the pregnancy.
The discovery causes her fragile world to collapse.
On the outskirts of N'Djamena in Chad, A.outcomes: Protagonist's daughter is forced to carry the pregnancy to term, resulting in a significant life change. The conflict is unresolved with respect to the protagonist's desire for her daughter's well-being and the societal constraints.
On the outskirts of N'Djamena in Chad, A.theme: unwanted pregnancy; social vs personal responsibility; moral complexity

Karin Mansdotter is the daughter of an o.coa:
King Erik falls madly in love with Karin Mansdotter, daughter of an ordinary soldier.
The king's adviser Göran Persson condones Erik's decision to make Karin his Queen after the plan to marry English princess Elizabeth Tudor fails.
The Swedish nobility resents this wedding and conspires against the king.
The isolated King eventually shows signs of distress, which are used by his enemies as evidence of his unfitness for rule.
Karin Mansdotter is the daughter of an o.outcomes: Protagonist loses her position and influence as the king is dethroned. The conflict is unresolved with a shift in power dynamics but no lasting resolution for Karin.

A mysterious, vengeful stranger rides in.coa: A mysterious stranger rides into town, causing chaos and destruction. He places crosses with names on them in the middle of the street, indicating his targets. The townspeople are shocked and frightened by these actions, unsure what to make of the stranger's motives or identity. As the stranger continues his rampage, a local historian begins to investigate the origins of the stranger's vendetta.
A mysterious, vengeful stranger rides in.outcomes: Protagonist (town) survives the initial onslaught but remains in a state of fear and uncertainty. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing threat from stranger.

The film tells the story of Simon Calmat.coa:
A French swimming coach is divorcing his wife.
He tries to help an Iraqi-Kurd immigrant whose dream is to cross the English Channel by any means possible.
The young immigrant, caught with others, is returned to France and given temporary refuge at the coach's home after registering for swimming lessons.
Police search the apartment, prompting the immigrant to make a final attempt to cross the Channel.
The film tells the story of Simon Calmat.outcomes: Protagonist Simon is left with emotional impact from the event. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation for Simon and loss for Bilal.

After an interview sequence with a girl,.coa:
A girl prepares to kill herself by jumping from a bridge.
Gabor intervenes to prevent the suicide.
He persuades the girl to become the target in his knifethrowing act.
They travel together to Monaco and then into Italy, performing their act on a cruise ship. 
Their companionship and teamwork bring them good luck.
They get separated, with her going to Greece and him to Turkey.
Their lives become unlucky again after being apart.
The girl saves Gabor from suicide on a bridge in Istanbul.
After an interview sequence with a girl,.outcomes: Protagonist survives and achieves companionship and teamwork, but later experiences separation and loss. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as they help each other through difficult times.

In Edwardian England, Ivy Lexton (Joan F.coa:
Ivy becomes dissatisfied with her husband's financial situation.
She sees an opportunity with wealthy Miles Rushworth and decides to pursue him, despite being married and having another romantic interest.
Miles shows no interest in Ivy due to her marital status.
Ivy attempts to persuade her husband to divorce her.
In Edwardian England, Ivy Lexton (Joan F.outcomes: Protagonist Ivy Lexton is revealed as a murderer and her scheme is exposed. The conflict is unresolved with no personal transformation or social reintegration for the protagonist.

Joey Davis is an unemployed former child.coa:
The unemployed individual uses sex to get his landlady to reduce his rent.
He then seduces Sally Todd, a former Hollywood starlet.
Sally tries to help him revive his career as an actor.
Her attempts prove unsuccessful due to her own lack of influence in the industry.
Joey Davis is an unemployed former child.outcomes: Protagonist fails to achieve career revival and remains emotionally numb. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.

The Blue brothers (Kane, Johnny and Fran.coa: The Blue brothers start a life of bank robbery. Their actions attract the attention of a local law enforcement officer, who begins to pursue them. The pursuit leads to a series of failed attempts by the officer to capture the brothers. The brothers continue to rob banks, eventually accumulating enough money to fund their desired lifestyle.
The Blue brothers (Kane, Johnny and Fran.outcomes: Protagonist achieves freedom and independence but at the cost of increased danger and uncertainty. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing tension between protagonist and authority.
The Blue brothers (Kane, Johnny and Fran.theme: rebellion vs control; freedom vs danger; the allure of adventure

During the closing stage of the Second W.coa:
The Second World War ends.
A marriage takes place between two people who will soon be separated.
Wartime events cause them to be apart for eight years.
During that time, one of them conceives a child with someone else.
They are reunited when the captive person returns home.
Their reunion is met with shock and betrayal by the other person.
This leads to a divorce being filed.
The person who was betrayed eventually comes to terms with what happened and reconciles with their spouse.
During the closing stage of the Second W.outcomes: Protagonist reconciles with spouse after overcoming initial feelings of betrayal. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation through forgiveness and understanding.

Claude (Claude Gauthier) leaves his smal.coa:
Leaves small town on Côte-Nord for Montreal.
Works several odd jobs in Montreal.
Meets and falls in love with waitress at local diner.
Enters singing contest that launches music career.
Becomes more well known, has affair with married woman.
Breaks up with waitress due to increasing fame.
Returns to hometown, finds nothing is the same.
Continues to become increasingly successful as a singer in Montreal.
Meets waitress backstage one night, learns she is now married.
Claude (Claude Gauthier) leaves his smal.outcomes: Protagonist achieves fame and success as a singer. The conflict is unresolved with personal growth and increased awareness of loneliness in both small town and big city.
Claude (Claude Gauthier) leaves his smal.theme: success vs isolation; love vs ambition; emptiness of material achievement

When an undercover FBI agent with a secr.coa: An undercover FBI agent murders the son of high-ranking crime boss Frank Serlano. The crime boss retaliates by kidnapping the son of the FBI agent in charge, Zach Grant. Yuji breaks free from Grant's supervision during a trans-Atlantic flight and forces the plane to make an emergency landing. The three crash survivors go off the grid to evade the yakuza and mafia.
When an undercover FBI agent with a secr.outcomes: Protagonist loses custody of child but survives the ordeal. The conflict is unresolved with potential for further escalation.
When an undercover FBI agent with a secr.theme: chaos; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Beppe Agosti, Florentine, baker's boy an.coa: The protagonist is called up to arms and sent to Catania, where he befriends a fellow soldier from Sicily. The Sicilian soldier becomes engaged to a waitress in the house of a well-known lawyer, but due to lack of discipline, he is put in prison and transferred. The protagonist consoles the waitress after her fiancé's departure and they fall in love, eventually getting married.
Beppe Agosti, Florentine, baker's boy an.outcomes: Protagonist Beppe survives and is reunited with his first wife Maria Antonia after the invalidation of his second marriage. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as Beppe learns from his mistakes and prioritizes his relationships.
Beppe Agosti, Florentine, baker's boy an.theme: deception; loyalty vs love; consequences of actions

The film takes place in the 15th century.coa: The Grand Duchy of Moscow wants to capture the Ural. The Prince of Great Perm forms an alliance with local residents and old gods to fight back against the Moscow troops. A young witch-lamia, capable of taking on a lynx form, becomes involved in the conflict through her relationship with the Prince. This sparks a confrontation between the two worlds: the Grand Duchy of Moscow and the Ural Parma.

The film takes place in the 15th century.theme: love vs duty; cultural clashes; survival under adversity

Set in Houston, Texas this novel tells t.coa:
A serial killer begins targeting people in a specific part of Houston, Texas.
The killings are particularly brutal and attention-grabbing.
As a result, the police department launches an investigation to catch the killer.
Police lieutenant leads the investigation.
Set in Houston, Texas this novel tells t.outcomes: The serial killer is ultimately brought to justice, but not before causing significant harm and trauma to the community. The conflict is resolved with a sense of closure for the victims' families, but broader systemic issues related to crime and policing remain unaddressed.

Shuji Ito (Yoshinori Okada) is a shy boy.coa:
A shy boy feels attracted to his classmate and best friend, but the latter is not aware of these intimate feelings.
The two spend time with Tōru Kanbara, whose comic actions hide his sensitive nature.
Kasane Aihara transfers to school and remains aloof with her cool attitude, hiding a traumatic past.
Yoshida becomes interested in Aihara, the mysterious new girl.
Shuji Ito (Yoshinori Okada) is a shy boy.outcomes: Protagonist begins to open up about his feelings, and relationships start to shift. The conflict is unresolved with potential for growth and change.
Shuji Ito (Yoshinori Okada) is a shy boy.theme: unrequited love; emotional vulnerability; coming of age

The story is that of the fierce passiona.coa:
A wealthy man takes in a poor child to be raised as his son.
The wealthy man's biological daughter forms a close bond with the adopted child.
The wealthy man dies, leaving behind a large estate and two children.
The biological son treats the adopted child poorly and has him beaten.
The adopted child grows up feeling mistreated and unloved by the biological family.
He falls deeply in love with the wealthy man's biological daughter, but she is promised to marry another man.
The adopted child becomes enraged and seeks revenge against those who wronged him.
He marries the sister of his former lover's husband, treating her cruelly.
The adopted child's former lover dies, leaving behind a young daughter.
The adopted child's son falls in love with the daughter of his former lover.
The story is that of the fierce passiona.outcomes: Protagonist loses loved one and is left alone, yet finds a measure of peace through their shared ghostly existence. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation for both protagonists.
The story is that of the fierce passiona.theme: forbidden love; class struggle; the destructive power of unchecked emotions

Tomo Ogawa is a neglected 11-year-old gi.coa: An 11-year-old girl lives with her single mother in a small apartment. Her mother falls in love with a new man and abandons the girl. The girl is forced to move in with her uncle, who has a girlfriend. The uncle's girlfriend is a transgender woman.
Tomo Ogawa is a neglected 11-year-old gi.outcomes: Protagonist finds acceptance in her non-traditional family environment. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and increased understanding of diverse relationships.

The journey begins with Frederic Otomo d.coa:
Frederic Otomo departs from his home early one morning to get a job at a factory in Germany.
He is refused employment due to his shoes not being proper for work.
He departs for home on a train, but is kicked off by a ticket inspector who claims his ticket has expired.
The ticket inspector tries to have him arrested, making Otomo a fugitive.
The journey begins with Frederic Otomo d.outcomes: Protagonist dies as a result of the confrontation with the police officers. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.

Boisterous gangster kingpin 'Bull' Weed.coa:
Weed rehabilitates Wensel, a former lawyer who has fallen into alcoholism.
Wensel's intelligence aids Weed in his schemes, and the two become confidants.
Wensel falls for McCoy, Weed's girlfriend.
Mulligan attempts to muscle in on Weed's territory.
Boisterous gangster kingpin 'Bull' Weed.outcomes: Protagonist escapes imprisonment and achieves freedom. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and relational change, as Rolls Royce and Feathers consider eloping together.

As a teenager, Diana is looking for true.coa:
A teenager looks up to their parents' relationship as a model of true love.
The teenager discovers that one of their parents is having an affair with someone else.
This discovery causes the teenager to question everything they thought they knew about their family and relationships.
While trying to intervene, the teenager learns more about themselves than they expected.
As a teenager, Diana is looking for true.outcomes: Protagonist gains self-awareness and insight into her own desires and values. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as she re-evaluates her idealized view of love.
As a teenager, Diana is looking for true.theme: illusion vs reality; self-discovery; the complexity of love

Mr. Dithers generously invites the three.coa:
A telegram arrives from a buyer anxious to meet soon to purchase a property the company has had on sale for a long time.
The arrival of the telegram causes Dithers to decide that only one person should return home to meet with the client, and he chooses Dagwood.
Before Dagwood can debark, the drummer of the ship's dance band is taken ill, forcing him to take his place despite his protests.
Dagwood dresses as a woman while drumming so his family and Dithers won't recognize him.
Mr. Dithers generously invites the three.outcomes: Dagwood survives the ordeal of being on the ship without his family knowing and helps foil a con man's plan. The conflict is resolved with personal success and social reintegration.

While en route to Wal-Mart for grass see.coa: The Burketts are on their way to Wal-Mart for grass seed when they start arguing about their lawn, his smoking, and her obesity. Mary demands they stop at a convenience store so she can buy a purple kickball for their niece's birthday. While Ray and the dog wait in the car, Mary suffers a heart attack in the store and dies immediately. The store employee fetches Ray from the car to inform him of Mary's death.

Vishal (Pankaj Dheer) is a respectable a.coa:
Vishal is abducted.
The abductor kills Vishal in front of his younger brother, who is present at the scene.
The brother is taken in by a jungle tribe and trained by its chief.
Raghunath becomes aware of the situation and demands action from Omiji.

Paul, a mechanic on an oil tanker, jumps.coa:
A mechanic on an oil tanker jumps ship during a stopover in Lisbon.
He settles in a small bar-hotel, sending 8 mm films and letters about his travels to someone back home.
The person receives these updates, which describe the city as being very white.
The mechanic meets a pretty waitress at a nearby bar.

Pier Ullman works at odd jobs and also d.coa:
Pier Ullman performs odd jobs and participates in break-ins with Kevin for Rachid.
Rachid informs Pier of the death of his father, who had been absent for several years.
Pier decides to avenge his father's mistreatment by his grandfather and uncle.
Pier makes contact with his father's family.
Pier Ullman works at odd jobs and also d.outcomes: Protagonist successfully infiltrates the diamond business and begins to exact revenge on his grandfather and uncle. The conflict is unresolved as protagonist continues to pursue vengeance.

Elske faithfully loves her husband Endri.coa:
A foreign schemer persuades someone to murder their loved one and run off together.
The person is lured into a boat as part of the plan to carry out the murder.
They realize the intent behind the plan and flee when they reach land.
Someone follows them to plead for forgiveness. 
A storm blows up while they are in the boat, causing them to separate.
One person gets ashore but believes the other has drowned.
The surviving person reacts with anger towards the schemer, who is revealed to have been lying about the other's fate.

After robbing a stagecoach, the bandit B.coa:
A stagecoach is robbed by a bandit.
The bandit befriends an honest engineer who works for the mine company.
The bandit earns the trust of the citizens and is offered the position of sheriff.
The bandit accepts the job, but it is unclear if he genuinely wants to serve or has ulterior motives.
After robbing a stagecoach, the bandit B.outcomes: Protagonist is appointed to a position of authority and gains access to valuable resources. The conflict is unresolved with potential for future tension between personal goals and responsibilities.

Signaller Charles Plumpick (Bates) is a.coa:
A bomb is placed in the town square by retreating Germans.
The townspeople learn about the booby trap and abandon the area, leaving their homes and businesses behind.
The asylum gates are left open, allowing its inmates to escape and take on the roles of the townspeople.
The inmates begin living among the evacuated residents, creating a surreal atmosphere that contrasts with the war-torn reality.
Signaller Charles Plumpick (Bates) is a.outcomes: Protagonist successfully disarms the bomb, preventing harm to himself and others. The conflict is resolved with a sense of order restored, but underlying social issues remain.

As Christmas break approaches, the dormi.coa: Christmas break approaches at Billingsley University, causing restlessness among students eager for sex. The dormitories are filled with students seeking romantic encounters. A student who is still a virgin decides to lose his virginity before the break. His older brother hires a prostitute to help him achieve this goal.
As Christmas break approaches, the dormi.outcomes: Protagonist achieves sexual experience with the help of authority figure. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social acceptance.

In France during the German occupation,.coa:
A young German naval officer is killed by a group of leftist activists in Paris.
The Vichy government seeks to appease the Germans by locating the perpetrators.
They agree to the execution of six people and set up a special section for this purpose, consisting of judges who are willing to carry out such work despite its moral implications.

The protagonist, a likeable American cow.coa:
The protagonist acquires a priceless scroll in Alaska by chance.
The protagonist travels to 19th-Century Japan and becomes trapped in the middle of a feud between two aristocratic families.
Both families want the scroll for themselves, leading to violent fighting involving Samuri swords, a Gatling gun, and a makeshift single-shot blunderbuss.
The fighting escalates until the protagonist is forced to return the scroll.
The protagonist, a likeable American cow.outcomes: Protagonist successfully navigates the treacherous situation and resolves the conflict without harm. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation as the warring families are brought together through protagonist's actions.

Four small-time gangsters from Copenhage.coa: Four small-time gangsters steal 4,000,000 Danish kroner from a gangster boss who had entrusted them with the money. They attempt to escape to Barcelona but are forced to stop in the countryside due to circumstances. The group hides in an old, wrecked house for several weeks. As they settle into their temporary refuge, some of the gangsters begin to consider abandoning their original plan and staying in the countryside permanently.
Four small-time gangsters from Copenhage.outcomes: Protagonists successfully establish a new life in the countryside but ultimately face consequences from their past actions. The conflict is unresolved as their past continues to haunt them.

Set during 1999, James is archiving vide.coa:
A television station employee discovers a video tape containing a broadcast signal intrusion (BSI) with a disguised person acting strangely.
The employee investigates reports of a similar BSI from an episode of a sci-fi TV show and finds that the FCC took all copies of the episode.
This discovery leads the employee to suspect a larger issue, prompting further investigation into the matter.
During this search, the employee uncovers information linking the BSIs to the disappearance of several women.
Set during 1999, James is archiving vide.outcomes: Protagonist uncovers evidence of a larger conspiracy and potential connection to his wife's disappearance. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing investigation and personal stakes.

Single mother Ellie West (Helen Slater),.coa: A single mother meets a surgeon at her consult for upcoming out-patient surgery and they begin a romantic relationship. She is encouraged by her close friend and supportive boss, but then has an unusual reaction when she is put under in the operating room. The lead anesthesiologist is dismayed by this reaction. Post-surgery, strange signs indicate that Ellie and her daughter may be in danger.
Single mother Ellie West (Helen Slater),.outcomes: Protagonist survives the unusual reaction but is left shaken. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing threats to protagonist's safety.
Single mother Ellie West (Helen Slater),.theme: love vs danger; trust issues; vulnerability

Like the two earlier Great Netherworld B.coa: The sun god begins his journey from the western horizon. He travels through the underworld, passing over the caverns of Hell where the enemies of the world order are being destroyed. His journey takes him past various divine creatures that he interacts with along the way. The Book of Caverns describes these interactions and landmarks as part of Ra's passage from west to east.
Like the two earlier Great Netherworld B.outcomes: Sun god successfully completes his journey, maintaining the world order. The conflict is resolved with cosmic stability.

Vittorio, nicknamed \"Accattone\", leads.coa:
Maddalena is hurt by a rivaling gang and sent to prison for false testimony.
Accattone's steady income is disrupted, leaving him without a reliable source of money.
He attempts to reconcile with the estranged mother of his child but is driven away by her relatives.
Accattone tries to lure Stella into prostituting herself for him.
Vittorio, nicknamed \"Accattone\", leads.outcomes: Protagonist dies in the end. The conflict is unresolved with no personal transformation or social change.

Ben Garvey, a reformed criminal, loses h.coa: Ben loses his job due to his criminal background. His brother convinces him to commit a robbery of gold dust from a laboratory, which goes horribly wrong and results in the deaths of Ricky and two others. Ben is sentenced to death. Ben's wife and daughter visit him before his execution, during which he tells his daughter that he won't be coming back.

It began as a contest of strength - a br.coa: A brazen challenge is issued to lure competitors into the main arena of a castle. The champion emerges among the warriors who defeat all opponents in combat. Combatants begin to mysteriously disappear from the castle. A Match of Titans ensues, pitting Deathstalker against an invincible army of Stone Warriors and their ruler.

A man whose hands shook with the tremors.coa: The man's hands shook while eating soup, causing him to spill it frequently. His family barred him from their table due to the mess he made. He broke a fine stoneware bowl while trying to eat by the stove. They replaced the broken bowl with a wooden one that wouldn't break.
A man whose hands shook with the tremors.outcomes: Protagonist is allowed to eat at the table again, indicating acceptance and inclusion. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation as family members learn to be more compassionate.

Jamil (Ramon Novarro) is a soldier in th.coa:
A war breaks out between Syria and Turkey.
The village is attacked by the Turks.
The ruler intends to hand over the orphaned children for slaughter, disguising his intentions as a move to Damascus for their safety.
Jamil arrives at the scene, having deserted his regiment.
Jamil (Ramon Novarro) is a soldier in th.outcomes: Protagonist becomes the new leader of the tribe and saves the children from slaughter. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and external change.
Jamil (Ramon Novarro) is a soldier in th.theme: responsibility; identity; courage in the face of adversity

Antoinette, a wealthy young widow, runs.coa:

She is forced to sell her villa and car due to her financial difficulties.
By chance, she encounters William P. Harrison, who has purchased her former villa.
She helps him fix his car after he asks for assistance.
Antoinette, a wealthy young widow, runs.outcomes: Protagonist achieves financial recovery through the bet. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and relational change.

Gold-mine operator \"Boss\" Kruger (Raym.coa: A group of fugitives, including two individuals, are forced to work in a gold mine under harsh conditions. The mine's operator exercises total control over the workers, treating them like prisoners. One of the workers befriends an outsider, a lawyer who has arrived at the mine with a hidden agenda. The lawyer begins to investigate the mine and its operator.
Gold-mine operator \"Boss\" Kruger (Raym.outcomes: Protagonist succeeds in exposing antagonist's crimes, leading to his downfall. The conflict is resolved with justice served and the rule of law upheld.

Manuel Espírito Santo (whose surname mea.coa:
Manuel Espírito Santo suffers an accident and dies.
His soul becomes trapped between the world of the living and the afterlife, unable to rest.
He discovers that he can appear in people's dreams and communicate with them through this means.
He appears in his sister's dream and asks her to retrieve his body from Amsterdam.
Manuel Espírito Santo (whose surname mea.outcomes: Protagonist's spirit finds peace after his body is returned to his homeland. The conflict is resolved with personal resolution and closure.

Set in London, it is the story of Charle.coa:
A successful businessman meets a free-spirited American woman and marries her.
The businessman becomes suspicious that she is having an affair because she spends long hours away from home during the day, so he hires a private detective to follow her.
The detective follows the woman, but she discovers she is being followed and realizes the detective's intentions.
The detective continues to follow the woman, but instead of abandoning the case, he begins an elaborate game of cat and mouse with her complicity.
