Bridegroom chronicles the story of Shane.coa: A man dies from an accidental fall from a four-story apartment building in Los Angeles. His partner is left behind and cut off without any legal protection or recognition of their relationship. The partner is denied permission to attend the deceased's funeral by the deceased's family. The partner begins to tell the story of their 6-year-long relationship through a film.
Bridegroom chronicles the story of Shane.outcomes: Protagonist survives but faces ongoing emotional trauma and social isolation. The conflict is unresolved with respect to societal acceptance and legal protections for same-sex partners.
Bridegroom chronicles the story of Shane.theme: love vs societal norms; loss and grief; identity and belonging

Willy Loman has led a life consisting of.coa:
Willy has led a life consisting of 60 years of failure.
The emotional toll from this failure causes Willy's grip on reality to slip, and he begins to lose track of time, frantically trying to find where he went wrong.
This struggle is exacerbated by the rift between him and his son, which further contributes to Willy's exhaustion and sense of hopelessness.
Willy becomes so exhausted that he starts his car in the garage.
Willy Loman has led a life consisting of.outcomes: Protagonist dies by suicide. The conflict is unresolved as the protagonist's emotional pain persists even in death.

After her marriage to Prince Charming, C.coa:
Cinderella marries Prince Charming.
Her two stepsisters become jealous and manage to get her away from the palace.
The Prince falls into a state of despair due to Cinderella's absence.
Bonaventura promises the Prince that he will go in search of Cinderella.
After her marriage to Prince Charming, C.outcomes: Protagonist returns to the palace with Prince Charming. The conflict is resolved with reunion.

A London banker makes a business trip to.coa:
A London banker makes a business trip to Venice.
He falls in love with a woman there.
She is unhappy because her friend thinks another man is cheating on him, but it turns out he's not.
The woman marries the banker, who becomes the "model husband" she always wanted.
She soon gets bored with him because he shows no interest in other women.
No one shows romantic interest in the model husband.
He realizes that his wife unconsciously desires someone else.
He starts to act like a Don Juan, but only slightly.
A London banker makes a business trip to.outcomes: Protagonist achieves reconciliation and rekindled romance with his wife. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation through mutual understanding and adaptation.
A London banker makes a business trip to.theme: unconscious desires; the dangers of conformity; the power of self-awareness

Criminal Gustav Bumke is released early.coa:
Gustav Bumke is released early from prison and returns to his tenement block.
He is approached by old colleagues who want him to join them on a bank robbery, but he is initially reluctant.
The group runs into trouble during the robbery, prompting Gustav to intervene and assist them.
After helping his colleagues, Gustav attends a party where he witnesses Annya cheating on him with Ralph.
Criminal Gustav Bumke is released early.outcomes: Protagonist Gustav Bumke is arrested and taken into custody. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation for the protagonist.
Criminal Gustav Bumke is released early.theme: deception; loyalty vs betrayal; truth in confinement

Max, Pop, Jen and Jake are deer hunting.coa:
A hiker reads an ancient book of spells and is transformed into a demon.
The hunting group discovers the demon in the brush, thinking it's a deer.
They shoot at the demon and take refuge in a nearby cabin.
More creatures, also transformed by the book, attack the group.
Max, Pop, Jen and Jake are deer hunting.outcomes: Protagonist (Max) survives and manages to fend off remaining threats. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing danger from zombies and freaks.
Max, Pop, Jen and Jake are deer hunting.theme: survival under supernatural threat; group dynamics vs individual survival; human resilience in the face of chaos

In London Jenny, an aspiring ballet danc.coa:
An Italian impresario discovers an aspiring ballet dancer in London.
The dancer goes to Venice to be trained as a great dancer.
A visiting nobleman becomes fascinated with her and engages her to perform at his state opera house, hoping also to make her his mistress.
The British authorities encourage the dancer to go to Stuttgart and live extravagantly at the nobleman's expense.
In London Jenny, an aspiring ballet danc.outcomes: Protagonist successfully navigates complex web of alliances and obligations, but ultimately achieves escape from immediate danger. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as she adapts to new circumstances.
In London Jenny, an aspiring ballet danc.theme: ambition vs loyalty; cultural exchange; survival under adversity

The body of an Oxford professor is found.coa:
The body of an Oxford professor is found floating in the river Thames.
The investigation begins, with Scotland Yard taking charge.
An autopsy is performed, which reveals that the professor's death was caused by a karate blow to the head.
The missing Kytang Wafers are discovered to be gone from their storage location.
The body of an Oxford professor is found.outcomes: Scotland Yard solves the murder case and recovers the stolen Kytang Wafers. The conflict is resolved with a restored balance of power between Red China and Kytang, but broader geopolitical tensions remain.
The body of an Oxford professor is found.theme: power dynamics; cultural heritage; international relations

One morning, \"mouse-burger\" Melody \"M.coa: A person is diagnosed with laryngeal cancer. The diagnosis leads to being fired from their job and abandoned by their partner. With only two months to live, they spend their entire life's savings on a loft in Greenwich Village. They use credit cards to support themselves after realizing they won't have to pay the landlord.
One morning, \"mouse-burger\" Melody \"M.outcomes: Protagonist survives cancer diagnosis with newfound appreciation for life. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation through self-discovery and empowerment.
One morning, \"mouse-burger\" Melody \"M.theme: resilience; finding purpose; living in the present

Ralph, a famous screenwriter now in his.coa:
The screenwriter is diagnosed with a terminal illness.
He begins to reflect on his life and the choices he has made, particularly regarding his relationship with his son.
He decides to reconnect with his estranged son, hoping to make amends before it's too late.
This decision prompts him to reach out to his son through various means.
Ralph, a famous screenwriter now in his.outcomes: Protagonist achieves reconciliation with his son and dies with dignity. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and emotional closure.

Actress Terry London (played by Mirta Ma.coa:
Actress and producer visit an unnamed country in South America.
They are stalked by a female biker cult led by a man named Satán.
The cult murders the pregnant actress and her circle of friends.
The action is interrupted as the camera pulls out to show the crew shooting the scene.
The director is seen flirting with a female crew member, and they start kissing while being filmed.
The director suddenly assaults the woman, and proceeds to torture, kill, and disembowel her.
The crew assists him and keeps filming.
The film ends with the camera running out of stock.
Actress Terry London (played by Mirta Ma.outcomes: Protagonist dies at the hands of the antagonist. The conflict is unresolved with no personal transformation or social reintegration.
Actress Terry London (played by Mirta Ma.theme: violence; exploitation; the blurring of reality and fiction

Motor racing champion Joe Greer returns.coa:
Joe returns home to compete in an exhibition race featuring his younger brother. Joe interferes with his brother's relationship with Anne, causing estrangement between Joe and his brother. This interference also causes tension between Joe and his girlfriend Lee, who feels "not good enough" to be around Eddie. The strain on their relationships leads to a rift between Joe and Lee.

During the exhibition race, Spud Connors wrecks and is burned alive. Joe blames himself for the accident and becomes consumed by guilt while driving lap after lap through the flames and the smell of burning flesh. This traumatic experience causes Joe to lose his will to race.

Eddie goes on to win the exhibition race, marking a turning point in their careers. As Eddie's career rises, Joe's plummets. The power of love eventually helps to heal the rifts between Joe and those around him, including Lee and Eddie.
Motor racing champion Joe Greer returns.outcomes: Protagonist (Joe) fails in his racing career but eventually achieves personal redemption through love. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation, as Joe's relationships with Lee and Eddie are rehabilitated.

Sid, Russ and Jerry are three wannabe cr.coa: Three wannabe criminals plan and prepare for an armored-truck robbery despite previous failure at a jewelry store heist. The heist's bungling is exposed to the public, causing embarrassment and ridicule among the community. As the trio focuses on their next caper, their personal lives become increasingly chaotic due to family problems. A series of domestic disputes escalates into full-blown crises within each of their households.
Sid, Russ and Jerry are three wannabe cr.outcomes: Protagonists fail in their attempt at the armored-truck robbery and are left with no clear path forward. The conflict is unresolved with a sense of impending doom.

On a volcanic island near the kingdom of.coa:
Count Dakkar designs a submarine boat with his daughter and chief engineer Nicolai Roget.
The submarine is tested on its initial voyage, piloted by Roget.
Baron Falon overruns the island, taking control from Count Dakkar.
Falon tortures Dakkar to reveal his discoveries, but Dakkar's loyal men intervene, allowing him to escape.
On a volcanic island near the kingdom of.outcomes: Protagonist survives torture and achieves escape from immediate danger. The conflict is unresolved with broader threats remaining.
On a volcanic island near the kingdom of.theme: survival under adversity; power vs benevolence; discovery of the unknown

On one of those rare days when all is qu.coa: A mild-mannered toy salesman visits Maigret at the Quai des Orfèvres with concerns about being poisoned or going crazy. The salesman's wife arrives shortly after, leaving Maigret perplexed. Despite an order not to investigate, Maigret decides to look into the matter due to his own marital worries. A body is eventually discovered, surprising everyone involved.
On one of those rare days when all is qu.outcomes: Protagonist uncovers the truth behind the citizen's suspicions and resolves the case. The conflict is resolved with justice served but underlying personal issues remain.

The film's main storyline follows the li.coa: Otík works as a truck driver assistant to Mr. Pávek in a tight-knit village community. The two coworkers become at odds over Otík's inability to perform tasks, leading Pávek to demand that Otík be transferred to assist another driver, Turek. Otík decides to accept an offer of employment in Prague instead, but finds he doesn't fit into city life.
The film's main storyline follows the li.outcomes: Protagonist Otík achieves reconciliation with antagonist Pávek and returns to their work together. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as Otík adapts to his limitations and finds a new path.
The film's main storyline follows the li.theme: acceptance; community support; resilience in adversity

In Naples the Inspector Rizzo, nicknamed.coa:
A gang of drug traffickers from Marseille arrives in Naples. They use frozen fish as a means to peddle their drugs. The Inspector defeats the gang with the help of Manomozza, a boss of the Neapolitan underworld. After the arrest, it is revealed that Manomozza had been playing both sides and intends to forge an alliance with the traffickers.

Manomozza's betrayal is discovered by the Inspector, who then receives a tip from a friend about the planned meeting between the drug dealers. The Inspector finds out the place and time of the meeting.
In Naples the Inspector Rizzo, nicknamed.outcomes: Protagonist successfully apprehends drug dealers, resolving the immediate conflict. The conflict is resolved with personal victory but broader threats remain.
In Naples the Inspector Rizzo, nicknamed.theme: loyalty vs duty; corruption in power; justice vs organized crime

Thirty-nine-year old divorcée Louise Har.coa: Louise Harrington receives an application from F. Scott Feinstadt to attend Columbia University School of the Arts. She calls F. Scott to arrange an interview, which she attends. During the interview, she is unnerved by his appearance and mannerisms, which closely resemble those of her former high school sweetheart who was killed in a car crash. Hours after meeting, Louise and F. Scott embark upon an affair.
Thirty-nine-year old divorcée Louise Har.outcomes: Protagonist's life is transformed by the rekindled relationship, but also exposed to underlying issues in her personal life. The conflict is unresolved due to the presence of external threats and internal turmoil.
Thirty-nine-year old divorcée Louise Har.theme: reincarnation; love vs deception; identity crisis

The story of the painter Antonio Ligabue.coa: Little Antonio is entrusted to adoptive parents. He immediately begins to have psychophysical disorders, ill with rickets. He is expelled from school and attacks his mother, leading to hospitalizations in an asylum multiple times. During these periods of confinement, he finds comfort in painting and sculpture.
The story of the painter Antonio Ligabue.outcomes: Protagonist achieves recognition and appreciation for his art, despite initial criticism as a 'naive artist'. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation through creative expression.
The story of the painter Antonio Ligabue.theme: artistic expression; identity formation; overcoming adversity

Allan, an idealistic engineer, wants to.coa: An idealistic engineer proposes building a tunnel at the bottom of the Atlantic Ocean to connect North America with Europe within a few years. The project is deemed unfeasible due to financial constraints, and construction begins despite these limitations. Several disasters occur during the construction process, particularly in a segment dug under a mountain. The series of mishaps leads to an army of workers revolting against their employer.
Allan, an idealistic engineer, wants to.outcomes: Protagonist fails in his endeavor and faces universal hatred. The conflict is unresolved with a sense of futility and obsolescence.
Allan, an idealistic engineer, wants to.theme: ambition vs reality; hubris; progress vs stagnation

The film follows Tommy, a suspended youn.coa:
A young seaman is suspended from duty and moves into a New York neighborhood.
The neighborhood is controlled by a gang called the Souls who steal and rob at will.
No one presses charges against the Souls due to fear of retribution.
The young seaman decides to take matters into his own hands to combat the growing violence.
The film follows Tommy, a suspended youn.outcomes: Protagonist successfully reduces violence in the neighborhood, but it is unclear if he has fully eradicated the problem. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation and community empowerment.
The film follows Tommy, a suspended youn.theme: community resilience; fighting against oppression; taking responsibility

Schlomo, an Ethiopian boy, is placed by.coa: A Christian woman places an Ethiopian boy with an Ethiopian Jewish woman whose child has died. This event is about to be followed by the airlift of the adoptive mother from a Sudanese refugee camp to Israel during Operation Moses in 1984. The airlift causes the boy to leave his birth mother, who tells him "Go, live, and become" as he departs.
Schlomo, an Ethiopian boy, is placed by.outcomes: Protagonist achieves integration into Israeli society despite carrying unresolved emotional baggage. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader identity questions remain.

A young Dutch woman packs her bags and l.coa:
A young Dutch woman packs her bags and leaves for Ireland.
She arrives in Ireland and puts out personal belongings and furniture on the pavement, which are quickly taken by people passing by from her window.
From her empty flat, she observes this scene before making a final gesture of taking off her wedding ring.
She then sets out to lead a solitary existence, wandering through the landscapes of Connemara.
A young Dutch woman packs her bags and l.outcomes: Protagonist successfully leaves her previous life behind and establishes a new, solitary existence in Ireland. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and relocation.
A young Dutch woman packs her bags and l.theme: rebirth; solitude vs connection; letting go

Ex-boxer Kevin \"Kid\" Collins is a drif.coa:
A drifter and an escapee from a mental hospital arrives in a desert town near Palm Springs.
He meets a widow who convinces him to help fix up her neglected estate and lets him sleep in a trailer out back.
Her acquaintance, an ex-cop claiming to have hatched a scheme to kidnap a rich man's child, shows up and recruits the drifter for his plan.
The drifter is initially reluctant but eventually persuaded by the ex-cop to execute the kidnapping.
Ex-boxer Kevin \"Kid\" Collins is a drif.outcomes: Protagonist participates in the kidnapping, but the outcome of this action is not specified. The conflict is unresolved with potential consequences for protagonist and others.

The film is a love story about a sevente.coa:
A seventeen-year-old saleswoman leaves her familial home and moves into West Berlin, drawn by its economic progress and brightness.
She initially dates Lord, a wealthy auto mechanic wannabe influenced by American movie heartthrobs.
She meets Hans, an unemployed auto mechanic who is image-conscious and aspiring to be trendy, but living in relative poverty.
She falls in love with Hans after being mesmerized by the glamour of West Berlin and Lord.
The film is a love story about a sevente.outcomes: Protagonist finds personal fulfillment through genuine relationships rather than material success. The conflict is resolved with a shift from superficiality to authenticity.
The film is a love story about a sevente.theme: authenticity vs superficiality; love conquers class differences; self-discovery

Upon being promoted to run the Statistic.coa: A top secret headquarters of French military intelligence is promoted to run its Statistical Section. The new leader begins investigating evidence used to convict someone of espionage, finding it flimsy at best. Further investigation reveals that the military and government doctored much of this evidence. This discovery leads to a realization that an actual spy remains unpunished within the military.
Upon being promoted to run the Statistic.outcomes: Protagonist succeeds in clearing Dreyfus' name, exposing government corruption and stopping the spy. The conflict is resolved with justice served and a wrong righted.
Upon being promoted to run the Statistic.theme: justice vs corruption; loyalty vs duty; truth vs power

Following the surrender of the great lea.coa:
The great leader surrenders.
Massai is captured and put on a prison train.
The train passes through Oklahoma, where Massai escapes.
Massai heads back to his homeland.
He arrives in his homeland and decides to settle down with his woman.
Following the surrender of the great lea.outcomes: Protagonist successfully evades capture and achieves escape. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing pursuit by antagonists.
Following the surrender of the great lea.theme: freedom vs captivity; redemption through self-determination; the struggle for identity

The 69-ton fishing vessel Jeonjinho fail.coa: The 69-ton fishing vessel fails to catch as much fish as its crew had hoped. The crew decides to smuggle thirty illegal immigrants into Korea to make more money. The Jeonjinho encounters heavy fog, rain, and waves on its return journey. The ship is also being chased by a South Korean Maritime Police vessel.
The 69-ton fishing vessel Jeonjinho fail.outcomes: Protagonist's crew is unsuccessful in smuggling operation, with the vessel being intercepted by authorities. The conflict is unresolved due to the presence of hidden immigrants and the danger they face.
The 69-ton fishing vessel Jeonjinho fail.theme: human trafficking; moral compromise; survival at sea

Terra Nostra is divided into three parts.coa: The novel begins with an event in Paris on July 14, 1999. This event sets off a chain reaction that leads to the rebirth of main characters in different ages. The characters are reborn in the 16th century, specifically around the unfinished El Escorial, where they must navigate their new surroundings and interact with one another.
Terra Nostra is divided into three parts.outcomes: Protagonist survives multiple rebirths and transformations. The conflict is unresolved with broader threats remaining.

Paula Tessier is a 40-year-old interior.coa:
Paula meets Philip, the 25-year-old son of one of her wealthy clients.
Philip falls in love with Paula and insists that the age difference will not matter.
Paula resists Philip's advances for a time, but eventually succumbs to his affections when Roger initiates another affair with one of his young "Maisies".
The relationship between Paula and Philip becomes public knowledge, causing her friends and business associates to disapprove of their May–December romance.
Paula Tessier is a 40-year-old interior.outcomes: Protagonist is left heartbroken and alone, having lost both her lover and her reputation. The conflict is unresolved with a twist of fate that highlights life's wry ironies.
Paula Tessier is a 40-year-old interior.theme: ageism; societal expectations vs personal desire; love vs social status

A 19th-century opera singer is murdered.coa: A 19th-century opera singer is murdered on-stage shortly before her forthcoming wedding. The murder is committed by Dr. Emmanuel Droz during a live performance. After being slain, the singer's body is spirited away to the inventor's remote villa. The singer is reanimated and forced to play the lead in a grim production staged to recreate her abduction.
A 19th-century opera singer is murdered.outcomes: Protagonist (Malvina) survives and regains memories through her interactions with Felisberto. The conflict is unresolved as Malvina's fate remains uncertain.

Emrah is a dreamer who hopes to be a gre.coa: Emrah tries to shoot his first feature film with funding from producers and help from friends and his mother. He is held back by bureaucratic obstacles. The main obstacle is an endorsement letter he needs from Müzeyyen, the head of the censorship board. Emrah's father disapproves of his filmmaking ambitions and wants him to become a pharmacist instead.
Emrah is a dreamer who hopes to be a gre.outcomes: Protagonist achieves partial success in pursuing filmmaking dreams despite setbacks and bureaucratic hurdles. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader creative challenges remain.
Emrah is a dreamer who hopes to be a gre.theme: artistic freedom; rebellion against authority; perseverance under adversity

FUBAR is the story of two lifelong frien.coa: Two lifelong friends grow up together, sharing experiences such as shotgunning their first beers and growing a distinctive hairstyle known as "Hockey Hair". A young documentarian decides to film their lives, exploring aspects of their friendship and maturity. The friends are snubbed by their party leader, leading to complications in their social dynamics.
FUBAR is the story of two lifelong frien.outcomes: Protagonist and friend survive the tragedy but are forever changed by their experience. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal struggle.

Suzanne Verrier, a dancer of mediocre ta.coa:
A dancer at the Paris Opéra begins an affair with an elderly aristocrat and conservative politician.
The politician uses his influence to assist her career as a dancer.
She starts a romance with a young, left-wing electrician who is also a union organiser.
He becomes elected to Parliament and appointed as a minister.
Suzanne Verrier, a dancer of mediocre ta.outcomes: Protagonist remains in a relationship with Comte de Montoire-Grandpré, sacrificing her romance with Gaillac. The conflict is unresolved with partial personal transformation as she adjusts to her circumstances.
Suzanne Verrier, a dancer of mediocre ta.theme: power dynamics; loyalty vs duty; compromise and sacrifice

In post-war East Germany, Peter Gottfrie.coa: The Communist regime decrees that all children of "dissidents" will be denied entry to a prestigious music conservatory. This decree creates anxiety for the son, who wants to attend the conservatory. The son prepares to answer the seven questions required by the conservatory, including one that would require him to deny his religious convictions. The son is invited to perform at the Berlin Youth Festival by the Socialist Unity Party.
In post-war East Germany, Peter Gottfrie.outcomes: Protagonist successfully defects to the West, escaping regime's control. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and escape from oppressive environment.
In post-war East Germany, Peter Gottfrie.theme: freedom vs oppression; loyalty vs duty; individual autonomy

In 2015, following Syriza's victory in t.coa: In 2015, a new government is elected and tasks its Minister of Finance with negotiating a new deal on a memorandum of understanding signed by the previous government. The Minister's proposals are met with flat refusals from the Troika in successive meetings of the Eurogroup. The Troika threatens to evict Greece from the Eurozone if their demands are not met, forcing the Prime Minister to sign the MoU despite public opposition. The Prime Minister signs the MoU, going against a significant portion of the population that rejected the bailouts.
In 2015, following Syriza's victory in t.outcomes: Protagonist fails to achieve his goal of a new deal and is forced out of office. The conflict is unresolved with the debt crisis looming and broader systemic issues remaining.
In 2015, following Syriza's victory in t.theme: powerlessness vs authority; compromise vs conviction; economic inequality

Kara ben Nemsi and his servant Halef Oma.coa:
A group of Persians headed to Baghdad are encountered by Kara ben Nemsi and his servant Halef Omar.
The Persians are attacked by their enemies, the Kurds.
Prince Hassan discovers there is a traitor among his people as a result of the attack.
Kara ben Nemsi offers to help guard the caravan on its journey to Baghdad in response to Prince Hassan's discovery.
Kara ben Nemsi and his servant Halef Oma.outcomes: Protagonist helps secure the caravan's journey, and the conflict with the Kurds is resolved. The conflict with the traitor remains unresolved.
Kara ben Nemsi and his servant Halef Oma.theme: loyalty vs duty; trust in strangers; protection of allies

Dr. Whitaker has disappeared after worki.coa:
Dr. Whitaker works on an innovation that could give one side of the Cold War an edge.
The scientist disappears after completing his work.
Lemmy Caution is assigned to find Dr. Whitaker despite being busy with other matters.
He is advised to start searching for Dr. Whitaker by finding Geraldine, the scientist's fiancée.
Dr. Whitaker has disappeared after worki.outcomes: Protagonist successfully completes his mission and returns the scientist. The conflict is resolved with conflict_resolved and personal transformation.

Officer Paul Almasy (Hall) is separated.coa:
Officer is separated from his unit behind enemy lines.
He hides in a hotel where chambermaid disguises him as a waiter.
Russian troops occupy the hotel and make it their headquarters.
The officer kills a Russian spy.
Officer Paul Almasy (Hall) is separated.outcomes: Protagonist is reunited with his unit after being helped by the chambermaid. The conflict is resolved with personal survival and bravery recognized.
Officer Paul Almasy (Hall) is separated.theme: survival under occupation; loyalty vs duty; human resilience

Antonio, a brazen, individualistic ETA t.coa: The ETA terrorist travels with two fellow cell members to Madrid to carry out an attack on a police station. They intend to use a car bomb containing 100 kg of explosives. The terrorist moves into the area under the guise of a photographer, and meets his neighbor, a naive prostitute with an impending drug problem. He falls for her and uses her desire to have their first tryst in Granada as an excuse to flee Madrid right after he shoots a police officer.
Antonio, a brazen, individualistic ETA t.outcomes: Protagonist dies in the explosion, sacrificing himself for love and possibly redemption. The conflict is unresolved as broader threats remain.
Antonio, a brazen, individualistic ETA t.theme: self-destruction; loyalty vs duty; human cost of terrorism

Alex is an aspiring actress, working as.coa: An aspiring actress agrees to house-sit a friend's home for the weekend to earn extra money. The friends warn her about a recent robbery and murder at the neighboring house, which she downplays. A stranger, Mickey, arrives at the house, and despite initial unease, Alex finds herself drawn to him. He takes her captive, revealing his true intentions as a thief.
Alex is an aspiring actress, working as.outcomes: Protagonist is held captive by stranger, but ultimately escapes or resolves situation. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation as protagonist faces her fears and vulnerabilities.
Alex is an aspiring actress, working as.theme: vulnerability; captivity vs freedom; human connection

Maia, a single mother, lives in Montreal.coa: On Christmas Eve, an unexpected delivery arrives at Maia's home containing notebooks, tapes, and photos she sent to her best friend from 1980s Beirut. Maia refuses to open the box or confront its memories, but Alex secretly begins exploring its contents. As Alex delves deeper into the box, she starts to enter a world that blurs fantasy and reality, transporting her to her mother's tumultuous adolescence during the Lebanese civil war. This entry into her mother's past unlocks mysteries of a hidden past.
Maia, a single mother, lives in Montreal.outcomes: Protagonist Maia is forced to confront her repressed memories and emotions. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation as Maia begins to heal and reconnect with her daughter.
Maia, a single mother, lives in Montreal.theme: reconciliation; intergenerational trauma; the power of memory

Harold Horne, an ambitious shoe salesman.coa: Horne meets Barbara and tells her he's a millionaire leather tycoon. He spends time around Barbara hiding his true circumstances in the shoe store. Horne accidentally stows away aboard a ship, trying to evade the crew by hiding in a mailbag. The mailbag is taken off the ship and falls onto a delivery cart, which then hoists it upwards with a window cleaner's cradle.
Harold Horne, an ambitious shoe salesman.outcomes: Protagonist survives the ordeal with no lasting physical harm. The conflict is resolved with a fortunate accident.
Harold Horne, an ambitious shoe salesman.theme: deception; social class; chance vs control

Don Fabijan (Krešimir Mikić) is a young.coa: A young priest comes to serve on a small island in the Adriatic. He decides to pierce condoms before they are sold to increase the birth rate on the island. The priest teams up with the newsagent and the pharmacist to implement this plan. They abolish all forms of birth control on the entire island, making it impossible for residents to access any form of contraception.
Don Fabijan (Krešimir Mikić) is a young.outcomes: Protagonist's actions lead to unintended and complex outcomes for the island community. The conflict is unresolved with long-term social and demographic implications.
Don Fabijan (Krešimir Mikić) is a young.theme: unintended consequences; moral absolutism vs pragmatism; population control

In Italy, two children, Michael (Martin.coa:
The mother leaves the children in Italy.
The children decide to run away to fetch their mother at the Villa Fiorita.
They sell Debbie's pony to afford the journey and arrive at the Villa.
Lorenzo informs the father that the children have run away, but allows them to stay after Michael falls ill.
In Italy, two children, Michael (Martin.outcomes: Protagonist Michael survives the ordeal and is reunited with his mother in England. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as Moira returns home with her children.
In Italy, two children, Michael (Martin.theme: family dynamics; loyalty vs duty; parental responsibility

Jeanne is a French essayist who writes t.coa: Jeanne travels around the world writing travelogues and arrives at an ancient forest with her assistant Hana to research a mythical herb called "vision". She is determined to find the herb because it is said to alleviate human pain when its spores scatter every 997 years. Jeanne meets Tomo, a mountain guardian who lives in the area covered in cedar trees, as she arrives at the ancient forest. The two begin to develop a rapport during their search for the herb.
Jeanne is a French essayist who writes t.outcomes: Protagonist Jeanne successfully finds a deeper understanding of the local culture and forms meaningful connections with the mountain guardians. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation through cross-cultural understanding and empathy.
Jeanne is a French essayist who writes t.theme: cultural exchange; human connection; transcending boundaries

A synopsis prior to the release of the f.coa:
Ana turns 18 years old.
As she approaches adulthood, a countdown begins in her mind, echoing the numbers 10, 9, 8, 7...
The countdown accelerates, reaching 0, which triggers Ana's memories of other young women who died tragically at the age of 22.
These memories reveal that their lives were cut short by male tyranny.
A synopsis prior to the release of the f.outcomes: Protagonist Ana achieves self-awareness and liberation from the influence of others' lives. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and a shift towards individuality.
A synopsis prior to the release of the f.theme: collective trauma; shared existence; female empowerment

Roy, a sensitive twenty-something man, a.coa: A macho, ex-army officer attempts to force a male identity upon his sensitive twenty-something son. The son gradually determines that they are actually trans. Rebelling against the father's attempts, the son leaves home and establishes a new life in a far away town under the name Wendy. In this new environment, Wendy flourishes but is constantly concerned about their secret being discovered.
Roy, a sensitive twenty-something man, a.outcomes: Protagonist successfully navigates transition and establishes a stable life under her chosen identity. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social acceptance.
Roy, a sensitive twenty-something man, a.theme: self-discovery; identity vs expectation; resilience in the face of adversity

Haunted by the memory of Walter Holderli.coa: A soldier is killed in World War I, leaving behind a letter with an address that Paul finds on the body. Paul confesses to a priest about killing the soldier and receives absolution. Using the address from the letter, Paul travels to Germany to find the soldier's family. Dr. Holderlin initially refuses to welcome Paul into his home due to anti-French sentiment.
Haunted by the memory of Walter Holderli.outcomes: Protagonist finds a sense of belonging and redemption through his relationship with the Holderlin family. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation, as Paul chooses to forgo easing his conscience and stay with his new family.
Haunted by the memory of Walter Holderli.theme: guilt vs forgiveness; redemption through relationships; confronting past trauma

Colin Diamond is a successful car salesm.coa:
Colin discovers his wife is having an affair.
He has an emotional breakdown due to this discovery.
His friends convince him to kidnap his wife's lover in order to help him cope with his emotions.
They lock the lover in a cupboard and threaten him.
Colin Diamond is a successful car salesm.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a sense of resolution and reconciliation with himself, but the emotional impact of his wife's infidelity remains. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation.
Colin Diamond is a successful car salesm.theme: revenge vs redemption; shame and torment; inner turmoil

Stan and Ollie are about to attend a pok.coa: The two men receive a telephone call telling them their absence is holding up a game. They tell their wives they have a business engagement at the Orpheum Theater and sneak off to the game instead. En route, they stop to assist two young ladies retrieve a hat that has blown under a parked car, but end up getting soaked by a passing street-cleaning vehicle while trying to do so. The girls invite them up to their apartment while their clothes dry.
Stan and Ollie are about to attend a pok.outcomes: Protagonists survive and manage to avoid immediate consequences for their deception. The conflict is unresolved as the wives discover the truth and chase them out.
Stan and Ollie are about to attend a pok.theme: deception; loyalty vs duty; human fallibility

Frustrated by the repetitious grind of o.coa: Rick experiences frustration with one-night stands and aimless hustling, leading him to seek meaning in his life. He sells small amounts of marijuana to cover expenses, insisting he's not doing it for a living. Rick meets Heather, the girl next door, and perceives a new opportunity for a committed relationship. This sparks tension between Rick and his friends, who are threatened by his desire to settle down.
Frustrated by the repetitious grind of o.outcomes: Protagonist fails to establish a committed relationship due to unresolved personal issues. The conflict is unresolved with potential for future growth and change.
Frustrated by the repetitious grind of o.theme: searching for identity; struggle between old and new values; tension between desire for stability and need for self-expression

Celestino (Amiel Cayo), a Quechua peasan.coa: The mother and wife die, leaving behind a daughter and her mother-in-law. The daughter wants to move to her aunt's place in search of a better life. Celestino is left alone with his cow Samichay, who provides him with milk but shows no signs of producing offspring despite his hopes that she will have a calf. Celestino decides to sell Samichay because the cow is malnourished and has lost weight.
Celestino (Amiel Cayo), a Quechua peasan.outcomes: Protagonist's daughter leaves for her aunt's place, and protagonist is left alone with his cow. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as protagonist adapts to urbanization.

Lord Greystoke (Tarzan) returns to Afric.coa:
Lord Greystoke returns to Africa with Lady Jane and friend Albert Werper.
Werper's true intentions are revealed: he wants the treasure of Opar for its gold value, not to help Lord Greystoke secure his estates.
Achmet Zek, an Arab slave trader, is introduced as a rival who seeks revenge against Lord Greystoke and Lady Jane.
Zek forms an alliance with Werper to obtain the treasure.
Lord Greystoke (Tarzan) returns to Afric.outcomes: Protagonist secures the treasure and returns to England. The conflict is resolved with personal success but broader threats remain.
Lord Greystoke (Tarzan) returns to Afric.theme: greed vs loyalty; power of revenge; colonial legacy

Actor Hans Wieland refuses to divorce hi.coa:
The Nazi authorities apply extreme pressure on Hans Wieland to divorce his Jewish wife.
Hans Wieland refuses to comply, despite the pressure.
He takes his wife to a premiere of one of his films, where she is introduced to a high Nazi Party official who turns out to be charming and Jewish.
The official's true identity is later discovered, and he orders her arrest.
Actor Hans Wieland refuses to divorce hi.outcomes: Protagonist dies alongside his Jewish wife. The conflict is unresolved as the broader Nazi threat remains.
Actor Hans Wieland refuses to divorce hi.theme: resistance vs oppression; love vs duty; human sacrifice

Soviet intelligence officer Aleksei Fedo.coa: A Soviet intelligence officer departs for German-occupied Vinnytsia. The officer's radio operator is sent ahead, but is executed by the enemy. This leads to the officer being forced to search for an alternative contact through the local underground. While searching, the officer accidentally discovers that one of the underground workers is a provocateur.
Soviet intelligence officer Aleksei Fedo.outcomes: Protagonist fails in his mission but survives with knowledge of the provocateur's identity. The conflict is unresolved due to protagonist's failure and the continued presence of the provocateur.
Soviet intelligence officer Aleksei Fedo.theme: deception; loyalty vs duty; trust in strangers

The story begins in the United States, w.coa:
The heroine suffers a mental breakdown after the death by drowning of her young son.
She recovers from the breakdown.
Her husband is reported lost at sea.
She learns about his disappearance while recovering from her breakdown.
Having acquired a fortune, she decides to launch an expedition to search for him.
She leads the expedition herself and it trails into the Australian hinterland.
The story begins in the United States, w.outcomes: Protagonist finds her husband and achieves reunion. The conflict is resolved with a happy ending, but the story does not specify any significant personal transformation or change.
The story begins in the United States, w.theme: hope in adversity; love transcends loss; resilience

Nineteen-year-old Anne escapes from pris.coa: Anne escapes from prison. While escaping, she injures her foot and fractures her talus bone. She meets Julien, who takes her in and looks after her while she recovers. As she heals, Anne falls deeply in love with Julien.
Nineteen-year-old Anne escapes from pris.outcomes: Protagonist (Anne) survives physical injury and emotional turmoil. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation and further entanglement in crime.
Nineteen-year-old Anne escapes from pris.theme: love vs survival; exploitation of vulnerability; consequences of desperation

Josef von Sternberg directed, photograph.coa:
Twelve Japanese seamen become stranded on an abandoned island after their ship sinks.
The island's only inhabitants are the overseer and an attractive young Japanese woman.
A former warrant officer maintains discipline among the seamen, but his authority is challenged when he suffers a catastrophic loss of face.
This event sparks a shift from discipline to a struggle for power.
Josef von Sternberg directed, photograph.outcomes: The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or transformation. The protagonist's status is failure as they are unable to escape their situation or achieve any significant change.
Josef von Sternberg directed, photograph.theme: power struggle; discipline vs chaos; human nature under duress

This is the story of a young clerk who h.coa:
A young clerk decides to enter a six-day bicycle race.
He does poorly in the early stages of the race, struggling to keep up with the other competitors.
His poor performance is witnessed by his girlfriend, who is embarrassed by his lack of skill.
The embarrassment motivates him to work harder and improve his chances of winning.
This is the story of a young clerk who h.outcomes: Protagonist succeeds in winning the bicycle race and achieving a romantic relationship. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation and personal success.
This is the story of a young clerk who h.theme: love conquers all; perseverance pays off; self-improvement through achievement

Yuriko Ono is a young promising dancer i.coa:
A young dancer is invited to study ballet at the Bolshoi Theatre in Moscow, providing an opportunity for professional growth.
The dancer falls in love with a local sculptor while studying in Moscow and wins a competition of graduates from the theatre.
The dancer receives a diagnosis of blood cancer, which abruptly halts their pursuit of art.
The dancer struggles with the legacy of war and dies in the arms of the sculptor in a hospital.
Yuriko Ono is a young promising dancer i.outcomes: Protagonist dies from illness, unable to pursue art career. The conflict is unresolved with no resolution or change.
Yuriko Ono is a young promising dancer i.theme: tragic fate; artistic passion vs mortality; legacy of war

A vulture hacks at the protagonist's fee.coa:
A vulture hacks at the protagonist's feet.
The protagonist tries to drive the vulture away, but eventually stops when he realizes it is about to attack his face.
A man passing by asks the protagonist why he doesn't do anything about the vulture.
The onlooker offers to go get a gun to shoot the vulture and the protagonist asks him to hurry.
A vulture hacks at the protagonist's fee.outcomes: Protagonist dies from vulture attack. The conflict is unresolved with no resolution or change.

A wealthy industrialist François Donge i.coa:
The industrialist becomes seriously ill in a hospital due to apparent food poisoning.
He begins to suspect that his wife is responsible for the poisoning.
He reflects on their marriage, recalling moments of tension and unhappiness.
His memories reveal a troubled relationship marked by infidelity and emotional distance.
A wealthy industrialist François Donge i.outcomes: Protagonist survives the attempted poisoning and achieves a deeper understanding of his marriage dynamics. The conflict is resolved with personal insight but broader issues remain.

The film deals with the Italian resistan.coa:
German troops occupy the country on 8 September 1943.
The protagonist's brother is involved with the resistance and needs her support.
As the occupation intensifies, the protagonist goes underground to join the resistance.
She meets Gina Moncigoli and Pierina Bonilauri while fighting in the resistance.
The film deals with the Italian resistan.outcomes: Protagonist Malavasi emerges from the war experience transformed and empowered. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social change.
The film deals with the Italian resistan.theme: emancipation; resistance against oppression; women's empowerment

A young Russian woman (Chatterton) marri.coa:
A young woman marries a wealthy man from a different country.
She has a child with him.
She begins an affair with one of his acquaintances.
The affair is discovered, leading to her being forced to leave the country where she lives with her husband.
She moves to Paris and starts a new life.
Many years pass.
Her daughter seeks her out, needing assistance.
A young Russian woman (Chatterton) marri.outcomes: Protagonist is separated from her family but finds a new life in Paris. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation, but underlying issues remain.
A young Russian woman (Chatterton) marri.theme: family secrets; loyalty vs duty; redemption

Cole works as a nursing aide at an elder.coa:
The nursing aide works at an elderly care facility in rural Appalachia, redistributing excess medication from residents to local buyers as a side job.
This redistribution of medication helps keep addicts out of the path of the town's dominant drug dealer.
A childhood friend returns after years away with plans to enter the local drug trade.
The friend's return and intentions put pressure on the nursing aide's double life.
Cole works as a nursing aide at an elder.outcomes: Protagonist's double life is exposed, leading to a confrontation with his childhood friend and potential consequences from the authorities. The conflict is unresolved with personal risk and relational strain.
Cole works as a nursing aide at an elder.theme: double lives; loyalty vs obligation; moral gray areas

Mordecai C. Jones (Scott) – a self-style.coa: A young man deserts from the United States Army. He meets a confidence trickster who makes his living defrauding people in the Southern United States using tricks such as rigged punchboards and playing cards. The two form a team to make money together, engaging in various scams. They steal a truck loaded with moonshine whiskey that they sell for profit.
Mordecai C. Jones (Scott) – a self-style.outcomes: Protagonist remains unrepentant and unchanged in his ways. The conflict is unresolved with no personal transformation or social reintegration.
Mordecai C. Jones (Scott) – a self-style.theme: moral ambiguity; the nature of identity; the American Dream

The film attempts to portray the crazine.coa: The Mothers of Invention go on tour. While on tour, they visit the small town of Centerville. They wander around and get beaten up in "Redneck Eats", a cowboy bar. This leads to an animated interlude that is passed off as a "dental hygiene movie".
The film attempts to portray the crazine.outcomes: The protagonist's music career is marked by turmoil and disorganization. The conflict is unresolved with no clear resolution or change.

Luigi \"The Chinaman\" Maietto escapes f.coa:
Luigi "The Chinaman" Maietto escapes from prison.
Maietto assigns two henchmen to murder the inspector who testified against him, leading to his imprisonment.
The henchmen attempt to kill the inspector, but he survives.
The local newspapers cover up the failed assassination and report that the inspector was killed.
Luigi \"The Chinaman\" Maietto escapes f.outcomes: Protagonist is still at large and has evaded capture. The conflict is unresolved with a personal transformation of the protagonist, who has adapted to his circumstances but remains a fugitive.

The film begins with the contorted body.coa:
A young woman lies contorted in a ditch covered in frost.
The camera is turned over to an unseen interviewer who speaks with the last people to see her and the one who found her body.
The story flashes back to the woman walking along a roadside, hiding from the police as she tries to get a ride.
She takes up with various vagabonds, including a Tunisian vineyard worker, a family of goat farmers, an agronomy professor, and a maid.
The film begins with the contorted body.outcomes: Protagonist Mona dies from exposure and hypothermia, marking the end of her journey. The conflict is unresolved with no resolution or change.
The film begins with the contorted body.theme: freedom vs responsibility; escape from societal expectations; human fragility

On 16 January 1944, a reconnaissance pil.coa:
A plane crashes on 16 January 1944 in German-occupied Belgium.
The wounded pilot is found by a local boy and taken to the house of two members of the Belgian Resistance.
The pilot recovers and informs them that he needs to retrieve a book of codes, but it's guarded by the Nazis.
The pilot and one of the Resistance members fall in love with each other.
On 16 January 1944, a reconnaissance pil.outcomes: Protagonist is captured by the Nazis. The conflict is unresolved with tragic consequences.
On 16 January 1944, a reconnaissance pil.theme: betrayal; love vs duty; survival under occupation

While travelling, Hercules is asked to i.coa:
Hercules is asked to intervene in a quarrel between two brothers over who should rule Thebes.
The brothers' quarrel distracts Hercules, allowing him to drink from a magic spring.
Drinking from the magic spring hypnotizes Hercules and causes him to lose his memory.
A harem girl's dance induces a trance-like state in Hercules.
While travelling, Hercules is asked to i.outcomes: Protagonist regains his memory and resolves conflicts through heroic actions. The conflict is resolved with conflict_resolved and personal transformation as the two brothers kill each other, allowing for a new ruler to be elected.
While travelling, Hercules is asked to i.theme: memory loss; captivity vs freedom; loyalty vs duty

Hit man Eric O'Byrne (Matthew Modine) ha.coa: A hit is ordered by the mob. A hit man travels to Miami to carry out the order. He poses as a stuntman in Lance's world to get close to the target. He also starts a romantic relationship with Lance's daughter. The hit man gains the trust of his target and becomes more entrenched in his life. Time draws near for the hit, causing tension within the hit man.
Hit man Eric O'Byrne (Matthew Modine) ha.outcomes: Protagonist fails to carry out hit and instead escapes danger. The conflict is unresolved with a personal transformation for the protagonist.
Hit man Eric O'Byrne (Matthew Modine) ha.theme: morality vs loyalty; redemption through sacrifice; human capacity for change

Young, promising violinist Joe Bonaparte.coa:
A young violinist decides to earn money as a boxer despite risking hand injuries.
He wins match after match, convincing his manager to let him continue fighting.
His conscience starts bothering him and he questions his decision to enter boxing.
The manager's girl is dispatched to convince him to keep fighting. 
Gangster Eddie Fuseli buys the manager's share of the operation, turning Joe into a hard-hearted boxer.
Joe enters the semi-final match against Chocolate Drop determined to win.
He knocks out his opponent in the second round, killing him.
Both he and Lorna's attitudes change after the fight.
He retires from boxing and returns with Lorna to his father.
Young, promising violinist Joe Bonaparte.outcomes: Protagonist survives the consequences of his actions and transitions back to a stable path with renewed focus on music. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and relational change through reconciliation with his father and Lorna.
Young, promising violinist Joe Bonaparte.theme: redemption; sacrifice vs ambition; human fallibility

La Chèvre features Depardieu as tough-gu.coa: A rich businessman hires a private detective to find his missing daughter who has disappeared while vacationing in Mexico. The case is complicated because several previous attempts to find her have failed. A psychologist suggests sending someone equally unlucky and accident-prone to follow the daughter's steps, which the detective agrees to despite being skeptical of the plan's success. An accountant, chosen for his clumsiness, is sent on the mission while being led to believe he is in charge.
La Chèvre features Depardieu as tough-gu.outcomes: Protagonist Campana successfully finds and returns the missing daughter Marie. The conflict is resolved with a successful outcome.
La Chèvre features Depardieu as tough-gu.theme: chance vs control; fate vs free will; redemption through perseverance

Fifteen years after the mysterious murde.coa: A young girl named Cassie's father asks her to stop by an auto shop for an oil change. She meets the owner, who is trying to become a mechanical engineer for the Marines, and he agrees to take her back to her high school when she leaves her car behind. At the high school, Cassie tells him about her father, and he expresses admiration for how much she cares about him. This conversation leads to Cassie learning that her father is having an affair with another teacher at the school.
Fifteen years after the mysterious murde.outcomes: Protagonist Daniel finds some emotional closure through his connection with Cassie. The conflict is unresolved with ongoing personal struggles and family dynamics.

Having buried his father in the previous.coa:
Zuckerman buries his father.
The burial causes him to face middle age and an undiagnosable pain.
The pain keeps him from his writing regime and leads to boredom due to inactivity.
The combination of pain and boredom allows his mind to wander anxiously over past memories.
Having buried his father in the previous.outcomes: Protagonist transitions into a new path of self-discovery and potential healing. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation and uncertain future.
Having buried his father in the previous.theme: midlife crisis; nostalgia vs ambition; health vs creativity

After con artist Joe Donan accidentally.coa: A con artist accidentally kills his father during a sting when he uses live ammunition instead of blanks. He tries to fulfill his father's dying wish by recovering "the cake". To do so, he seeks out his uncle, also a con artist, who attempts to recruit him for one of his cons. The con artist falls in love with the uncle's assistant's girlfriend and decides to take the money from the con and run away with her.
After con artist Joe Donan accidentally.outcomes: Protagonist survives and escapes immediate danger but is left with unresolved conflict and personal transformation due to learning about his family's deceit. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader issues remain.
After con artist Joe Donan accidentally.theme: deception; loyalty vs duty; consequences of actions

As the film begins, Mademoiselle is show.coa: Mademoiselle opens floodgates to inundate the village, causing destruction and chaos. She sets fire to houses and poisons the drinking troughs, resulting in the death of farm animals. The villagers suspect an Italian woodcutter, who is accused out of prejudice, and he becomes the target of their anger. Mademoiselle falsely denounces the woodcutter, leading to his brutal murder by the villagers.
As the film begins, Mademoiselle is show.outcomes: Protagonist escapes punishment and leaves village, while her victim's son remains aware of her guilt. The conflict is unresolved with a sense of injustice and ongoing threat.
As the film begins, Mademoiselle is show.theme: prejudice; manipulation; justice vs mob rule

Dutch documentary film director, Jan Hol.coa:
A documentary film director travels to the Czech Republic to make a film about curing alcoholism.
He attends an Alcoholics Anonymous meeting and meets a man who becomes his friend.
The friend has another acquaintance who suddenly becomes mute.
The friend decides to take this person on tour with the band Čechomor in order to help him recover.
Dutch documentary film director, Jan Hol.outcomes: Protagonist witnesses and documents the tour's unconventional approach to healing. The conflict is unresolved with many questions remaining about the efficacy of this method.
Dutch documentary film director, Jan Hol.theme: healing through art; unconventional approaches to recovery; the power of human connection

Daniel Shore, a 28-year-old German Ameri.coa:
The narrator returns from Morocco after a trip intended to escape work-related troubles and his unhappy life.
Upon returning, the narrator's mental state becomes unstable due to unresolved issues from the trip.
A boy is killed in Morocco during the narrator's stay, leading the narrator to suspect involvement in the crime.
The narrator's guilt over the incident causes them to be haunted by images of what happened.
Daniel Shore, a 28-year-old German Ameri.outcomes: Protagonist is unable to escape his guilt and trauma, leading to a breakdown. The conflict is unresolved with personal transformation but broader emotional turmoil remains.

The film is about a projection-equipment.coa: A man drives his car into a river in a half-hearted suicide attempt. A projection-equipment repair mechanic picks up the man and allows him to ride with him while his clothes dry. The two encounter several people in various states of despair, including a man whose wife has committed suicide by driving her car into a tree. The depressed man berates his elderly father for disrespecting his mother.
The film is about a projection-equipment.outcomes: Protagonist Robert's immediate emotional state stabilizes through interactions with Bruno and others. The conflict is unresolved as broader issues of despair and exploitation remain.
The film is about a projection-equipment.theme: despair; isolation vs connection; human resilience

In a rural village, the tyrannical Jonas.coa:
The tyrannical leader disappears one night, causing widespread speculation about his fate.
It is widely believed that his eldest daughter has murdered him.
A new investigating judge arrives in the village and begins an investigation into the disappearance.
He becomes romantically involved with the suspected murderer.
In a rural village, the tyrannical Jonas.outcomes: Protagonist (judge) resolves conflict through discovery of truth, but broader social dynamics remain unchanged. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation for the judge, but systemic issues persist.
In a rural village, the tyrannical Jonas.theme: justice vs mob mentality; redemption through forgiveness; love vs duty

9-year-old Elias and his father are goin.coa:
Elias and his father go for a walk in Berlin.
Elias loses sight of his father while they are walking.
Elias starts searching for his father on his own.
Many people help Elias with his search and teach him basic German lessons.
9-year-old Elias and his father are goin.outcomes: Protagonist finds his father through the help of strangers and successfully communicates with them in German. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and adaptation to a new environment.
9-year-old Elias and his father are goin.theme: cultural immersion; language acquisition; resilience in unfamiliar situations

Many years ago, in Lapland, a boy named.coa: A family is killed in an accident, leaving a boy orphaned. The heads of the families in the village meet to decide the boy's future due to the difficulty of life in the arctic. They decide to raise him communally, with each family taking care of him for one year before moving him on to the next. This communal arrangement becomes a tradition, and the boy begins whittling toys as a gift for the families that cared for him each Christmas.
Many years ago, in Lapland, a boy named.outcomes: Protagonist grows into Santa Claus, embodying the spirit of Christmas. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and social integration through tradition.

Layla is a young Dutch Muslim of Morocca.coa:
A young Muslim woman is born and raised in Amsterdam but faces daily Islamophobia and racism.
She starts to rebel against her assimilated family and moves towards Islamic fundamentalism after watching short films about Syria and Gaza on the internet.
The ban on wearing burqas strengthens her resolve to wear one, which angers her family further.
She meets a young radical and decides to marry him.
Layla is a young Dutch Muslim of Morocca.outcomes: Protagonist's radicalism is tested as she struggles to adjust to a patriarchal society abroad. The conflict is partially resolved with personal transformation but broader issues of extremism remain.
Layla is a young Dutch Muslim of Morocca.theme: radicalization; cultural identity; extremism vs hypocrisy

A young woman from the country comes to.coa:
The young woman moves to the city to work as a maid in a wealthy household.
She begins working for the family, interacting with its members daily.
She starts an affair with one of the family's children, who is known to be unreliable.
He gets her pregnant, causing tension and conflict within the household.

The child's behavior worsens, but he eventually reforms after realizing the impact of his actions on others.
The young woman gives birth to a child, solidifying their relationship.
They decide to get married in a ceremony attended by family members.
A young woman from the country comes to.outcomes: Protagonist achieves a stable relationship and starts a new life through marriage. The conflict is resolved with relational transformation and personal growth.

The Provençal town of Tarascon is so ent.coa: The town's inhabitants notice that no game lives near Tarascon due to its enthusiastic hunting culture and resort to telling stories and throwing caps in the air as a substitute. A middle-aged man is mistaken for planning a real hunting expedition to Algeria after reacting enthusiastically to seeing an Atlas lion in a menagerie. The townspeople pressure him into going, and he gathers an absurd amount of equipment and weapons. He sets off on the boat from Marseille to Algiers.
The Provençal town of Tarascon is so ent.outcomes: Protagonist survives the expedition and returns home with a fabricated tale of adventure. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as protagonist gains notoriety for his supposed bravery.

On Christmas Eve, Della Young discovers.coa:
Della Young discovers she has only $1.87 to buy a present for her husband on Christmas Eve.
She visits Madame Sofronie's shop and sells her long hair for $20.
Della uses the money to buy a pocket watch chain for Jim.
Jim comes home from work that evening, unaware of Della's sacrifice.
Della admits to Jim that she sold her hair to buy him the chain.
Jim gives Della a set of ornamental combs as his gift.
Della gives Jim the watch chain, and he reveals that he sold the watch to buy the combs.
On Christmas Eve, Della Young discovers.outcomes: Protagonist Della and Jim experience mutual understanding and appreciation of their love despite the impractical gifts. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation through selfless acts of love.
On Christmas Eve, Della Young discovers.theme: sacrificial love; resourcefulness in adversity; value beyond material possessions

The grief-stricken Captain Fritz Schoell.coa: The terminally ill wife of the Captain dies after euthanasia is carried out with his assistance. The Captain's funeral is attended by a party of men who whisk him away for a briefing on a special mission he will lead. The party, consisting of several skilled commandos, briefs the Captain on their mission to assassinate Allied leaders in Casablanca. They parachute into Morocco, where they are met by an Arab woman named Faddja Hassen, who guides them towards their target.
The grief-stricken Captain Fritz Schoell.outcomes: Protagonist successfully completes the mission and eliminates the target. The conflict is resolved with a decisive outcome.
The grief-stricken Captain Fritz Schoell.theme: deception; loyalty vs duty; moral ambiguity

After a number of years of marriage, whi.coa:
Ernst Vossberg has an affair with his secretary, resulting in her pregnancy.
The mistress confronts Ernst's wife, demanding that she release him from their marriage.
The wife refuses to comply, leading to tension between the two women.
Sometime later, after the mistress gives birth, a physical confrontation occurs between the two women, and the mistress is killed.
After a number of years of marriage, whi.outcomes: Protagonist's wife (Maria) is found guilty of murder, but the outcome is not specified. The conflict is unresolved with a focus on the consequences of Maria's actions.

Warren Nefron is a klutz who cannot do a.coa:
A klutz tells their problems to a psychiatrist through therapy sessions.
The therapist uses hypnosis to cure the patient, but this has an unintended consequence of transferring all the patient's problems to the therapist instead.
As a result of this transfer, the therapist now experiences the same issues as the patient.
The cured patient and a young woman attend a film titled Smorgasbord.
Warren Nefron is a klutz who cannot do a.outcomes: Protagonist is cured of his issues but now faces new challenges as a result. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation and transfer of responsibility.
Warren Nefron is a klutz who cannot do a.theme: self-acceptance; burden of others' problems; the power of hypnosis

Sara and her husband Jean swim in the se.coa:
Sara and her husband swim in the sea while on vacation, kissing and caressing one another.
They return home to a wintry Paris.
Jean is contacted by his former friend François, who wants him to work as a talent scout at his new sports agency.
François' re-entrance into their lives causes tension in Sara's relationship with Jean.
Sara and her husband Jean swim in the se.outcomes: Protagonist's relationship with her partner is put in jeopardy but ultimately remains intact. The conflict is unresolved with potential for future tension.
Sara and her husband Jean swim in the se.theme: longing vs commitment; past relationships; identity

Olivia (Charlotte Church) is a young gir.coa: A young girl is conceived when her mother meets a Scottish rock star one night. The couple falls out of touch soon after, and the rock star never knows that he has a daughter. Years later, the rock star is hospitalized after being sectioned following a motorcycle accident that is perceived as a suicide attempt. While in the hospital, the rock star meets his now 16-year-old daughter for the first time.
Olivia (Charlotte Church) is a young gir.outcomes: Protagonist (Olivia) finds a sense of belonging and connection with her father through their shared love of music. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as they heal past wounds and form a new relationship.
Olivia (Charlotte Church) is a young gir.theme: family bonds; redemption; the power of music

In the 1918 war-stricken Giorgio Volli r.coa:
The wife of the orphanage owner commits suicide.
Giorgio arrives at the orphanage and finds out about the tragedy.
It is revealed that the house is almost empty and abandoned, and the children died under unclear circumstances, with only one girl surviving.
Giorgio starts to investigate what happened.
In the 1918 war-stricken Giorgio Volli r.outcomes: Protagonist uncovers truth about Dr. Degrace's actions and brings justice to the victims' families. The conflict is resolved with personal transformation as protagonist finds closure and a sense of responsibility for the tragedy.

The story details transactions between A.coa:
A meets B at H and comes home pleased with the events.
A meets B again at H after a delay, but B is not there.
A learns that B had arrived early waiting for him.
A has an opportunity to explain to B what happened.
The story details transactions between A.outcomes: Protagonist fails to reconnect with antagonist due to his own mistake (tripping). The conflict is unresolved with a sense of regret and unfulfilled connection.
The story details transactions between A.theme: missed opportunities; communication breakdowns; consequences of carelessness

Four dancers, nearing their eighties, ta.coa: Four dancers nearing their eighties decide to return to the stage in an opera house. They are challenged by Heike Hennig to take on this endeavor. During the Second World War, a three-yea.coa:

As the Orient Express hurtles through th.coa: A loud scream is heard on the train, prompting the pulling of the communication cord. Salt and Pepper join forces to uncover the truth about their brother's death.
Chris Pepper and Charlie Salt lose their.outcomes: Protagonist succeeds in exposing diamond smugglers and bringing them to justice. The conflict is resolved with conflict_resolved and systemic change.

Prof. Walter Forrester (Ángel Menéndez).coa:
A mysterious stone is found by an assistant in the Akasava jungle.
The stone is stolen from the assistant, and Prof. Forrester vanishes.
Forrester becomes the sole suspect in his disappearance.
A Scotland Yard detective enters Forrester's office in London and is murdered.

The landowner and bachelor Philipp Klapp.coa: Philipp receives a letter from his nephew asking for 20,000 marks to invest in an insane asylum. Philipp decides to examine the supposed institution before giving the money, and makes his way to see his nephew. He follows Tommy's advice and leads his uncle to the Pension Schöller, which is presented as "Peter's insane asylum". The ruse fails, and Peter becomes suspicious that something is not right.

His father's bees have disappeared unexpectedly, threatening his livelihood as a beekeeper. A bizarre accident kills the father while he is out searching for the missing bees. The boy continues to wander the woods alone after his father's death.

In 1960, in the aftermath of the Anpo Pr.coa:
In 1950, student demonstrations against the Zengakuren's authoritarian leadership take place. A young student named Takao lets a "spy" free, leading to his own subsequent suicide. This event sparks criticism of the Zengakuren's leadership and Nakayawa's role in it. In 1960, a wedding ceremony between two former student radicals is interrupted by uninvited guests who accuse the couple and their assembled guests of forgetting their political commitments.

The interruption triggers flashbacks to 1950 and 1960, focusing on Nozawa's subjective experiences during both periods. The memory of Takao's death is reconstructed as a criticism of Nakayawa's actions in 1950. Other forgotten comrades from 1950 are invoked, along with fresh memories from the bloody demonstrations of 1960.

The guests stand immobile to Nakayawa's stilted speech at the wedding ceremony, which has been interrupted by the uninvited guests.
In 1960, in the aftermath of the Anpo Pr.outcomes: Protagonist (Nozawa) is left with unresolved emotions and unclear change. The conflict remains unresolved with no clear resolution or transformation.
In 1960, in the aftermath of the Anpo Pr.theme: unresolved trauma; authoritarianism vs activism; memory vs change

Kim Seok-go showed a lot of promise as a.coa: Seok-go overhears a fight between Hwa-sun's ex-husband and her niece from his house. Hwa-sun kills her ex-husband during the fight. Ramu is a young carefree man who raises.coa:

Holy Weapon presents the story of Seven.coa: A group of Seven Maidens are recruited to challenge a powerful Japanese swordsman. Count Alfred (Maurice Chevalier), milita.coa: Count Alfred is ordered back to Sylvania to report to Queen Louise for a reprimand following a string of scandals. The Queen is royally fed-up with her subjects' preoccupation with whom she will marry and invites Count Alfred to dinner, intrigued by his dossier rather than offended. During the dinner, the Queen tries to find a suitable "punishment" for him, but their conversation sparks a romance between them. They eventually get married, despite Count Alfred's initial qualms about being a consort.

Thessaly, overrun with barbarian invader.coa: A kingdom is overrun by barbarian invaders and plagued by natural disasters, prompting its ruler to send a group of warriors on a quest for a valuable treasure. The ruler's absence creates an opportunity for a trusted cohort to plot against him and his queen. A lord in the kingdom plots to seize power from an absent king, while another character seeks to marry the king's loyal wife. The ruler's departure sets off a chain of events that leads to encounters with various mythical creatures, including a queen who controls siren witches on her island.
Thessaly, overrun with barbarian invader.outcomes: Protagonist King Jason succeeds in retrieving the Golden Fleece and securing his kingdom's future. The conflict is resolved with personal triumph but broader threats from barbarian invaders remain.

American physicist Professor Bower is ef.coa: A shady CIA agent blackmails Professor Bower into helping obtain secret microfilm from a defecting Russian scientist. Ruza (Mirjana Karanović) a Serb, left Be.coa: A woman leaves Belgrade over 25 years ago to start a new life in Zurich. The owner hires the young woman as a worker at the cafeteria.
Ruza (Mirjana Karanović) a Serb, left Be.outcomes: Protagonist Ruza undergoes a transformation as she forms a connection with Ana, leading to a more joyful existence. The conflict is resolved with personal growth and relational change.

Juan (Jorge Mistral) returns to town aft.coa: A person returns to town after being locked up for ten years. Superintendent Stafford of the United Pr.coa:

Travis Cornell, a former Delta Force ope.coa:
A former Delta Force operator explores a canyon near his home and encounters two genetically engineered creatures that have escaped from a top-secret government laboratory.

A military coup occurs in 1955, led by Lieutenant General Eduardo Lonardi. The coup deposes Argentine president Juan Perón. A right-wing dictatorship comes to power, with General Pedro Eugenio Aramburu taking over after Lonardi is deemed too lenient towards Peronism.

Optimistic psychiatrist Dr. Donovan MacL.coa:
Dr. MacLeod introduces his new method of group therapy to treat mental patients.
Head Nurse Terry resists Dr. MacLeod's approach, advocating for traditional methods like strait-jackets and padded cells.
The hospital's head, Dr. Harrington, fails to take a firm stance against Terry, allowing her opposition to continue.
Dr. MacLeod continues to implement his method despite the resistance, with some patients showing initial improvement but also experiencing setbacks.
A patient is nearly raped during one of the therapy sessions, causing a turning point in the hospital's approach.
The near-rape incident prompts Dr. Harrington to finally support Dr. MacLeod's method, and Terry's assistant, nurse Bracken, begins to question her superior's stance.
Dr. MacLeod's method eventually prevails, meeting some success with patients like Lorna, Marion, Edna, and Irene showing improvement.

Marc and Philippe are two teenage summer.coa: Two teenage summer-camp counselors work at a vacation camp in the French country in 1960. The Ministry for the Future, an international organization based in Zurich, is established as a subsidiary body under the Paris Agreement to advocate for the rights of future generations. The organization's leader seeks to convince central banks of the threats to currency and market stability posed by climate change effects. A coordinated global round of unconventional quantitative easing through the issuance of a complementary currency, called the carbon coin, is proposed.

Feldsher Kuryatin, substituting for a ch.coa:

Barbara is married to the distinguished.coa: The professor saves the narrator's father's life, leading to their marriage. The couple has a mentally handicapped child together. Fred plans to marry his girlfriend Mara,.coa: Fred proposes to Mara in front of a sold-out basketball arena, but she puts off her decision to their son Linus. To gain Linus's approval, Fred decides to give him a basketball from his favorite team. Norman returns to Danger City seeking re.coa: A group of men, including Norman's father, arrive in Danger City seeking fortune. El Hadji Abdoukader Beye, a Senegalese b.coa: A Senegalese businessman takes on a third wife, demonstrating his social and economic success. He discovers that he is unable to consummate the marriage on their wedding night due to impotence. He suspects one or both of his first two wives have put a spell on him, unaware that the true culprit is someone else. He walks by this person every day without realizing it.

The film opens aboard a U-boat as it ret.coa:
A U-boat returns from a mission and docks onshore.

The next day, they ship off for their next mission.

The Royal Navy ship attempts to torpedo the U-boat but misses, instead sinking itself.

The plot functions primarily as link bet.coa:
An entrepreneur is in debt with many companies.
He fakes his own death by jumping out of a plane, making it appear as though he has committed suicide.

Boris's partner is struggling with writing a new novel, while Boris has found a new interest in someone else, specifically his younger colleague Tim.
